title: fix(agent-studio): surface --archive flag in share + pack/command hints at session start (#133)
message:
fix(agent-studio): surface --archive flag in share + pack/command hints at session start (#133)

* fix(agent-studio): surface --archive flag in share + pack/command hints at session start

- agent-studio-share/SKILL.md: document the existing `--archive <path>` flag so
  creators can share a pre-packed `.tar.gz` without repackaging the current
  workspace. The Python already supported it; SKILL.md was hiding it from agents.
- AGENTS.md: add a one-line orientation on first DEV reply (active pack name +
  `/studio` prefix reminder) so creators stop typing `/new` instead of
  `/studio new` and always know which pack the workspace is editing.
- Bump version 1.3.0 → 1.3.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(agent-studio): trim tokens in share SKILL.md + AGENTS.md THIRD step

Tighten the prose added in the previous commit. No behavioral change.

- agent-studio-share/SKILL.md: collapse the `--archive` bullet (was ~70 words,
  now ~20) — drop where-to-find-archives illustration and the "package.py
  skipped" sentence (already covered in Internal behavior). Shorten
  `--artifacts-base-url` to `<url>` placeholder. Trim "that path … no
  packaging step" → "directly, no packaging".
- AGENTS.md: trim THIRD step ~30 tokens — drop "mode" / "of session"
  (parallel with FIRST/SECOND), drop "(the name: from agent/agent-pack.yaml)"
  (already read in SECOND), drop ", not /new" (positive example suffices),
  make skip-condition mechanically checkable ("starts with /studio").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(agent-studio): tighten --archive fallback note + THIRD-step scope

Address review feedback on PR #133:
- SKILL.md: disclose the unversioned-filename fallback for --archive
  (version="unknown") so the documented identity contract matches
  run_share.py's actual parsing behavior.
- AGENTS.md THIRD step: state DEV-only / once-per-session scope and
  explicit TEST = no orientation, removing ambiguity around when the
  one-line orientation fires.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ning Hu <ning@gensmo.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: felix-srp
pr_body:
## Summary

Two small docs/SOP fixes for Agent Studio v1.3.1, motivated by friction seen with real creators.

- **`/studio share --archive <path>` is now reachable.** `run_share.py` already supported the flag (validates and stages a pre-packed `.tar.gz` without invoking `package.py`), but `agent-studio-share/SKILL.md` never told the agent it existed — so the share flow was locked to "repackage the current workspace" with no way to share a foreign archive. SKILL.md now documents the flag, the no-repackage path, and that an unversioned filename falls back to `version="unknown"` (mirrors `run_share.py`'s actual parsing).
- **Session-start orientation in DEV mode.** AGENTS.md now asks the agent to lead its first reply of a DEV session with a one-line orientation: which pack is active (`name:` from `agent/agent-pack.yaml`) and that creator commands use the `/studio` prefix (e.g. `/studio new`, not `/new`). Scope is explicit: DEV only, once per session, skipped when the user's first message already starts with `/studio`, and silent in TEST so the pack-agent persona is preserved.
- Version bump `1.3.0 → 1.3.1`.

No Python changes.

## Test plan

Verified in a sibling openclaw-docker container (`ghcr.io/serendipityoneinc/openclaw-docker:2026.5.7`) on the patched workspace.

Script-level (run via `uv run --python 3.12 .agents/skills/agent-studio-share/scripts/run_share.py`):

- [x] No `--archive`, no existing `zip/<pack>-<version>.tar.gz` → calls `package.py`; `archive_reused=false`, archive built and staged into `artifacts/shares/`.
- [x] No `--archive`, matching zip already in `zip/` → reuse path; `archive_reused=true`, `package.py` not invoked.
- [x] `--archive zip/imported-pack-2.0.5.tar.gz` → skips `package.py`; `pack_name=imported-pack`, `version=2.0.5` parsed from filename even though the workspace manifest says otherwise (workspace manifest correctly ignored).
- [x] `--archive zip/myarchive.tar.gz` (no version segment) → succeeds with `pack_name=myarchive`, `version="unknown"` (documented fallback).

LLM-level (manual webchat session against the deployed pack):

- [x] DEV first session, neutral message → first reply names the active pack and mentions the `/studio` prefix.
- [x] DEV first message is itself `/studio …` → orientation line skipped, command handled directly.
- [x] TEST mode first reply → pack-agent persona, no orientation line.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

files.patch:
@@ -16,13 +16,16 @@ Two agent actions: run the script, present what it returned.
 uv run --python 3.12 scripts/run_share.py --pack-dir . --output zip/
 ```
 
-Optional: `--artifacts-base-url https://artifacts.example.com/.../artifacts`.
+Optional flags:
+
+- `--archive <path>` — share a pre-packed `.tar.gz` instead of repackaging. Identity is parsed from the filename — name it `<name>-<version>.tar.gz`; an unversioned filename falls back to `version="unknown"`. The workspace manifest is ignored either way.
+- `--artifacts-base-url <url>` — override the artifacts base URL.
 
 Returns `share_url` (public install link) and `share_text_base64` (base64-encoded share text for the same link). `share_base64` may also appear for compatibility — prefer `share_text_base64`.
 
 ## Internal behavior (not steps for the agent to re-run)
 
-- Reuses `zip/<pack>-<version>.tar.gz` if present (validates layout first); otherwise invokes `package.py`. **Never** hand-craft an archive (`tar`/`zip`/`npm pack`) — workspace root `.md` files are Agent Studio's, raw archives deploy as impersonators. See `../agent-studio/references/packaging.md` §5e.
+- Without `--archive`: reuses `zip/<pack>-<version>.tar.gz` if present (validates layout first); otherwise invokes `package.py`. With `--archive <path>`: validates and stages directly, no packaging. **Never** hand-craft an archive (`tar`/`zip`/`npm pack`) — workspace root `.md` files are Agent Studio's, raw archives deploy as impersonators. See `../agent-studio/references/packaging.md` §5e.
 - Copies the archive to `artifacts/shares/`.
 - Resolves artifacts base URL from `--artifacts-base-url`, `ECAP_ARTIFACTS_BASE_URL` / `ARTIFACTS_BASE_URL`, or the convention in `TOOLS.md`.
 - Builds the public link, base64-encodes it.

@@ -10,6 +10,8 @@
   `uv run --python 3.12 .agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/migrate_manifest.py --pack-dir .`
   Then read `agent/agent-pack.yaml` + `.agents/skills/agent-studio/SKILL.md`. If `agent/agent-pack.yaml` has a `name:` and `snapshots/<that-name>/context.md` exists, read that too. **Don't respond until all required files are read.**
 
+**THIRD (DEV only, once per session):** Lead your first reply with a one-line orientation — active pack name (from `agent/agent-pack.yaml`) and that creator commands use the `/studio` prefix (e.g. `/studio new`). Skip when the user's first message starts with `/studio` (the creator is already issuing a command). In TEST, do not emit this line.
+
 ## Mode
 
 Persisted in `.mode`. Default **DEV**. Creator can switch via `/dev` or `/test`. Auto-switch to DEV on unrecoverable TEST errors.

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 name: agent-studio
 display_name: Agent Studio
-version: 1.3.0
+version: 1.3.1
 description: >
   Helps creators turn their domain expertise into agent packs through a
   guided, conversational, no-code workflow — Discovery, Skill Design,

title: feat(video-duplicate): upgrade to v1.7.2 (#134)
message:
feat(video-duplicate): upgrade to v1.7.2 (#134)

Adds shared video-analyze skill (Gemini 3.1 Pro pre-processing) and
migrates Workflows A/B/C to consume the unified video_analysis.json
schema. Workflow C is now flagged as ⭐ recommended; Workflow A switches
to per-scene frame_time + global_style anchors; Workflow B prompt
composer adopts the new lighting / spatial_layers / emotional_arc
fields with old-key fallbacks. Description.json version aligned with
agent-pack.yaml (the build tarball shipped 1.6.3, fixed to 1.7.2).

Co-authored-by: vincent-srp <180138700+vincent-srp@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: vincent-srp
pr_body:
## 一句话总结

v1.6.4 时 Gemini 只能告诉 AI"画面在哪里切"；v1.7.2 之后，Gemini 像导演一样读懂整部片子的气质、节奏、情绪和运镜意图，再用这份"导演意图说明书"指挥每一帧的重新生成。三条工作流（A/B/C）全部统一走同一个分析入口。

---

## 🎯 用户视角的核心升级（v1.6.4 → v1.7.2）

### 视频理解从 12 字段 → 20+ 字段

| 新增理解维度 | 用户感知到的差异 |
|---|---|
| **主体运动弧线** (`action.subject_motion`) | 生成视频里人不再是站着不动的塑料人 |
| **镜头运动意图** (`camera.motion` / `lens_feel`) | 复刻镜头语言更贴近原片（推/拉/跟，光学质感） |
| **情绪弧线** (`emotional_arc`) | 每个镜头有"戏剧张力"，不是平白画面 |
| **空间层次** (`spatial_layers`) | 画面有纵深感，前中后景关系清楚 |
| **音画同步点** (`audio_sync`) | 生成视频的动作与音频节奏咬合 |
| **全局风格签名** (`global_style.*`) | 跨片段视觉风格统一，不再跳帧 |
| **叙事结构** (`narrative_structure.hook/arc/cta`) | 换台词后叙事节奏保留，钩子还在张力 |
| **微表情** (`action.micro_expression`) | 不再"蜡像脸"，眼神/呼吸/嘴角都有 |

### 三条工作流的具体改善

**Workflow A — 分镜重绘（storyboard-studio）**
- gpt-image-2 重绘时拿到"全片色调锚点 + 人物外貌锚点"：同一个人在第 1 段和第 5 段看起来是同一个个体
- `visual_static`（Gemini 专为图像模型写的冻帧描述）以前白白产出从未使用，现在作为每个 panel 的主描述
- Seedance 生成时显式传镜头运动指令，告别"PPT 静止帧感"

**Workflow B — 风格迁移（style-duplicate）**
- Claude 现在有 7 条 GLOBAL CONTEXT RULES 作为硬约束（不是参考数据），替换内容时不会自由发挥破坏原片节奏
- 把全局风格从 JSON blob 改成具名标签块（`CAMERA SIGNATURE:` 触发约束模式，而 `"camera_signature": "..."` 只触发数据模式）
- VO 替换保留叙事功能：hook chunk 仍然有张力开场，cta chunk 仍然有行动力收场
- 修复 9 处字段名断裂（旧代码读不到新 schema 的值）

**Workflow C — 故事板复刻 ⭐ 推荐（storyboard-replicate）**
- 故事板 prompt 从"手工填模板"升级为"从 video_analysis.json 自动组装"——质量稳定，agent 仍可审阅/编辑
- Seedance chunk prompt 改成结构化标签：每个 scene 显式带运动/情绪/镜头意图
- Chunk 切分从"暴力均分"升级为"Gemini 叙事切分"（≤14s/块，切点在 VO 呼吸点）

### 架构层面：三条路径合一

```
旧（v1.6.4）：A/B/C 各自有自己的 Gemini 分析脚本，schema 各异
新（v1.7.2）：video-analyze（唯一 Gemini 入口）
                ↓ video_analysis.json
                ├── A：frame_time 提帧 → panel redraw → Seedance
                ├── B：全量字段 → Claude prompt 合成 → Seedance
                └── C：chunks[] 叙事切分 → 故事板 → Seedance
```

---

## 🐛 Bug 修复

- **珠宝引用遗漏**：项链/戒指的参考图现在自动注入到所有有人物的镜头，不再依赖 Gemini 关键词覆盖率
- **字幕流程统一**：三条 workflow 全部走 `transcribe → burn` 新路径，废弃 `subtitle_burn.py`（已 `.deprecated`）
- **台词时间戳**：兼容新旧两套数据格式（`chunks[].voiceover[]` ↔ `captions[]`），旧数据不丢字幕
- **Stage 3 模型**：`gpt-4o` → `claude-sonnet-4-6`（ZooClaw 环境内可用）
- **description.json 版本漂移**：tar 包内为 `1.6.3`，已手动对齐为 `1.7.2`（匹配 agent-pack.yaml）

---

## 📋 本次改动的代码层清单

**新增 skill**
- `.agents/skills/video-analyze/` — 唯一的 Gemini 视频分析入口
  - `SKILL.md`、`scripts/analyze_cli.py`（426 行）

**Workflow 脚本更新**
- `storyboard-studio/scripts/run_pipeline.py` — Stage 1 迁移到 `video-analyze`，fallback 保留
- `storyboard-replicate/scripts/run_pipeline.py` — Step 1 迁移到 `video-analyze`，叙事切分驱动 Step 5；新增 `_auto_build_storyboard_prompt()` 和 `_auto_build_chunk_prompts()`
- `style-duplicate/scripts/run_pipeline.py` — 9 处字段名断裂修复，新字段注入 shot 块
- `style-duplicate/scripts/prompt_composer_cli.py` — SYSTEM_PROMPT 加入 7 条 GLOBAL CONTEXT RULES，locked 块加入 lighting / spatial_layers / emotional_arc / vfx_and_grade
- `style-duplicate/scripts/style_analyze_cli.py` — 字段扩展
- `seedance-runner/scripts/run_chunks.py` — 小幅更新

**Manifest & docs**
- `agent-pack.yaml` — 注册 `video-analyze`、`style-duplicate`、`storyboard-replicate`；版本 1.6.4 → 1.7.2
- `description.json` — 版本对齐到 1.7.2
- `style-duplicate/SKILL.md` — 新增 Prompt Architecture Best Practices 章节
- `AGENTS.md` — Workflow A/C 描述、Step 5 工作流说明、路由表全部更新

**Diff 规模**：12 files, +1982 / −183

---

## ⚠️ Reviewer Notes

- Build tarball (`build/video-duplicate-1.7.2.tar.gz`) 内夹带了 `__pycache__/`、`artifacts/shares/`、`zip/video-duplicate-1.6.1.tar.gz`、`docs/v165-todo.md` 这些本地构建产物——**均未入提交**，只 ship 了 cleaned payload
- `docs/CHANGELOG.md` 被仓库 `.gitignore`（`docs/` 规则）排除，本次 v1.7.2 changelog 仅在本地工作树。如果想让 changelog 入库，需要单独调整 .gitignore
- `description.json` 上游 tar 写的是 `1.6.3`，本次修正为 `1.7.2`——package.py 只校验 `agentPack_id` 不校验 version，所以这是体面问题不是阻塞问题，但建议上游打包脚本同步修复

---

## ✅ Test plan

- [ ] Pack loader 读到 v1.7.2 manifest（agent-pack.yaml 与 description.json 版本一致）
- [ ] 11 个 skill 全部解析成功：`pack-onboarding`、`prepare-source`、`seedance-runner`、`storyboard-replicate`、`storyboard-studio`、`style-duplicate`、`video-analyze`、`video-burn-subtitle`、`video-editor`、`video-transcribe`、`zooclaw-forcedalign`
- [ ] Workflow C 端到端冒烟跑通（analyze → storyboard → seedance → editor）
- [ ] Workflow B 走新 schema 路径（验证 `lighting` / `spatial_layers` / `emotional_arc` 真的到了 Seedance prompt）
- [ ] Workflow A 走 `video-analyze` 路径（验证 `video_analysis.json` 生成；fallback 路径不被触发）
- [ ] 字幕烧录走 `transcribe.sh` → `burn.sh` 新路径（无 `subtitle_burn.py` 调用）

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

+def _auto_build_storyboard_prompt(analysis: dict) -> str | None:
+    """Auto-generate a storyboard prompt from video_analysis.json rich fields.
+
+    Uses visual_static, emotional_arc, subject_motion, spatial_layers, lighting,
+    global_style, and narrative_structure to produce a high-quality panel prompt
+    for gpt-image-2 without requiring the agent to manually fill the template.
+
+    Returns None if analysis lacks enough data (agent should fall back to manual).
+    """
+    gs  = analysis.get("global_style",        {}) or {}
+    ns  = analysis.get("narrative_structure",  {}) or {}
+    chunks = analysis.get("chunks", [])
+    if not chunks:
+        return None
+
+    # ── Collect all scenes ──
+    all_scenes: list[dict] = []
+    for chunk in chunks:
+        all_scenes.extend(chunk.get("scenes", []))
+    if not all_scenes:
+        return None
+
+    n_shots = len(all_scenes)
+
+    # ── Global style block ──
+    color_palette    = gs.get("color_palette",      "")
+    lighting_sig     = gs.get("lighting_signature", "")
+    visual_feel      = gs.get("visual_feel",        "")
+    camera_sig       = gs.get("camera_signature",   "")
+    edit_rhythm      = gs.get("edit_rhythm",        "")
+    sound_sig        = gs.get("sound_signature",    "")
+    hook_txt         = ns.get("hook", "")
+    arc_txt          = ns.get("arc",  "")
+    cta_txt          = ns.get("cta",  "")
+
+    # ── Narrative summary ──
+    narrative_parts = [p for p in [hook_txt, arc_txt, cta_txt] if p]
+    narrative_summary = " → ".join(narrative_parts) if narrative_parts else "progression through scenes"
+
+    # ── Extract character/subject anchor from first scene with rich subject ──
+    character_desc = ""
+    for s in all_scenes:
+        subj = s.get("subject", {})
+        if isinstance(subj, dict):
+            d = subj.get("description", "") or subj.get("appearance", "")
+        else:
+            d = str(subj) if subj else ""
+        if d and len(d) > 20:
+            character_desc = d
+            break
+    if not character_desc:
+        character_desc = "main subject as analysed"
+
+    # ── Panel grid ──
+    grid_map = {1: "1×1", 2: "1×2", 3: "1×3", 4: "2×2",
+                5: "2×3", 6: "2×3", 7: "3×3", 8: "2×4",
+                9: "3×3", 10: "2×5", 11: "3×4", 12: "3×4"}
+    grid = grid_map.get(n_shots, f"{(n_shots+2)//3}×3")
+
+    # ── Per-panel descriptions ──
+    panel_lines = []
+    for i, s in enumerate(all_scenes, 1):
+        sid   = s.get("scene_id", i)
+        t0    = s.get("start", 0)
+        t1    = s.get("end",   t0 + 2)
+        # visual_static is specifically written for image models — use it first
+        vs    = s.get("visual_static", "")
+        vm    = s.get("visual_motion",  "")
+        ea    = s.get("emotional_arc",  "")
+        tr    = s.get("transition_out", "")
+        act   = s.get("action", {}) if isinstance(s.get("action"), dict) else {}
+        sm    = act.get("subject_motion",   "")
+        me    = act.get("micro_expression", act.get("person", ""))
+        cam   = s.get("camera", {}) if isinstance(s.get("camera"), dict) else {}
+        frm   = cam.get("framing",   cam.get("shot_type",   ""))
+        cm    = cam.get("motion",    "")
+        lf    = cam.get("lens_feel", "")
+        spl   = s.get("spatial_layers", {}) if isinstance(s.get("spatial_layers"), dict) else {}
+        bg    = spl.get("background", s.get("environment", {}).get("background", "")
+                        if isinstance(s.get("environment"), dict) else "")
+        fg    = spl.get("foreground", "")
+        df    = spl.get("depth_feel", "")
+        lit   = s.get("lighting", {}) if isinstance(s.get("lighting"), dict) else {}
+        ks    = lit.get("key_source",   s.get("environment", {}).get("lighting_detail", "")
+                        if isinstance(s.get("environment"), dict) else "")
+        cm_txt = lit.get("color_mood",  s.get("environment", {}).get("color_mood", "")
+                         if isinstance(s.get("environment"), dict) else "")
+        # Compose panel description (visual_static is the anchor if available)
+        desc_parts = []
+        if vs:  desc_parts.append(vs)
+        elif vm: desc_parts.append(vm)
+        if sm:  desc_parts.append(f"motion arc: {sm}")
+        if me:  desc_parts.append(f"expression: {me}")
+        if frm: desc_parts.append(f"framing: {frm}")
+        if cm:  desc_parts.append(f"camera: {cm}" + (f" {lf}" if lf else ""))
+        if ks or cm_txt:
+            desc_parts.append(f"light: {ks or cm_txt}")
+        if bg:  desc_parts.append(f"bg: {bg}")
+        if df:  desc_parts.append(f"depth: {df}")
+        if ea:  desc_parts.append(f"emotion: {ea}")
+        desc = " | ".join(p for p in desc_parts if p) or "scene"
+        caption = ""
+        # try to find voiceover for this scene's time window
+        for chunk in chunks:
+            for vo in chunk.get("voiceover", []):
+                vs_t, ve_t = float(vo.get("start", 0)), float(vo.get("end", 0))
+                if t0 <= (vs_t + ve_t) / 2 <= t1:
+                    caption = vo.get("text", "")[:60]
+                    break
+        panel_line = (
+            f"Panel {i} SHOT {sid} | {t0:.1f}-{t1:.1f}s | {desc}"
+            + (f" | Caption: \"{caption}\"" if caption else "")
+        )
+        panel_lines.append(panel_line)
+    panels_block = "\n".join(panel_lines)
+
+    # ── Assemble ──
+    style_desc = "; ".join(p for p in [visual_feel, lighting_sig] if p)                  or "High-end commercial advertisement quality, cinematic."
+    color_desc = color_palette or "natural, commercially graded"
+    cam_note   = camera_sig or "cinematic mixed"
+    audio_note = sound_sig  or "ambient sound + voiceover, no BGM"
+
+    prompt = f"""Generate a professional 16:9 film production board / visual planning board for a short-form social media advertisement recreation. This storyboard should feel like a high-spec pre-production reference guide.
+
+STORY CONTENT:
+{narrative_summary}. Character: {character_desc}. {n_shots} shots total.
+
+OVERALL STYLE:
+{style_desc}. Color palette: {color_desc}. Edit rhythm: {edit_rhythm or "natural commercial pacing"}.
+
+LAYOUT — strict labeled grid sections:
+
+TOP BANNER: CREATIVE DIRECTION
+Text: Shots: {n_shots} | Color Palette: {color_desc} | Narrative Arc: {narrative_summary}
+
+LEFT COLUMN: CHARACTER AND STYLE REFERENCE
+Show the following subject from multiple angles: front view, side view, face close-up.
+{character_desc}
+
+CENTER: STORYBOARD — {n_shots} panels in {grid} grid
+
+{panels_block}
+
+RIGHT COLUMN TOP: LIGHTING AND MOOD NOTES
+(1) Global lighting — {lighting_sig or "as analysed"}
+(2) Camera language — {cam_note}
+(3) Emotion Arc — {arc_txt or "progressive commercial narrative"}
+(4) Color Palette swatches: {color_desc}
+
+RIGHT COLUMN MIDDLE: MOOD KEYWORDS (tag style)
+{edit_rhythm or "cinematic"} | {visual_feel or "commercial"} | {color_desc} | premium | high-end
+
+RIGHT COLUMN BOTTOM: AUDIO AND CINEMATOGRAPHY NOTES
+Audio: {audio_note}
+Cinematography: {cam_note} | {edit_rhythm or "natural pacing"} | cinematic DoF
+
+TEXT REQUIREMENTS: All text must be perfectly legible, sharp, clear. Section labels large and prominent. High contrast text on backgrounds. No garbled letters, no fake text, no meaningless symbols.
+
+IMAGE QUALITY: Professional illustration/design quality. Clean layouts, smooth gradients, no noise, no grain, elegant line weights. Should look like a polished high-end production company deliverable."""
+
+    return prompt
+
+
+def _auto_build_chunk_prompts(analysis: dict, asset_id: str,
+                              ratio: str, resolution: str) -> list[dict]:
+    """Build per-chunk Seedance prompts from video_analysis.json rich fields.
+
+    Returns list of chunk dicts for generation_plan.json.
+    Each prompt uses visual_static, subject_motion, emotional_arc, camera.motion,
+    audio_sync, and spatial_layers for maximum Seedance fidelity.
+    """
+    gs     = analysis.get("global_style", {}) or {}
+    chunks = analysis.get("chunks", [])
+    gs_cam = gs.get("camera_signature", "")
+    gs_rhy = gs.get("edit_rhythm",      "")
+    gs_snd = gs.get("sound_signature",  "")
+    gs_col = gs.get("color_palette",    "")
+    gs_vis = gs.get("visual_feel",      "")
+
+    plan_chunks = []
+    for chunk in chunks:
+        cid   = chunk.get("chunk_id", chunk.get("id", 0))
+        c0    = float(chunk.get("start", 0))
+        c1    = float(chunk.get("end",   c0 + 10))
+        dur   = min(c1 - c0, 15.0)
+        nlabel = chunk.get("narrative_label", "")
+
+        lines = []
+        if gs_vis or gs_col:
+            lines.append(f"[GLOBAL STYLE] {gs_vis}" + (f" | color: {gs_col}" if gs_col else ""))
+        if gs_cam:
+            lines.append(f"[CAMERA LANGUAGE] {gs_cam}")
+        if gs_rhy:
+            lines.append(f"[PACING] {gs_rhy}")
+        if nlabel:
+            lines.append(f"[NARRATIVE ROLE] {nlabel}")
+
+        for s in chunk.get("scenes", []):
+            sid = s.get("scene_id", "?")
+            # visual_static → best single-frame description for image model
+            vs  = s.get("visual_static", "")
+            # visual_motion → dynamic arc for video model
+            vm  = s.get("visual_motion",  "")
+            ea  = s.get("emotional_arc",  "")
+            tr  = s.get("transition_out", "")
+            as_ = s.get("audio_sync",     "")
+            act = s.get("action", {}) if isinstance(s.get("action"), dict) else {}
+            sm  = act.get("subject_motion",   "")
+            me  = act.get("micro_expression", act.get("person", ""))
+            pb  = act.get("product_behavior", act.get("product", ""))
+            ia  = act.get("interaction",      "")
+            cam = s.get("camera", {}) if isinstance(s.get("camera"), dict) else {}
+            cm  = cam.get("motion",    "")
+            lf  = cam.get("lens_feel", "")
+            spl = s.get("spatial_layers", {}) if isinstance(s.get("spatial_layers"), dict) else {}
+            df  = spl.get("depth_feel", "")
+            lit = s.get("lighting", {}) if isinstance(s.get("lighting"), dict) else {}
+            lm  = lit.get("light_motion", "")
+
+            scene_lines = [f"  [SCENE {sid}]"]
+            if vs:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [VISUAL STATIC — reference frame] {vs}")
+            if sm:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [ACTION — subject motion] {sm}")
+            if vm and vm != vs:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [VISUAL MOTION] {vm}")
+            if me:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [MICRO EXPRESSION] {me}")
+            if pb:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [PRODUCT BEHAVIOR] {pb}")
+            if ia:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [INTERACTION] {ia}")
+            if cm:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [CAMERA MOTION] {cm}" + (f" | {lf}" if lf else ""))
+            if df:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [DEPTH] {df}")
+            if lm:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [LIGHT MOTION] {lm}")
+            if ea:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [EMOTIONAL ARC] {ea}")
+            if as_:
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [AUDIO SYNC] {as_}")
+            if tr and tr.lower() not in ("hard cut", "cut"):
+                scene_lines.append(f"    [TRANSITION OUT] {tr}")
+            lines.extend(scene_lines)
+
+        for vo in chunk.get("voiceover", []):
+            t = float(vo.get("start", 0)) - c0
+            lines.append(f"[VOICEOVER @{t:.1f}s] {vo.get('text', '')}")
+
+        lines.append("[AUDIO] No BGM or background music. Ambient sound only.")
+        if gs_snd:
+            lines.append(f"[SOUND REFERENCE] {gs_snd}")
+        lines.append("IMPORTANT — FIRST FRAME: first frame must NOT be a static copy of the reference image.")
+
+        plan_chunks.append({
+            "index":           cid - 1,
+            "prompt":          "\n".join(lines),
+            "duration":        dur,
+            "ratio":           ratio,
+            "resolution":      resolution,
+            "reference_asset": f"asset://{asset_id}",
+        })
+    return plan_chunks
+

+---
+
+## Prompt Architecture Best Practices (v1.6.5)
+
+### Reference Image Subject Description
+When `reference_assets[]` are provided (e.g. model photo, outfit, necklace, ring), the
+`_build_chunk_prompt_text()` function **must not** re-describe subjects in free text.
+Instead reference images by index — and for **every jewelry item**, include an explicit
+match instruction to force the model to anchor to the reference photo:
+
+```
+SUBJECT: Image 1 person appearance (face, hair, skin tone, makeup — match exactly)
+       + Image 2 outfit & styling (match fabric, cut, and color exactly)
+       + Image 3 necklace — match the EXACT design, pendant shape, chain pattern,
+         and all gemstone/pearl details from Image 3. Worn naturally at collarbone.
+       + Image 4 ring — match the EXACT gemstone cut, color, prong setting, and band
+         design from Image 4. Prominently displayed on finger.
+```
+
+**Critical rule**: jewelry images (necklace, ring, earrings, bracelet) must always include
+a self-referencing "from Image N" instruction in the match clause. Without this, the model
+will ignore the reference photo and hallucinate a generic jewelry design.
+
+The pattern is: `{Image N} {item} — match the EXACT {key features} from {Image N}.`
+The double-reference (prefix + inline "from Image N") is intentional — it forces the
+model to cross-check the reference at generation time.
+
+This anchors Seedance to the actual reference photos and prevents visual drift from
+text re-description. The image index map is:
+
+| Index | Default Role |
+|-------|-------------|
+| Image 1 | model — face, hair & skin tone |
+| Image 2 | outfit — dress & styling details |
+| Image 3 | jewelry A — necklace design & chain |
+| Image 4 | jewelry B — ring gemstone & setting |
+| Image N | additional reference (role auto-labeled) |
+
+If a storyboard panel is also injected (Stage 4), it becomes Image 1 and all others
+shift by +1. The label map in `_build_chunk_prompt_text()` handles this automatically.
+
+### BGM Placement
+BGM **must** be at the very top of the prompt, before reference images and shot descriptions,
+tagged as highest priority:
+
+```
+【AUDIO — HIGHEST PRIORITY】
+<bgm description>
+Generate this background music track. This instruction overrides any default no-BGM behavior.
+```
+
+Placing BGM in `[CONSTRAINTS]` at the end causes Seedance to suppress it due to the
+`• No BGM unless explicitly stated below` constraint being parsed first.
+
+`bgm_description` is injected per-run via `chunk["bgm_description"]` in `chunk_prompts.json`.
+The default is empty (no BGM). Pass `--extra-constraints` to `run_pipeline.py` for ad-hoc
+BGM overrides without touching defaults.
+
+### Camera Motion
+**Never write `static locked-off` alone.** Every shot must have camera motion with:
+- Direction + speed: `slow dolly push-in (~0.1x pace)`, `gentle pan right ~1°/sec`
+- Emotional intent: `builds intimacy`, `maintains hypnotic rhythm`, `final authoritative hold`
+- If truly locked: `locked-off, no movement — static hold for brand recognition`
+
+The `rhythm_feel` field from Gemini Stage 1 analysis feeds directly into camera description.
+
+### Stage 1 Gemini Schema (v1.6.5 additions)
+Two new fields added to `camera` and `action.person`:
+
+```json
+"camera": {
+  "motion": "FULL directional description with speed + emotional intent",
+  "rhythm_feel": "pacing energy: slow and contemplative / tense held breath / etc."
+},
+"action": {
+  "person": "micro-expression detail: eye softness, brow tension, lip set, jaw angle, shoulder posture"
+}
+```
+
+These fields prevent Seedance from generating wax-figure static faces and PPT-style
+slide cuts.
+
+---
+
+## Video Analyze — Shared Service Architecture
+
+> **Status**: Planned for v1.6.6. Current state: each workflow has its own Gemini call.
+
+The three workflows (A/B/C) all call Gemini to analyze the source video but with
+different prompts and output schemas:
+
+| Workflow | Script | Output Format | Key Fields |
+|----------|--------|---------------|------------|
+| A (storyboard-studio) | `scene_analyze_cli.py` | JSON schema | `visual_static`, `visual_motion`, `frame_time` |
+| B (style-duplicate) | `style_analyze_cli.py` | JSON schema (6-field) | `camera`, `action`, `environment`, `style`, `audio` |
+| C (storyboard-replicate) | `analyze_video_gemini.py` | Free text (Markdown) | 8-dimension director narrative |
+
+**Implemented in v1.6.5**: `video-analyze` skill is live at `.agents/skills/video-analyze/`.
+
+`style-duplicate` Stage 1 now auto-detects and uses `video-analyze/scripts/analyze_cli.py`
+when present, falling back to legacy `style_analyze_cli.py` if not found.
+Output is written to `video_analysis.json` and symlinked as `style_analysis.json` for
+backward compatibility with downstream stages.
+
+```
+video-analyze/
+  scripts/
+    analyze_cli.py        # unified entry point — used by Stage 1
+  SKILL.md
+```
+
+`video_analysis.json` output contains (superset of old style_analysis.json):
+1. **Structured JSON** (6-field scenes) — consumed by all workflows
+2. **`camera.rhythm_feel`** — pacing energy per shot, drives prompt motion intensity
+3. **`action.person` micro-expression** — eye/brow/lip/shoulder detail, prevents wax-figure faces
+4. **`visual_static`** per scene — frozen-frame description for storyboard redraw (A/C)
+5. **`visual_motion`** per scene — temporal motion arc for video generation (A/B/C)
+6. **`frame_time`** per scene — ffmpeg screenshot extraction point (A)
+7. **`workflow_recommendation`** — Gemini recommends A/B/C after seeing the video
+8. **`global_style`** — color/lighting/camera/edit-rhythm signatures for all workflows
+9. **`narrative_structure`** — hook/arc/cta analysis
+
+Migration status (v1.6.5):
+- ✅ B (style-duplicate): Stage 1 uses `video-analyze`, symlinks output
+- 🔲 A (storyboard-studio): still uses `scene_analyze_cli.py` — v1.6.6
+- 🔲 C (storyboard-replicate): still uses `analyze_video_gemini.py` — v1.6.6
+

+# video-analyze — Shared Video Analysis Module
+
+> **Role**: Pre-processing step that runs BEFORE workflow selection.
+> All three video-duplicate workflows (A/B/C) consume this module's output.
+
+---
+
+## Purpose
+
+`video-analyze` is the **universal first step** of the video-duplicate pack.
+
+Before asking the user which workflow they want, run this analysis.
+The output `video_analysis.json` contains:
+
+1. A **workflow recommendation** based on the video's structure
+2. Rich **per-scene JSON** for AI video re-generation (Seedance)
+3. **`visual_static`** per scene for storyboard image redrawing (A/C)
+4. **`visual_motion`** per scene for video model generation (A/B/C)
+5. **`frame_time`** per scene for ffmpeg screenshot extraction (A)
+6. **Director-level camera/expression/lighting fields** for prompt richness (B)
+7. **Substitution hints** to pre-fill user questions in Stage 2 (B)
+
+---
+
+## When to Call
+
+**Always call first**, before presenting workflow options to the user.
+
+```
+User uploads video
+       ↓
+[video-analyze] — Gemini analyzes video
+       ↓
+workflow_recommendation → present to user
+       ↓
+User confirms or overrides workflow choice
+       ↓
+[Workflow A / B / C] — reads video_analysis.json
+```
+
+The result is **cached** — subsequent stages within the same run skip re-analysis.
+Pass `--force` to re-run.
+
+---
+
+## Usage
+
+```bash
+python3 analyze_cli.py \
+  --video    source.mp4 \
+  --out-dir  runs/run-01/ \
+  [--model   gemini-3.1-pro-preview] \
+  [--max-chunk-duration 14] \
+  [--max-tokens 12000]
+```
+
+Outputs `runs/run-01/video_analysis.json`.
+
+---
+
+## Output Schema
+
+```json
+{
+  "meta": {
+    "ratio": "9:16",
+    "duration_s": 35.1,
+    "language": "Mandarin Chinese",
+    "video_type": "奢侈品珠宝广告·品牌形象",
+    "video_purpose": "..."
+  },
+
+  "workflow_recommendation": {
+    "recommended": "style-duplicate",
+    "reason": "Video has fixed structure with clear person/product swap targets",
+    "substitution_hints": {
+      "person":  "lead model, Asian female 20s",
+      "product": "baroque gold cross necklace",
+      "scene":   "navy blue studio backdrop",
+      "voiceover": "no VO; music-only luxury ad"
+    }
+  },
+
+  "global_style": {
+    "color_palette": "deep navy + warm gold — authoritative luxury",
+    "lighting_signature": "large softbox front-left, feathered, warm 5600K, gold reflector on jewelry",
+    "visual_feel": "ultra-high-end jewelry campaign, cold-glamour, European luxury house",
+    "camera_signature": "imperceptible slow push-ins, hypnotic rhythm, never handheld",
+    "edit_rhythm": "slow dissolves every 1.5–2s, breath-like pacing",
+    "sound_signature": "soft piano + warm strings, no VO, near-silent ambient"
+  },
+
+  "voice_tone": { "has_voiceover": false, "gender": "none", ... },
+
+  "narrative_structure": {
+    "hook": "model face fills frame instantly — cold gaze, jewelry glints in first 0.5s",
+    "arc": "person → jewelry detail → profile → product solo → full look → brand outro",
+    "cta": "viewer left with brand name and prestige feeling, no direct CTA"
+  },
+
+  "chunks": [
+    {
+      "chunk_id": 1,
+      "start": 0.0, "end": 10.8,
+      "narrative_label": "showcase | brand-identity",
+      "voiceover": [],
+      "scenes": [
+        {
+          "scene_id": 1,
+          "start": 0.0, "end": 1.2,
+          "frame_time": 0.5,
+

+            "description": "Asian woman 20s, warm wheat skin, sleek dark-brown straight hair center-parted...",
+            "position": "center-frame, face angled left, eyes upper-third"
+          },
+

+            "product": "Gold cross pendant rests against collarbone — perfectly still, every pearl catches front-left softbox with equal exposure, no sway",
+            "interaction": "Pendant touches bare skin at collarbone; warmth of skin against cool gold creates tonal contrast"
+          },
+
+          "camera": {
+            "shot_type": "close-up",
+            "motion": "slow dolly push-in (~0.1x pace), imperceptible at first — builds intimacy and draws focus toward jewelry at collarbone without breaking the locked feel",
+            "composition": "centered, eyes on upper-left rule-of-thirds intersection",
+            "stability": "tripod",
+            "rhythm_feel": "slow and contemplative — the pace of a luxury print ad that moves"
+          },
+

+            "space": "minimalist photo studio",
+            "background": "solid deep navy blue seamless backdrop",
+            "props": "none",
+            "lighting_detail": "large softbox front-left at 45°, feathered edge, 3:1 ratio, 5600K; gold reflector from below bouncing warm fill onto necklace",
+            "color_mood": "deep navy cold authority + warm gold warmth = tension-luxury"
+          },
+
+          "style": {
+            "color": "deep navy, warm skin tone, bright gold",

+            "depth_of_field": "shallow — subject sharp, background softly blurred"
+          },
+

Large softbox light from front-left creates soft shadow on right cheek. Gold cross necklace catches warm light at collarbone. Expression: cool, detached, lips barely parted. Hair falls behind left shoulder. White lace dress strap visible at edge.",
+
+          "visual_motion": "Camera breathes slowly forward over 1.2s — nearly imperceptible push toward collarbone level. Model holds gaze left throughout, making no micro-movement except the faintest rise of chest with breath. Pendant remains still. Shot cuts to ECU of hand at 1.2s.",
+
+          "audio": {
+            "voiceover_text": "",
+            "voice_style": "none",
+            "ambient": "near-silent studio, faint piano note sustaining from BGM"
+          }
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## Field Reference — What Each Downstream Workflow Uses
+
+| Field | A (storyboard-studio) | B (style-duplicate) | C (storyboard-replicate) |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `meta.ratio` | ✅ ffprobe aspect | ✅ Seedance payload | ✅ storyboard gen |
+| `workflow_recommendation` | — | — | — (used pre-workflow) |
+| `global_style.*` | ✅ style consistency | ✅ prompt global context | ✅ storyboard style |
+| `narrative_structure.*` | ✅ chunk labels | ✅ chunk labels | ✅ narrative pacing |
+| `scene.frame_time` | ✅ ffmpeg screenshot | — | ✅ panel capture |
+| `scene.subject.description` | ✅ panel redraw | ✅ → replaced by Image refs | ✅ panel redraw |
+| `scene.action.person` | ✅ `visual_motion` | ✅ micro-expression in prompt | ✅ storyboard pose |
+| `scene.action.product` | ✅ `visual_motion` | ✅ product motion in prompt | ✅ product placement |
+| `scene.camera.motion` | ✅ `camera_motion` | ✅ camera block in prompt | ✅ shot direction |
+| `scene.camera.rhythm_feel` | — | ✅ pacing energy | ✅ director notes |
+| `scene.environment.lighting_detail` | ✅ panel lighting | ✅ lighting block | ✅ storyboard light |
+| `scene.visual_static` | ✅ image model prompt | — | ✅ image model prompt |
+| `scene.visual_motion` | ✅ Seedance prompt | ✅ Seedance motion | ✅ Seedance motion |
+| `voiceover[].text` | ✅ subtitle ASS | ✅ VO substitution base | ✅ caption extraction |
+
+---
+
+## Workflow Recommendation Logic
+
+Gemini outputs `workflow_recommendation.recommended` based on the video's structure:
+
+| Condition | Recommended Workflow |
+|---|---|
+| Video has a clear person/product that the user would want to swap | `style-duplicate` (B) |
+| Video has a strong narrative arc the user wants to replicate exactly | `storyboard-replicate` (C) |
+| User wants to build a new video inspired by the style but with new content | `storyboard-studio` (A) |
+
+After `video-analyze` completes, present the recommendation to the user with the reason,
+and offer them the choice to override before proceeding.
+
+---
+
+## Migration from Per-Workflow Analysis
+
+| Old script | Replace with |
+|---|---|
+| `storyboard-studio/scripts/scene_analyze_cli.py` | `video-analyze/scripts/analyze_cli.py` |
+| `style-duplicate/scripts/style_analyze_cli.py` | `video-analyze/scripts/analyze_cli.py` |
+| `storyboard-replicate/scripts/analyze_video_gemini.py` | `video-analyze/scripts/analyze_cli.py` |
+
+**Output file mapping**:
+- Old `style_analysis.json` → new `video_analysis.json` (superset of old schema)
+- Old `analysis.json` (C) → new `video_analysis.json`
+
+Each workflow's `run_pipeline.py` Stage 1 should be updated to:
+1. Call `video-analyze/scripts/analyze_cli.py`
+2. Read `video_analysis.json` instead of the old per-workflow output
+3. Map fields as per the table above
+
+Until migration is complete, `style-duplicate` and `storyboard-studio` retain their
+own `stage_1` scripts as fallback. The unified `video_analysis.json` schema is a
+**superset** of both — any workflow reading the old fields will still find them.
+
+---
+
+## Prompt Design Principles (for maintainers)
+
+The Gemini prompt in `analyze_cli.py` follows these principles:
+
+### 1. Three downstream tasks, one prompt
+The prompt explicitly tells Gemini it is serving three tasks simultaneously:
+video generation (Seedance), storyboard redrawing (image model), and workflow recommendation.
+This framing causes Gemini to provide appropriate detail across all dimensions.
+
+### 2. Director framing
+"Think and write like a director giving a shot list to their crew."
+This single instruction is responsible for the quality difference between C (good) and A/B (poor).
+It unlocks: directional motion language, micro-expression detail, lighting as DoP would describe it.
+
+### 3. camera.motion — no enums
+Free-text description with three required components:
+`direction + speed + emotional intent`
+Examples baked into the schema as inline guidance prevent regression to bare `static`.
+
+### 4. action.person — micro-expression required
+The prompt explicitly states: "This is what separates alive AI faces from wax figures."
+Required elements: eye softness, brow tension, lip set, jaw, neck, shoulder, hand energy.
+
+### 5. visual_static vs visual_motion — dual-purpose fields
+- `visual_static`: written for an image model (frozen frame, enough to redraw)
+- `visual_motion`: written for a video model (temporal verbs, full arc from start to cut)
+These two fields co-exist on every scene. A uses static, B/C use motion, all can use both.
+
+### 6. workflow_recommendation — baked in
+Gemini has full video context. Let it recommend the workflow, then let the user confirm.
+This is better UX than asking the user "which workflow do you want?" before they've seen the analysis.
+
+---
+
+## Jewelry / Product Reference Anchoring Rule
+
+When reference assets include **jewelry or detailed product images** (necklace, ring, earrings,
+bracelet, bag, watch, etc.), every shot that features the item must include a **self-referencing
+match instruction** in the SUBJECT field:
+
+```
+Image N <item> — match the EXACT <key visual features> from Image N. <wearing context>.
+```
+
+The double-reference pattern (`Image N` prefix + `from Image N` inside the clause) is
+intentional — it forces the video model to cross-check the reference photo at generation time
+rather than hallucinating a generic design.
+
+**Wrong** (model ignores ref image):
+```
+SUBJECT: Image 3 necklace worn naturally
+```
+
+**Correct** (model anchors to ref image):
+```
+SUBJECT: Image 3 necklace — match the EXACT pendant shape, chain pattern, pearl and
+         crystal details from Image 3. Worn naturally at collarbone.
+```
+
+This rule is enforced in `style-duplicate/scripts/run_pipeline.py` → `_build_chunk_prompt_text()`
+for necklace, ring, and any future jewelry/product types.

@@ -0,0 +1,426 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""
+video-analyze — Shared Gemini video analysis module for all video-duplicate workflows.
+
+Replaces:
+  storyboard-studio/scripts/scene_analyze_cli.py
+  style-duplicate/scripts/style_analyze_cli.py
+  storyboard-replicate/scripts/analyze_video_gemini.py
+
+All three workflows call this single entry point. Output is a unified
+`video_analysis.json` that each workflow reads selectively.
+
+Usage:
+    python3 analyze_cli.py \\
+        --video source.mp4 \\
+        --out-dir runs/run-01/ \\
+        [--model gemini-3.1-pro-preview] \\
+        [--max-chunk-duration 14] \\
+        [--max-tokens 12000]
+
+Output:
+    <out-dir>/video_analysis.json
+
+Environment variables required:
+    LITELLM_API_BASE
+    LITELLM_API_KEY
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import base64
+import json
+import os
+import re
+import subprocess
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Gemini prompt
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+GEMINI_PROMPT = """\
+You are simultaneously a seasoned commercial film director, a senior storyboard artist,
+and a professional video prompt engineer for AI video generation models.
+
+Watch this video in full — every frame, every cut, every audio beat, every micro-movement.
+Output a SINGLE JSON object as described below.
+Output ONLY raw JSON starting with { and ending with }. No markdown, no backticks, no explanation.
+
+Your analysis serves THREE downstream tasks at once:
+  1. AI video re-generation (Seedance) — needs subject motion + camera motion + lighting + emotion
+  2. Storyboard panel redrawing (image model) — needs visual_static + frame_time per shot
+  3. Workflow selection — needs video_purpose + narrative structure + substitution hints
+
+Think and write like a director briefing a crew. Every field should contain what a
+DoP, AC, or editor would actually say on set — not what a caption writer would describe.
+Avoid enumerating fixed options; use your own precise language for each shot.
+
+SCHEMA:
+{
+  "meta": {
+    "ratio": "{RATIO}",
+    "duration_s": {DURATION},
+    "language": "spoken language in the video, or 'none'",
+    "video_type": "your own label describing genre + sub-type, e.g. '奢侈品珠宝广告·品牌形象' or 'lifestyle product unboxing·warm tonality'",
+    "video_purpose": "2-3 sentences: what this video achieves, who it targets, why it works emotionally and commercially"
+  },
+
+  "workflow_recommendation": {
+    "recommended": "style-duplicate | storyboard-replicate | storyboard-studio",
+    "reason": "1-2 sentences grounded in the video's actual structure — why this workflow fits",
+    "substitution_hints": {
+      "person":    "describe the swappable person/model element and their role",
+      "product":   "describe the hero product — name, material, distinguishing features",
+      "scene":     "describe the dominant environment that could be replaced",
+      "voiceover": "describe VO presence, language, and tone — or 'none'"
+    }
+  },
+
+  "global_style": {
+    "color_palette":       "dominant colors and their emotional meaning across the whole video",
+    "lighting_signature":  "DoP-level summary: key light approach, quality, color temperature, signature technique",
+    "visual_feel":         "commercial register and emotional texture — what kind of world does this video live in",
+    "camera_signature":    "the director's dominant camera language as a single characterization",
+    "edit_rhythm":         "cutting pattern with emotional intent — pace, regularity, how cuts land",
+    "sound_signature":     "BGM character, VO presence, ambient texture, how sound supports the visual"
+  },
+
+  "voice_tone": {
+    "has_voiceover": true,
+    "gender":        "perceived gender of the speaker, or 'none'",
+    "age_range":     "estimated age feel, or 'N/A'",
+    "language":      "spoken language",
+    "tone":          "emotional quality of the delivery",
+    "pace":          "speaking pace as felt by the viewer",
+    "characteristic":"what makes this voice distinctive or memorable"
+  },
+
+  "narrative_structure": {
+    "hook":       "exactly how the first 3 seconds grab attention — be specific about the technique",
+    "arc":        "the emotional and informational journey of the full video",
+    "cta":        "what action or emotional state the viewer is left in at the end"
+  },
+
+  "chunks": [
+    {
+      "chunk_id": 1,
+      "start": 0.0,
+      "end": 12.5,
+      "narrative_label": "your own label for this chunk's narrative role, e.g. 'hook', 'product reveal', 'emotional proof', 'brand close'",
+      "voiceover": [
+        {"start": 0.0, "end": 3.1, "text": "exact transcript with natural punctuation"}
+      ],
+      "scenes": [
+        {
+          "scene_id": 1,
+          "start": 0.0,
+          "end": 2.8,
+          "frame_time": 0.5,
+
+          "subject": {
+            "description": "Who or what is the primary subject. For a person: perceived age, ethnicity, hair, skin, makeup, outfit including fabrics and fit, jewelry and accessories — described specifically enough for an image model to draw them. For a product: full visual description. For a scene/object/graphic: describe for redrawing.",
+            "position":    "Where in the frame the subject sits at shot start — be spatial and specific",
+            "screen_trajectory": "How the subject moves through the frame across the shot duration — from where to where, at what pace. Describe as spatial movement in the frame (left-to-right, foreground-to-mid, descends in frame, holds position). Separate from subject_motion which describes the body action itself."
+          },
+
+          "spatial_layers": {
+            "foreground":  "What is in the foreground — objects, blur, framing elements. 'none' if absent.",
+            "midground":   "The primary subject plane — what is here",
+            "background":  "What is visible behind — color, texture, depth, any elements",
+            "depth_feel":  "How the three-dimensional space feels — compressed, deep, flat, intimate, vast"
+          },
+
+          "action": {
+            "subject_motion": "MOST IMPORTANT FIELD. The complete body motion arc of the primary subject, timestamped. Describe: initial body state → what moves, in what direction, at what speed and quality (deliberate, fluid, snappy, weightless) → end state. e.g. 'model stationary throughout; at 0.3s right hand lifts slowly from side, fingers extend and graze pendant at 0.9s, hand settles — no other movement' or 'no body displacement — held still, only chest rises with a single breath at ~1.0s'. If no person: describe the object's motion arc.",
+            "micro_expression": "Facial state and its evolution through the shot — eye direction and quality (soft gaze, sharp focus, averted), brow tension, lip set and any micro-change, jaw angle, neck line. Describe as a continuous state, not a list of attributes.",
+            "product_behavior": "How the product moves, is positioned, catches light, reveals texture, or sways. Describe the quality of any motion. If static: describe the intent of the stillness.",
+            "interaction":  "The physical and visual relationship between person and product — proximity, touch quality, gaze connection, any reflection or shadow relationship. 'none' if only one is present."
+          },
+
+          "camera": {
+            "framing":     "How the shot frames the subject — what is included and excluded, what the frame emphasizes. Use director language, not technical labels.",
+            "motion":      "Describe what the camera does across the shot duration: direction, speed, quality, and the emotional effect of that movement. If the camera does not move, name the quality of that stillness. Never leave this as just 'static'.",
+            "composition": "The compositional logic of this frame — balance, weight, use of negative space, leading lines, symmetry or asymmetry, what the composition says",
+            "stability":   "The physical feel of the camera — locked, mechanically smooth, organically moving, intimately shaky",
+            "lens_feel":   "The perceptual quality of the lens — focal length impression (wide, compressed, intimate), any distortion, breathing, or optical character",
+            "rhythm_feel": "The emotional pacing energy of this shot as a director would feel it on the edit"
+          },
+
+          "lighting": {
+            "key_source":    "Primary light: position relative to subject, quality (hard/soft), size impression, color temperature",
+            "fill_and_rim":  "Fill light quality and ratio; any rim, backlight, or separation light; 'none' if absent",
+            "practical_lights": "Any visible light sources in frame — windows, screens, candles, practicals",
+            "shadow_quality": "Hardness and direction of shadows — what they contribute to the mood",
+            "light_motion":  "Any movement in the lighting during the shot — shifting, flickering, sweeping, glinting on surface. 'static' if none.",
+            "color_mood":    "The emotional color read of this specific shot — what the combination of hues says"
+          },
+
+          "style": {
+            "color_grade":    "The color grading treatment of this shot — shadows, highlights, saturation, any stylization",
+            "texture_quality":"The tactile and sharpness quality — what can you almost feel through the screen",
+            "depth_of_field": "What is in focus, what is not, and the depth feel this creates",
+            "focus_pull":     "Any rack focus or focus shift during the shot. 'none' if absent.",
+            "commercial_feel":"The genre and register this shot belongs to — what world of advertising does it live in"
+          },
+
+          "emotional_arc": "The internal dramatic arc within this single shot — how tension, emotion, or energy moves from shot start to cut. Even a quiet shot has an arc: name it.",
+
+          "vfx_and_grade": "Any visual effects, speed changes, in-camera techniques, or distinctive grade treatment applied. Describe what you see: speed ramping, slow-motion, film grain, lens flares, bleach bypass, digital overlays, etc. 'none' if clean.",
+
+          "audio_sync": "How sound relates to the image in this shot — does the BGM beat align with a cut or action? Does the VO breath match a visual pause? Does ambient sound reinforce or contrast the visual? Describe the audio-visual relationship, not just the sound itself.",
+
+          "visual_static": "ENGLISH ONLY. A complete frozen-frame description at frame_time, written for an image generation model. Include: exact framing, subject position and appearance, spatial layers, light direction and quality, color, expression at that moment. Self-contained — the image model sees nothing else.",
+
+          "visual_motion":  "ENGLISH ONLY. A full motion arc description for a video generation model, using temporal verbs. Describe what moves, when, how, in what direction, with what quality. Cover: subject motion, camera motion, product motion, light changes. Do NOT include the transition — that goes in transition_out.",
+
+          "transition_out": "How this shot ends and connects to the next: the type, speed, and feel of the transition. For the final shot: how the video closes. Never leave empty.",
+
+          "audio": {
+            "voiceover_text": "exact words spoken during this scene, empty string if none",
+            "voice_style":    "the quality and delivery of the voice in this moment",
+            "ambient":        "environmental and background sound texture in this shot"
+          }
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  ]
+}
+
+RULES:
+1. Chunks: split at natural VO breath boundaries or narrative arc transitions. Max {MAX_CHUNK_DURATION}s each. Never split mid-sentence.
+2. voiceover[]: transcribe EXACTLY what you hear, every word, with natural punctuation. Timestamps to ±0.3s. Empty array [] if no VO.
+3. scenes — CRITICAL: one scene = one continuous camera shot. Capture every hard cut and transition, no matter how brief. Never merge two shots. A missed cut = a missing panel. Err on more scenes.
+4. frame_time: start + 0.5s. If scene < 1.0s: start + (end-start)/2.
+5. camera.motion — never write just 'static'. Every shot has a camera intention. Name what the camera does or deliberately does not do, and why it works.
+6. action.subject_motion — the single most important field for video generation. Timestamp the motion arc. 'Stationary throughout' is valid but must be explicit. Without this, AI models generate frozen figures.
+7. action.micro_expression — describe as a continuous state evolving through the shot, not a checklist. What does the face say, and does it change?
+8. spatial_layers — commercial videos are carefully lit and composed in depth. Name what is in each plane.
+9. audio_sync — the relationship between sound and image is a directorial choice. Describe it.
+10. visual_static — write as a self-contained brief for an image model. It sees nothing else.
+11. visual_motion — write as a self-contained brief for a video model. Temporal verbs, full arc.
+12. Do NOT invent fixed option lists in your output. Describe what you actually see in your own precise language. Every video is different.
+13. All visual content fields must be in ENGLISH. voiceover_text and narrative_label may be in the video's original language.
+14. Output ONLY the JSON object. No other text. No markdown.
+"""
+
+
+
+
+def build_prompt(ratio: str, duration: float, max_chunk_duration: int = 14) -> str:
+    return (GEMINI_PROMPT
+            .replace("{RATIO}", ratio)
+            .replace("{DURATION}", f"{duration:.1f}")
+            .replace("{MAX_CHUNK_DURATION}", str(max_chunk_duration)))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Video metadata
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def probe_video(video_path: Path) -> dict:
+    cmd = [
+        "ffprobe", "-v", "quiet", "-print_format", "json",
+        "-show_streams", "-show_format", str(video_path),
+    ]
+    try:
+        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
+        info = json.loads(out)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        print(f"  [warn] ffprobe failed ({exc}); using defaults", file=sys.stderr)
+        return {"width": 1080, "height": 1920, "duration_s": 0.0, "ratio": "9:16"}
+
+    width = height = 0
+    for s in info.get("streams", []):
+        if s.get("codec_type") == "video":
+            width  = int(s.get("width",  0) or 0)
+            height = int(s.get("height", 0) or 0)
+            break
+    duration_s = float((info.get("format") or {}).get("duration", 0) or 0)
+    return {"width": width, "height": height,
+            "duration_s": duration_s, "ratio": _infer_ratio(width, height)}
+
+
+def _infer_ratio(w: int, h: int) -> str:
+    if w == 0 or h == 0:
+        return "9:16"
+    ar = w / h
+    candidates = {"21:9": 21/9, "16:9": 16/9, "4:3": 4/3,
+                  "1:1": 1.0, "3:4": 3/4, "9:16": 9/16}
+    return min(candidates, key=lambda k: abs(candidates[k] - ar))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Gemini call
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def call_gemini(video_path: Path, prompt: str, model: str, max_tokens: int) -> str:
+    """Call Gemini via LiteLLM proxy. Returns raw text content."""
+    import urllib.request
+
+    base = os.environ["LITELLM_API_BASE"].rstrip("/")
+    key  = os.environ["LITELLM_API_KEY"]
+
+    video_bytes = video_path.read_bytes()
+    video_b64   = base64.b64encode(video_bytes).decode()
+    _mime = {".mp4": "video/mp4", ".mov": "video/quicktime",
+             ".webm": "video/webm"}.get(video_path.suffix.lower(), "video/mp4")
+
+    print(f"  video: {len(video_bytes)/1024:.0f} KB  model: {model}  "
+          f"prompt: {len(prompt)} chars", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    payload = {
+        "model": model,
+        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
+        "temperature": 0.1,
+        "messages": [{
+            "role": "user",
+            "content": [
+                {"type": "text", "text": prompt},
+                {"type": "image_url",
+                 "image_url": {"url": f"data:{_mime};base64,{video_b64}"}},
+            ],
+        }],
+    }
+    data = json.dumps(payload).encode()
+    req  = urllib.request.Request(
+        f"{base}/chat/completions",
+        data=data,
+        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json",
+                 "Authorization": f"Bearer {key}"},
+        method="POST",
+    )
+    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=300) as resp:
+        return json.loads(resp.read())["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# JSON extraction
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def extract_json(text: str) -> str:
+    text = text.strip()
+    # Strip markdown fences
+    text = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", text)
+    text = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", text)
+    text = text.strip()
+    # Find outermost { ... }
+    start = text.find("{")
+    if start == -1:
+        raise ValueError("No JSON object found in response")
+    depth, end = 0, -1
+    for i, ch in enumerate(text[start:], start):
+        if ch == "{":
+            depth += 1
+        elif ch == "}":
+            depth -= 1
+            if depth == 0:
+                end = i
+                break
+    if end == -1:
+        raise ValueError("Unbalanced JSON object")
+    return text[start:end + 1]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Post-processing: ensure frame_time on every scene
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def _fix_frame_times(data: dict) -> dict:
+    for chunk in data.get("chunks", []):
+        for scene in chunk.get("scenes", []):
+            s, e = float(scene.get("start", 0)), float(scene.get("end", 0))
+            dur = e - s
+            if "frame_time" not in scene or scene["frame_time"] is None:
+                scene["frame_time"] = round(s + (0.5 if dur >= 1.0 else dur / 2), 3)
+    return data
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Main
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Unified Gemini video analysis")
+    ap.add_argument("--video",              required=True)
+    ap.add_argument("--out-dir",   "-o",    required=True)
+    ap.add_argument("--model",              default="gemini-3.1-pro-preview")
+    ap.add_argument("--max-chunk-duration", type=int, default=14)
+    ap.add_argument("--max-tokens",         type=int, default=12000)
+    ap.add_argument("--force",              action="store_true",
+                    help="Re-analyze even if video_analysis.json already exists")
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    for var in ("LITELLM_API_BASE", "LITELLM_API_KEY"):
+        if not os.environ.get(var):
+            sys.exit(f"env {var} not set")
+
+    video_path = Path(args.video).expanduser().resolve()
+    if not video_path.is_file():
+        sys.exit(f"video not found: {video_path}")
+
+    out_dir  = Path(args.out_dir).expanduser().resolve()
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    out_path = out_dir / "video_analysis.json"
+
+    # Cache: skip re-analysis if already done (unless --force)
+    if out_path.exists() and not args.force:
+        print(f"[video-analyze] cached result found → {out_path} (use --force to re-run)",
+              file=sys.stderr)
+        data = json.loads(out_path.read_text())
+        _print_summary(data)
+        return
+
+    # Probe
+    print("[video-analyze] Probing video metadata…", file=sys.stderr)
+    meta = probe_video(video_path)
+    print(f"  ratio={meta['ratio']}  {meta['width']}x{meta['height']}  "
+          f"duration={meta['duration_s']:.1f}s", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    # Call Gemini
+    prompt = build_prompt(meta["ratio"], meta["duration_s"], args.max_chunk_duration)
+    print(f"[video-analyze] Calling Gemini ({args.model})…", file=sys.stderr)
+    raw_text = call_gemini(video_path, prompt, args.model, args.max_tokens)
+
+    # Parse
+    try:
+        json_str = extract_json(raw_text)
+        data     = json.loads(json_str)
+    except (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
+        raw_path = out_dir / "video_analysis_raw.txt"
+        raw_path.write_text(raw_text, encoding="utf-8")
+        sys.exit(f"[video-analyze] JSON parse failed: {exc}\n  Raw saved → {raw_path}")
+
+    # Ensure meta fields from ffprobe are authoritative
+    data.setdefault("meta", {})
+    data["meta"]["ratio"]      = meta["ratio"]
+    data["meta"]["duration_s"] = meta["duration_s"]
+
+    # Post-process
+    data = _fix_frame_times(data)
+
+    # Write
+    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
+    print(f"[video-analyze] Done → {out_path}", file=sys.stderr)
+    _print_summary(data)
+
+    # Print JSON path for downstream pipeline consumption
+    print(json.dumps({"video_analysis_json": str(out_path)}, ensure_ascii=False))
+
+
+def _print_summary(data: dict) -> None:
+    meta     = data.get("meta", {})
+    rec      = data.get("workflow_recommendation", {})
+    chunks   = data.get("chunks", [])
+    n_scenes = sum(len(c.get("scenes", [])) for c in chunks)
+    print(f"  type:     {meta.get('video_type', '?')}", file=sys.stderr)
+    print(f"  duration: {meta.get('duration_s', '?')}s  chunks: {len(chunks)}  scenes: {n_scenes}",
+          file=sys.stderr)
+    print(f"  recommended workflow: {rec.get('recommended', '?')} — {rec.get('reason', '')}",
+          file=sys.stderr)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

pr_body:
## Summary
- Add new pack `video-duplicate/` (agentPack_id: `video-duplicate`) from `build/video-duplicate-1.6.4.tar.gz`
- This is a separate pack from the existing `video-ads-duplicate/` (different agentPack_id `video_ads_duplicate`), so both coexist
- Fix `description.json` version field (upstream build left it at `1.6.3`; now synced to `1.6.4` to match `agent-pack.yaml` and CHANGELOG)

## Pack overview
- Role: 分镜复刻·剪辑编导大师 (AI Short-Video Replication Director)
- Three workflows: Workflow C (storyboard-guided, recommended) / A (frame-precise) / B (style transfer)
- Stack: Gemini 3.1 Pro · gpt-image-2 · Seedance 2.0 Pro · Demucs · ffmpeg

## Notes
- Upstream 1.6.4 fix vs 1.6.3: removed broken `subtitle_burn.py` reference in `agent-pack.yaml`, migrated 3 pipelines to `transcribe.sh` + `burn.sh`, dropped bundled 1.6.1 tarball leftovers (`artifacts/`, `zip/`)
- `subtitle_burn.py.deprecated` is still shipped in the tarball as dead weight (no longer referenced by yaml); not blocking — flag for upstream build cleanup

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify pack loads at runtime in an OpenClaw bot pod
- [ ] Smoke-test Workflow C (storyboard-guided) end-to-end
- [ ] Confirm `video-ads-duplicate` pack remains unaffected

+---
+name: pack-onboarding
+description: First-time setup for video-duplicate. Run when data/config.json does not exist. Collects user preferences and initializes the agent. Triggers on first use or /init.
+---
+
+# Onboarding — video-duplicate Setup
+
+1 steps, ~1 user turns. Target: under 5 minutes.
+
+## Language
+
+Read `lang` from `USER.md`. Default to `zh`.
+
+## State Saving
+
+Before each step, save progress:
+```json
+// data/.test-state.json
+{"phase":"onboarding","onboarding_step":N,"onboarding_answers":{...}}
+```
+On completion, delete `data/.test-state.json`.
+
+## Step 0 — Session Context Capture (silent, no user prompt)
+
+Resolve delivery identifiers and save to `data/config.json`. **Never ask the user** — these are platform identifiers.
+
+1. `agent_id`, `account_id` — from the `<!-- BEGIN:ZooClaw-Agent-Policy -->` block in `AGENTS.md`. Main agent → `account_id = "default"`; specialists → `account_id = agent_id`.
+2. `channel` — `mattermost` (override if the live session key says otherwise).
+3. `peer_id` — parse from the session key `agent:<agent_id>:<channel>:<account_id>:direct:<peer_id>` (env `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` or platform session metadata).
+4. `timezone` — from `USER.md` (injected by the platform; default `UTC` if missing).
+
+Write `{"agent_id":"...","account_id":"...","channel":"mattermost","peer_id":"...","timezone":"..."}` to `data/config.json`.
+
+If any value can't be resolved, defer cron-job creation and log a retry note in `MEMORY.md` — never surface this to the user.
+
+## Step 1 — Welcome
+
+Ask: "👋 I am Video Duplicate — give me a reference video and I will recreate it with your brand. Let me get you set up in a few seconds."
+
+
+## Provision (auto)
+
+Writes `data/config.json` and substitutes `{{key}}` / `{{config.key}}` placeholders in `SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md`.
+
+```bash
+uv run --python 3.12 scripts/provision.py --config '<json>'
+```
+
+## Finalize (auto)
+
+Merges `data/config.json` + `data/*_baseline.json` into `MEMORY.md` and `data/plan.json`, deletes `data/.test-state.json`.
+
+```bash
+uv run --python 3.12 scripts/finalize.py
+```
+
+## Complete
+
+Send completion message with available commands. Switch to normal mode.

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Resolve {{config.*}} placeholders in a cron job template against data/config.json.
+
+The agent pipes the cron template JSON through this script before calling the
+platform `cron` tool. Fails loudly if any placeholder or required delivery
+field is missing — automation.md warns that omitting any of those causes
+silent misdelivery.
+"""
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import re
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+_REQUIRED_DELIVERY = ("channel", "accountId", "to")
+_PLACEHOLDER_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{config\.([\w]+)\}\}")
+
+
+def main():
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    parser.add_argument("--config", default="data/config.json",
+                        help="Path to config.json (default: data/config.json)")
+    parser.add_argument("--template", default="-",
+                        help="Path to job template JSON, or '-' for stdin (default)")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    config_path = Path(args.config)
+    if not config_path.exists():
+        _fail(f"{args.config} not found — run onboarding before adding crons")
+
+    try:
+        config = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
+        _fail(f"{args.config} is not valid JSON: {e}")
+
+    template = sys.stdin.read() if args.template == "-" else Path(args.template).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+
+    placeholders = sorted(set(_PLACEHOLDER_RE.findall(template)))
+    # Treat None / "" as missing but accept 0 / False — `config.get(k)` would
+    # falsely flag a numeric peer_id of 0 as missing.
+    missing = [k for k in placeholders if k not in config or config[k] in (None, "")]
+    if missing:
+        _fail(f"data/config.json missing/empty keys for placeholders: {missing}", missing=missing)
+
+    resolved = _PLACEHOLDER_RE.sub(lambda m: str(config[m.group(1)]), template)
+
+    try:
+        job = json.loads(resolved)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
+        _fail(f"resolved job is not valid JSON: {e}", resolved=resolved)
+
+    delivery = (job.get("job") or {}).get("delivery") or {}
+    missing_delivery = [f for f in _REQUIRED_DELIVERY if f not in delivery or delivery[f] in (None, "")]
+    if missing_delivery:
+        _fail(
+            f"job.delivery missing/empty required fields: {missing_delivery} (would cause silent misdelivery)",
+            missing_delivery=missing_delivery,
+        )
+
+    json.dump({"status": "ok", "job": job}, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
+
+
+def _fail(message, **extra):
+    payload = {"status": "error", "message": message}
+    payload.update(extra)
+    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False)
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Finalize: merge preferences + baselines into MEMORY.md and initial plan.
+
+Reads:
+- data/config.json — user preferences from interactive steps
+- data/*_baseline.json (or data/baseline.json) — outputs from history_scan
+
+Writes:
+- MEMORY.md at workspace root (not memory/ subdir)
+- data/plan.json — initial plan stub keyed off preferences + baseline summary
+
+Returns `corrupt_baselines` in the JSON output so the caller can surface
+which baseline files failed to parse — silent skip would mask broken scans.
+"""
+
+import json
+import sys
+from datetime import date
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+def main():
+    workspace = Path.cwd()
+    today = date.today().isoformat()
+
+    config_path = workspace / "data" / "config.json"
+    config = {}
+    if config_path.exists():
+        try:
+            config = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
+            _fail(f"data/config.json is not valid JSON: {e}")
+
+    baselines, corrupt = _collect_baselines(workspace / "data")
+
+    memory_path = workspace / "MEMORY.md"
+    memory_path.write_text(_render_memory(today, config, baselines), encoding="utf-8")
+
+    plan_path = workspace / "data" / "plan.json"
+    plan_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    plan_path.write_text(json.dumps({
+        "created": today,
+        "preferences": config,
+        "baselines": {name: _baseline_summary(b) for name, b in baselines.items()},
+        "next_actions": [],
+    }, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+    progress = workspace / "data" / ".test-state.json"
+    if progress.exists():
+        progress.unlink()
+
+    json.dump({
+        "status": "ok",
+        "memory": str(memory_path.relative_to(workspace)),
+        "plan": str(plan_path.relative_to(workspace)),
+        "baselines": sorted(baselines.keys()),
+        "corrupt_baselines": corrupt,
+    }, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False)
+
+
+def _collect_baselines(data_dir):
+    """Returns (baselines_dict, list_of_corrupt_filenames).
+
+    Corrupt files are reported, not raised — onboarding should finalize
+    even if one scan produced garbage, but the operator must be told.
+    """
+    if not data_dir.exists():
+        return {}, []
+    baselines = {}
+    corrupt = []
+    files = sorted(data_dir.glob("*_baseline.json"))
+    if not files:
+        single = data_dir / "baseline.json"
+        if single.exists():
+            files = [single]
+    for f in files:
+        try:
+            baselines[f.stem] = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+        except json.JSONDecodeError:
+            corrupt.append(f.name)
+    return baselines, corrupt
+
+
+def _baseline_summary(baseline):
+    if not isinstance(baseline, dict):
+        return {"records": 0}
+    data = baseline.get("data")
+    return {
+        "connector": baseline.get("connector"),
+        "records": len(data) if isinstance(data, list) else 0,
+        "note": baseline.get("note"),
+    }
+
+
+def _render_memory(today, config, baselines):
+    lines = ["# Memory", "", f"## Onboarding completed {today}", ""]
+    if config:
+        lines.append("### Preferences")
+        lines.append("")
+        for k, v in config.items():
+            lines.append(f"- **{k}**: {v}")
+        lines.append("")
+    if baselines:
+        lines.append("### Baselines")
+        lines.append("")
+        for name, b in baselines.items():
+            s = _baseline_summary(b)
+            note = f" — {s['note']}" if s.get("note") else ""
+            lines.append(f"- **{name}**: {s['records']} records{note}")
+        lines.append("")
+    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"
+
+
+def _fail(message, **extra):
+    payload = {"status": "error", "message": message}
+    payload.update(extra)
+    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False)
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Provision: write config and personalize workspace from onboarding responses.
+
+Mechanically substitutes `{{key}}` and `{{config.key}}` placeholders in
+SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md from the collected config — no LLM string replacement.
+
+Exits 1 if any placeholder remains unresolved: a deployed pack shipping
+literal `{{persona_description}}` in SOUL.md is the OB-1 failure mode this
+script exists to close.
+"""
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import re
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+_PERSONALIZE = ("SOUL.md", "IDENTITY.md")
+_PLACEHOLDER_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{([\w.]+)\}\}")
+
+
+def main():
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    parser.add_argument("--config", required=True, help="JSON string of collected config")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    try:
+        config = json.loads(args.config)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
+        _fail(f"--config is not valid JSON: {e}")
+
+    workspace = Path.cwd()
+    config_path = workspace / "data" / "config.json"
+
+    # /test mode signal: pack template files live under agent/. Deployed packs
+    # have no agent/ subdir (it's stripped at install time). Checking for the
+    # actual template files — not just the directory — avoids a stale-or-empty
+    # agent/ silently disabling personalization when we should be running.
+    in_studio_test = any(
+        (workspace / "agent" / name).is_file() for name in _PERSONALIZE
+    )
+
+    # Stage all substitutions BEFORE any disk write — config.json is the
+    # onboarding sentinel (per automation.md), so writing it before validation
+    # passes would leave the pack in a false-complete state on _fail.
+    pending = []
+    unresolved = set()
+    if not in_studio_test:
+        for relpath in _PERSONALIZE:
+            target = workspace / relpath
+            if not target.exists():
+                continue
+            original = target.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+            substituted, leftover = _substitute(original, config)
+            unresolved.update(leftover)
+            if substituted != original:
+                pending.append((target, substituted))
+
+    if unresolved:
+        _fail(
+            f"unresolved placeholders: {sorted(unresolved)} — "
+            "pack would ship literal {{...}} to the user. "
+            "Add the missing keys to onboarding config, or remove the placeholders from the template.",
+            unresolved_placeholders=sorted(unresolved),
+        )
+
+    config_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    config_path.write_text(json.dumps(config, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+    personalized = []
+    for target, substituted in pending:
+        target.write_text(substituted, encoding="utf-8")
+        personalized.append(str(target.relative_to(workspace)))
+
+    result = {
+        "status": "ok",
+        "config_path": str(config_path.relative_to(workspace)),
+        "personalized": personalized,
+    }
+    if in_studio_test:
+        result["skipped_reason"] = "agent/ pack template present — /test mode; personalization runs at real-deploy onboarding"
+    json.dump(result, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False)
+
+
+def _substitute(text, config):
+    """Replace {{key}} and {{config.key}} with values from config.
+
+    Returns (new_text, set_of_unresolved_keys). Unresolved placeholders are
+    left intact so the caller can surface them and exit.
+    """
+    unresolved = set()
+
+    def repl(match):
+        key = match.group(1)
+        lookup = key[len("config."):] if key.startswith("config.") else key
+        if lookup in config and config[lookup] not in (None, ""):
+            return str(config[lookup])
+        unresolved.add(key)
+        return match.group(0)
+
+    return _PLACEHOLDER_RE.sub(repl, text), unresolved
+
+
+def _fail(message, **extra):
+    payload = {"status": "error", "message": message}
+    payload.update(extra)
+    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False)
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
