+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Stage 6 — compose a multi-shot storyboard panel for ONE chunk.
+
+Usage (spike / single-chunk mode):
+    python3 panel_compose_cli.py \\
+        --shot spike/shots/shot_01.png:0.0:2.14 \\
+        --shot spike/shots/shot_02.png:2.14:6.43 \\
+        --shot spike/shots/shot_03.png:6.43:10.71 \\
+        --shot spike/shots/shot_04.png:10.71:15.0 \\
+        --source-frame 1080x1920 \\
+        --tier 2k \\
+        --out spike/panels/chunk_01_source.png
+
+The full pipeline will feed this from chunks.json (chunk_to_panels mode, TBD).
+For now we expose the single-chunk form — that's all Spike 1 / 2 need.
+
+Burned overlays:
+    - top-left: rounded badge "#NN" (28px font)
+    - bottom strip: 36px black bar with timestamp "M:SS-M:SS" (24px font)
+
+Both overlays are anchors the LLM / gpt-image-2 / Seedance can use to keep
+shot order straight even after the panel is redrawn.
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import sys
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
+
+sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
+from _grid import GridPlan, describe, panel_box, pick_grid  # noqa: E402
+
+BADGE_FONT_PX = 28
+TIMESTAMP_FONT_PX = 24
+TIMESTAMP_STRIP_PX = 36
+BADGE_PAD_X = 12
+BADGE_PAD_Y = 6
+BADGE_RADIUS = 6
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Shot:
+    path: Path
+    start: float
+    end: float
+    index: int  # 1-based shot id within the panel
+
+
+def _parse_shot_spec(spec: str, idx_1based: int) -> Shot:
+    # Format: <path>:<start>:<end>  (path can contain colons on Windows but we
+    # operate on POSIX-only inputs — split from the right twice).
+    parts = spec.rsplit(":", 2)
+    if len(parts) != 3:
+        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(
+            f"--shot expects <path>:<start>:<end>, got: {spec!r}"
+        )
+    path_str, start_str, end_str = parts
+    path = Path(path_str).expanduser().resolve()
+    if not path.is_file():
+        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"shot image not found: {path}")
+    try:
+        start = float(start_str)
+        end = float(end_str)
+    except ValueError as exc:
+        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"invalid start/end in {spec!r}") from exc
+    if end <= start:
+        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"end must be > start in {spec!r}")
+    return Shot(path=path, start=start, end=end, index=idx_1based)
+
+
+def _parse_size(spec: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
+    try:
+        w_str, h_str = spec.lower().split("x", 1)
+        return int(w_str), int(h_str)
+    except (ValueError, AttributeError) as exc:
+        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(
+            f"--source-frame expects WxH (e.g. 1080x1920), got: {spec!r}"
+        ) from exc
+
+
+def _load_font(size_px: int) -> ImageFont.FreeTypeFont:
+    # macOS-friendly fallbacks. PingFang renders CJK + Latin cleanly.
+    for candidate in (
+        "/System/Library/Fonts/PingFang.ttc",
+        "/System/Library/Fonts/Helvetica.ttc",
+        "/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial.ttf",
+    ):
+        if Path(candidate).exists():
+            try:
+                return ImageFont.truetype(candidate, size_px)
+            except OSError:
+                continue
+    return ImageFont.load_default()
+
+
+def _fmt_timestamp(start: float, end: float) -> str:
+    def mmss(t: float) -> str:
+        m = int(t) // 60
+        s = t - m * 60
+        return f"{m}:{s:04.1f}"
+    return f"{mmss(start)}–{mmss(end)}"
+
+
+def _draw_badge(draw: ImageDraw.ImageDraw, x: int, y: int, text: str,
+                font: ImageFont.FreeTypeFont) -> None:
+    # Rounded black rect with white text.
+    bbox = font.getbbox(text)
+    text_w = bbox[2] - bbox[0]
+    text_h = bbox[3] - bbox[1]
+    rect_w = text_w + 2 * BADGE_PAD_X
+    rect_h = text_h + 2 * BADGE_PAD_Y
+    draw.rounded_rectangle(
+        (x, y, x + rect_w, y + rect_h),
+        radius=BADGE_RADIUS,
+        fill=(0, 0, 0, 230),
+    )
+    # Pillow font baseline weirdness — re-offset using bbox top.
+    draw.text(
+        (x + BADGE_PAD_X - bbox[0], y + BADGE_PAD_Y - bbox[1]),
+        text,
+        fill=(255, 255, 255, 255),
+        font=font,
+    )
+
+
+def _draw_timestamp_strip(draw: ImageDraw.ImageDraw, box: tuple[int, int, int, int],
+                          text: str, font: ImageFont.FreeTypeFont) -> None:
+    x0, _, x1, y1 = box
+    strip_y0 = y1 - TIMESTAMP_STRIP_PX
+    draw.rectangle((x0, strip_y0, x1, y1), fill=(0, 0, 0, 230))
+    bbox = font.getbbox(text)
+    text_w = bbox[2] - bbox[0]
+    text_h = bbox[3] - bbox[1]
+    tx = x0 + ((x1 - x0) - text_w) // 2 - bbox[0]
+    ty = strip_y0 + (TIMESTAMP_STRIP_PX - text_h) // 2 - bbox[1]
+    draw.text((tx, ty), text, fill=(255, 255, 255, 255), font=font)
+
+
+def compose_panel(
+    shots: list[Shot],
+    *,
+    frame_w: int,
+    frame_h: int,
+    tier: str = "2k",
+    background: tuple[int, int, int] = (0, 0, 0),
+) -> tuple[Image.Image, GridPlan]:
+    if not shots:
+        raise ValueError("no shots provided")
+    plan = pick_grid(len(shots), frame_w=frame_w, frame_h=frame_h, tier=tier)
+    board = Image.new("RGB", (plan.board_w, plan.board_h), background)
+    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(board, "RGBA")
+    badge_font = _load_font(BADGE_FONT_PX)
+    ts_font = _load_font(TIMESTAMP_FONT_PX)
+
+    for idx, shot in enumerate(shots):
+        box = panel_box(plan, idx)
+        x0, y0, x1, y1 = box
+        target_w = x1 - x0
+        target_h = y1 - y0
+        with Image.open(shot.path) as src:
+            src = src.convert("RGB")
+            resized = src.resize((target_w, target_h), Image.LANCZOS)
+            board.paste(resized, (x0, y0))
+        # Badge top-left of the painted panel.
+        _draw_badge(draw, x0 + 12, y0 + 12, f"#{shot.index:02d}", badge_font)
+        # Timestamp strip along the bottom of the painted panel.
+        _draw_timestamp_strip(
+            draw, box, _fmt_timestamp(shot.start, shot.end), ts_font
+        )
+
+    return board, plan
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
+        description="Compose a multi-shot storyboard panel for one chunk."
+    )
+    ap.add_argument(
+        "--shot",
+        action="append",
+        required=True,
+        metavar="PATH:START:END",
+        help="Shot spec, repeatable up to 12 times. Order = panel order.",
+    )
+    ap.add_argument(
+        "--source-frame",
+        type=_parse_size,
+        required=True,
+        help="Source video frame size WxH, e.g. 1080x1920.",
+    )
+    ap.add_argument(
+        "--tier",
+        choices=["1k", "2k", "4k"],
+        default="2k",
+        help="gpt-image-2 output tier; controls max board edge.",
+    )
+    ap.add_argument(
+        "--out",
+        required=True,
+        help="Output PNG path.",
+    )
+    ap.add_argument(
+        "--start-idx",
+        type=int,
+        default=1,
+        help="First shot's badge number (default 1). Use for chunk panels where "
+             "the chunk doesn't start at shot #01 (e.g. chunk 2 starts at #05).",
+    )
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    shots = [_parse_shot_spec(spec, idx + args.start_idx) for idx, spec in enumerate(args.shot)]
+    if len(shots) > 12:
+        ap.error("max 12 shots per panel")
+
+    frame_w, frame_h = args.source_frame
+    panel, plan = compose_panel(shots, frame_w=frame_w, frame_h=frame_h, tier=args.tier)
+    out_path = Path(args.out).expanduser().resolve()
+    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    panel.save(out_path, format="PNG", optimize=True)
+    print(f"OK  {describe(plan)}  -> {out_path}")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""storyboard-studio orchestrator — runs all stages end-to-end.
+
+ARCHITECTURE (V3 — Gemini-native, no PySceneDetect, shared execution/post-production layers):
+    Stage 1: Gemini 3.1 Pro watches mp4 → scene_analysis.json
+             (chunks, scenes with frame_time + visual_static + visual_motion, VO+timestamps,
+              voice_tone, video_type, video_purpose)
+    Stage 2: Extract one frame per scene via ffmpeg at scene.frame_time (start+0.5s)
+             Compose per-chunk source panels → panels/source/chunk_NN.png
+    Stage 3: User Review (MANDATORY pause) — user looks at panels + JSON, confirms chunk cuts
+    Stage 4: gpt-image-2 redraw panels (designer skill)
+    Stage 5: Seedance submit (Pro model) + poll until done  [→ seedance-runner/run_chunks.py]
+    Stage 6: Audio — Demucs BGM separation → stitch + mix BGM  [→ video-editor/concat.py]
+    Stage 7: Subtitles — ASR forced-align → word-level SRT → LLM seg + burn  [→ video-transcribe + video-burn-subtitle]
+
+Key rationale:
+    Gemini outputs scene.frame_time = start+0.5s per scene (avoids transition blur).
+    ffmpeg extracts frames at those exact timestamps. No PySceneDetect needed.
+    Panels are composed BEFORE user review so the user has actual images to look at.
+
+Two run modes:
+    --mode quick   : run continuously; pause only at Stage 4 (mandatory review)
+    --mode manual  : run ONE stage then exit; re-run with --from-stage N
+
+Resume: manifest.json in --out-dir tracks completed stages. Re-running skips done stages.
+
+Quick start:
+    python3 run_pipeline.py \\
+        --source path/to/video.mp4 \\
+        --elements elements.json \\
+        --out-dir runs/<id>/ \\
+        --mode quick
+
+elements.json schema:
+    [
+      {"name": "model",   "description": "post-procedure aesthetic face",
+       "image": "refs/portrait.jpg"},
+      {"name": "product", "description": "product bottle on marble", "image": "refs/product.jpg"}
+    ]
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import os
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import textwrap
+import time
+from pathlib import Path
+
+SCRIPTS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
+
+STAGE_NAMES = {
+    1: "gemini_analyze",
+    2: "compose_chunk_panels",   # extract frames + stitch panels
+    3: "review_scene_plan",      # mandatory review checkpoint
+    4: "redraw_chunk_panels",    # gpt-image-2 redraw via designer skill
+    5: "seedance_submit_poll",   # → shared: seedance-runner/run_chunks.py
+    6: "audio_postprocess",      # → shared: video-editor/concat.py (demucs + stitch)
+    7: "stitch_and_subtitles",   # → shared: video-transcribe + video-burn-subtitle
+}
+
+# Shared skill paths (relative to .agents/skills/)
+_SKILL_DIR      = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent  # → .agents/skills/
+_SR_RUN         = str(_SKILL_DIR / "seedance-runner"    / "scripts" / "run_chunks.py")
+_VE_CONCAT      = str(_SKILL_DIR / "video-editor"       / "scripts" / "concat.py")
+_VBS_TRANSCRIBE = str(_SKILL_DIR / "video-transcribe"   / "scripts" / "transcribe.sh")
+_VBS_BURN       = str(_SKILL_DIR / "video-burn-subtitle" / "scripts" / "burn.sh")
+
+REVIEW_STAGES = {3}
+
+
+def _run(cmd: list, **kw) -> None:
+    print(f"  $ {' '.join(str(c) for c in cmd)}", file=sys.stderr)
+    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, **kw)
+
+
+def _load_manifest(out_dir: Path) -> dict:
+    m = out_dir / "manifest.json"
+    return json.loads(m.read_text()) if m.is_file() else {"completed": [], "outputs": {}}
+
+
+def _save_manifest(out_dir: Path, manifest: dict) -> None:
+    (out_dir / "manifest.json").write_text(json.dumps(manifest, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+
+
+def _done(manifest: dict, stage: int) -> bool:
+    return STAGE_NAMES[stage] in manifest["completed"]
+
+
+def _mark_done(manifest: dict, stage: int, outputs: dict, out_dir: Path) -> None:
+    name = STAGE_NAMES[stage]
+    if name not in manifest["completed"]:
+        manifest["completed"].append(name)
+    manifest["outputs"][name] = outputs
+    _save_manifest(out_dir, manifest)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Stage runners
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def stage_1(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path, source: str,
+            model: str, max_chunk_duration: int) -> None:
+    """Gemini 3.1 Pro watches the video → scene_analysis.json.
+    Each scene includes frame_time = start+0.5s for clean frame extraction.
+    """
+    if _done(manifest, 1):
+        print("[1] Gemini video analyze — already done; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    print(f"[1] Gemini {model} — direct video understanding", file=sys.stderr)
+    cmd = ["python3", str(SCRIPTS_DIR / "scene_analyze_cli.py"),
+           "--video", source,
+           "--out-dir", str(out_dir),
+           "--model", model,
+           "--max-chunk-duration", str(max_chunk_duration)]
+    _run(cmd)
+    _mark_done(manifest, 1, {
+        "scene_analysis": str(out_dir / "scene_analysis.json"),
+    }, out_dir)
+
+
+def stage_2(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path, source: str) -> None:
+    """Extract one frame per scene at scene.frame_time using ffmpeg.
+    Then compose per-chunk panels. No PySceneDetect needed.
+    frame_time = scene.start + 0.5s (avoids transition blur).
+    """
+    if _done(manifest, 2):
+        print("[2] compose chunk panels — already done; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    print("[2] extract frames (ffmpeg) + compose per-chunk source panels", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    ana = json.loads(Path(manifest["outputs"]["gemini_analyze"]["scene_analysis"]).read_text())
+
+    # Probe video for frame size
+    probe_result = subprocess.check_output([
+        "ffprobe", "-v", "error", "-select_streams", "v:0",
+        "-show_entries", "stream=width,height",
+        "-of", "json", source
+    ])
+    probe = json.loads(probe_result)["streams"][0]
+    frame_size = f"{probe['width']}x{probe['height']}"
+
+    frames_dir = out_dir / "frames"
+    frames_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    panels_dir = out_dir / "panels" / "source"
+    panels_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    # Extract all scene frames in one pass (one ffmpeg call per scene)
+    frame_paths: dict[int, str] = {}   # scene_id → frame path
+    for chunk in ana["chunks"]:
+        for scene in chunk.get("scenes", []):
+            sid = scene["scene_id"]
+            ft  = float(scene.get("frame_time", scene["start"] + 0.5))
+            frame_out = frames_dir / f"scene_{sid:03d}.jpg"
+            _run([
+                "ffmpeg", "-y",
+                "-ss", str(ft),
+                "-i", source,
+                "-vframes", "1",
+                "-q:v", "2",
+                str(frame_out)
+            ])
+            frame_paths[sid] = str(frame_out)
+            print(f"  scene {sid}: frame_time={ft:.3f}s → {frame_out.name}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    # Compose one panel per chunk
+    panel_paths: dict[int, str] = {}
+    for chunk in ana["chunks"]:
+        cid    = chunk["chunk_id"]
+        scenes = chunk.get("scenes", [])
+        out_path = panels_dir / f"chunk_{cid:02d}.png"
+        cmd = ["python3", str(SCRIPTS_DIR / "panel_compose_cli.py"),
+               "--source-frame", frame_size, "--tier", "2k",
+               "--out", str(out_path)]
+        for scene in scenes:
+            sid = scene["scene_id"]
+            if sid in frame_paths:
+                cmd += ["--shot", f"{frame_paths[sid]}:{scene['start']}:{scene['end']}"]
+            else:
+                print(f"  WARNING: no frame for scene_id {sid}", file=sys.stderr)
+        _run(cmd)
+        panel_paths[cid] = str(out_path)
+        print(f"  chunk {cid}: {len(scenes)} scenes → {out_path}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    _mark_done(manifest, 2, {
+        "frame_paths": {str(k): v for k, v in frame_paths.items()},
+        "panels": panel_paths,
+    }, out_dir)
+
+
+def stage_3(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path) -> None:
+    """Mandatory review checkpoint — user sees panels + JSON, confirms chunk cuts + VO."""
+    if _done(manifest, 3):
+        print("[3] review checkpoint — already confirmed; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    ana_path = Path(manifest["outputs"]["gemini_analyze"]["scene_analysis"])
+    ana = json.loads(ana_path.read_text())
+    panels = manifest["outputs"]["compose_chunk_panels"]["panels"]
+
+    print(textwrap.dedent(f"""
+        ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ STAGE 3 REVIEW (mandatory) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
+        Storyboard panels (review these images!):
+    """), file=sys.stderr)
+    for cid, path in sorted(panels.items(), key=lambda x: int(x[0])):
+        print(f"  Chunk {cid}: {path}", file=sys.stderr)
+    print(textwrap.dedent(f"""
+        Gemini analysis: {ana_path}
+        video_type   : {ana.get("video_type", "?")}
+        video_purpose: {ana.get("video_purpose", "?")}
+        chunks       : {len(ana.get("chunks", []))}
+    """), file=sys.stderr)
+    for c in ana.get("chunks", []):
+        scenes = c.get("scenes", [])
+        vc = c.get("voiceover_combined", "")
+        print(
+            f"  chunk {c['chunk_id']}  {c['start']:.2f}–{c['end']:.2f}s  "
+            f"scenes={len(scenes)}  label={c.get('narrative_label','')}",
+            file=sys.stderr,
+        )
+        print(f"    VO: {vc[:100]}{'…' if len(vc)>100 else ''}", file=sys.stderr)
+    print(textwrap.dedent(f"""
+        Review panels + scene_analysis.json:
+          - Do the chunk panels look correctly grouped?
+          - Correct any VO mishearing (brand names, domain terms)
+          - If chunk cuts are wrong: edit scene_analysis.json + re-run from --from-stage 2
+            (frames will be re-extracted at new frame_time values, panels recomposed)
+          - Confirm video_type and video_purpose (used verbatim in Seedance prompt)
+        Then confirm to continue:
+          touch {out_dir}/.stage_3_confirmed
+        ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
+    """), file=sys.stderr)
+    if not (out_dir / ".stage_3_confirmed").is_file():
+        sys.exit(0)
+    _mark_done(manifest, 3, {"confirmed_at": time.time()}, out_dir)
+
+
+def stage_4(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path, elements: list[dict],
+            prompt_template: str) -> None:
+    if _done(manifest, 4):
+        print("[4] redraw panels — already done; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    print("[4] gpt-image-2 redraw each chunk panel (designer skill)", file=sys.stderr)
+    ana = json.loads(Path(manifest["outputs"]["gemini_analyze"]["scene_analysis"]).read_text())
+    panel_paths = {int(k): v for k, v in manifest["outputs"]["compose_chunk_panels"]["panels"].items()}
+    redrawn_dir = out_dir / "panels" / "redrawn"
+    redrawn_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    redrawn: dict[int, str] = {}
+    mygpt = (Path.home() / ".openclaw" / "skills" / "designer" / "scripts"
+             / "image_generation_cli.py")
+    size_map = {
+        1: "1080x1920", 2: "2048x1808", 3: "2048x1216", 4: "1152x2048",
+        5: "1728x2048", 6: "1728x2048", 7: "2048x1808", 8: "2048x1808",
+        9: "1152x2048", 10: "2048x1456", 11: "1536x2048", 12: "1536x2048",
+    }
+    for chunk in ana["chunks"]:
+        cid = chunk["chunk_id"]
+        scenes = chunk.get("scenes", [])
+        n = len(scenes)
+        scene_ids = [s["scene_id"] for s in scenes]
+        first, last = scene_ids[0], scene_ids[-1]
+        size = size_map.get(n, "2048x2048")
+        out_path = redrawn_dir / f"chunk_{cid:02d}.png"
+        elem_lines = "\n".join(
+            f"- IMAGE {i + 2}: {e['name']} — {e.get('description', '')}"
+            for i, e in enumerate(elements)
+        )
+        badges = " ".join(f"#{sid:02d}" for sid in scene_ids)
+        prompt = prompt_template.format(
+            n=n, first=first, last=last, badges=badges, size=size, elements=elem_lines,
+        )
+        all_images = [panel_paths[cid]] + [e["image"] for e in elements if e.get("image")]
+        images_arg = ",".join(all_images)
+        cmd = ["python3", str(mygpt),
+               "--model", "gpt-image-2",
+               "--quality", "medium",
+               "--images", images_arg,
+               "--prompt", prompt]
+        result = subprocess.check_output(cmd, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).decode().strip()
+        generated_path = result.split("\n")[-1].strip()
+        import shutil
+        shutil.copy(generated_path, str(out_path))
+        redrawn[cid] = str(out_path)
+        print(f"  chunk {cid} → {out_path}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+        # Write back redraw_prompt to scene_analysis.json so Stage 5 (Seedance)
+        # can merge it with visual_motion for text-image consistency.
+        for c in ana["chunks"]:
+            if c["chunk_id"] == cid:
+                for s in c.get("scenes", []):
+                    s["redraw_prompt"] = prompt
+                break
+
+    # Persist updated scene_analysis.json (with redraw_prompt fields added)
+    ana_path = Path(manifest["outputs"]["gemini_analyze"]["scene_analysis"])
+    ana_path.write_text(json.dumps(ana, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+    _mark_done(manifest, 4, {"redrawn_panels": redrawn}, out_dir)
+
+
+def stage_5(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path, ratio: str, resolution: str,
+            seedance_model: str) -> None:
+    """Upload redrawn panels to R2, build generation_plan.json, invoke seedance-runner."""
+    if _done(manifest, 5):
+        print("[5] Seedance — already done; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    print("[5] upload panels → seedance-runner/run_chunks.py", file=sys.stderr)
+    import urllib.request as _urlreq
+
+    redrawn = {int(k): v for k, v in
+               manifest["outputs"]["redraw_chunk_panels"]["redrawn_panels"].items()}
+    ana = json.loads(Path(manifest["outputs"]["gemini_analyze"]["scene_analysis"]).read_text())
+
+    # Upload each redrawn panel to R2 as Seedance reference
+    panel_urls: dict[int, str] = {}
+    proxy = os.environ["ECAP_PROXY_BASE_URL"].rstrip("/")
+    jwt   = os.environ["USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN"]
+    for cid, png_path in redrawn.items():
+        boundary = "----r2upload"
+        body = bytearray()
+        for fk, fv in [("prefix", "storyboard-studio"), ("file_type", "image")]:
+            part = "--" + boundary + "\r\n"
+            part += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"" + fk + "\"\r\n\r\n"
+            part += fv + "\r\n"
+            body += part.encode()
+        file_hdr = "--" + boundary + "\r\n"
+        file_hdr += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; "
+        file_hdr += "filename=\"chunk_" + f"{cid:02d}" + ".png\"\r\n"
+        file_hdr += "Content-Type: image/png\r\n\r\n"
+        body += file_hdr.encode()
+        body += Path(png_path).read_bytes()
+        body += ("\r\n--" + boundary + "--\r\n").encode()
+        req = _urlreq.Request(proxy + "/storage/r2/upload", data=bytes(body), headers={
+            "Authorization": "Bearer " + jwt,
+            "User-Agent": "storyboard-studio/1.0",
+            "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary,
+        })
+        with _urlreq.urlopen(req, timeout=180) as resp:
+            url = json.loads(resp.read())["url"]
+        panel_urls[cid] = url
+        print(f"  uploaded chunk {cid} \u2192 {url}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    # Build generation_plan.json for seedance-runner/run_chunks.py
+    chunks_plan = []
+    for chunk in ana["chunks"]:
+        cid   = chunk["chunk_id"]
+        lines = []
+        vtype = ana.get("video_type", "")
+        vpurp = ana.get("video_purpose", "")
+        if vtype:
+            lines.append(f"[CONTEXT] {vtype} — {vpurp}")
+        for scene in chunk.get("scenes", []):
+            lines.append(f"[VISUAL] {scene.get('visual_motion', '')}")
+        for vo in chunk.get("voiceover", []):
+            t = vo["start"] - chunk["start"]
+            lines.append(f"[VOICEOVER] {t:.1f}s: {vo['text']}")
+        lines.append("[AUDIO] No BGM, ambient only. Do NOT add music.")
+        lines.append(
+            "IMPORTANT — FIRST FRAME: first frame must NOT be the reference storyboard panel."
+        )
+        chunks_plan.append({
+            "index":           cid - 1,
+            "prompt":          "\n".join(lines),
+            "duration":        min(float(chunk["end"]) - float(chunk["start"]), 15.0),
+            "reference_asset": panel_urls[cid],
+        })
+    plan = {
+        "version":      "1",
+        "source_video": manifest.get("source", ""),
+        "chunks":       chunks_plan,
+        "captions":     [],
+        "seedance":     {"model": seedance_model, "ratio": ratio,
+                         "resolution": resolution, "max_workers": 3},
+    }
+    plan_path = out_dir / "generation_plan.json"
+    plan_path.write_text(json.dumps(plan, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+
+    # Invoke shared seedance-runner
+    result_raw = subprocess.check_output(
+        ["python3", _SR_RUN, "--plan", str(plan_path), "--out-dir", str(out_dir)],
+        stderr=sys.stderr,
+    )
+    mp4_paths    = json.loads(result_raw)["mp4_paths"]
+    chunk_videos = {str(i + 1): p for i, p in enumerate(mp4_paths)}
+    _mark_done(manifest, 5, {
+        "panel_urls":       {str(k): v for k, v in panel_urls.items()},
+        "chunk_videos":     chunk_videos,
+        "plan_json":        str(plan_path),
+        "seedance_results": str(out_dir / "seedance_results.json"),
+    }, out_dir)
+
+
+
+def stage_6(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path, source: str,
+            target_lufs: float, bgm_db: float, crossfade_ms: int) -> None:
+    """Demucs BGM separation + stitch via shared video-editor/concat.py."""
+    if _done(manifest, 6):
+        print("[6] audio postprocess — already done; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    print("[6] video-editor/concat.py — Demucs BGM + stitch", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    cv           = manifest["outputs"]["seedance_submit_poll"]["chunk_videos"]
+    mp4_list     = [cv[k] for k in sorted(cv, key=lambda x: int(x))]
+    chunks_json  = out_dir / "seedance_results.json"
+    if not chunks_json.exists():
+        chunks_json.write_text(json.dumps({"mp4_paths": mp4_list}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+
+    final = out_dir / "final.mp4"
+    _run(["python3", _VE_CONCAT,
+          "--source",       source,
+          "--chunks-json",  str(chunks_json),
+          "--out",          str(final),
+          "--bgm-db",       str(bgm_db),
+          "--target-lufs",  str(target_lufs),
+          "--crossfade-ms", str(crossfade_ms)])
+    _mark_done(manifest, 6, {"final_mp4": str(final)}, out_dir)
+
+
+
+def stage_7(manifest: dict, out_dir: Path) -> None:
+    """ASR forced-align → word-level SRT → LLM segmentation + ASS burn."""
+    if _done(manifest, 7):
+        print("[7] subtitles — already done; skipping", file=sys.stderr); return
+    print("[7] video-transcribe → video-burn-subtitle — word-level SRT + burn", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    final_in = Path(manifest["outputs"]["audio_postprocess"]["final_mp4"])
+
+    words_srt = out_dir / "words.srt"
+    final_sub = out_dir / "final_with_subtitles.mp4"
+
+    # Stage 7a: forced-align → word-level SRT (--vocals-only strips BGM before ASR)
+    _run(["bash", _VBS_TRANSCRIBE,
+          "-i", str(final_in),
+          "-o", str(words_srt),
+          "--vocals-only"])
+
+    # Stage 7b: LLM segmentation + ASS burn
+    _run(["bash", _VBS_BURN,
+          "-i", str(final_in),
+          "-s", str(words_srt),
+          "-o", str(final_sub),
+          "--orientation", "portrait"])
+
+    _mark_done(manifest, 7, {
+        "final_with_subtitles": str(final_sub),
+        "words_srt":            str(words_srt),
+    }, out_dir)
+
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Main
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# NotoSansSC-Bold loaded via fontsdir in Stage 7 ffmpeg subtitles filter (ASS file, MarginV=256)
+
+DEFAULT_REDRAW_PROMPT = textwrap.dedent("""\
+    You are redrawing a multi-panel storyboard image for a marketing video.
+
+    The FIRST image is a grid containing {n} SEQUENTIAL cinematic shots labeled {badges} \
+(left-to-right, top row first then bottom row).
+
+    Use the additional images as identity/product references:
+    {elements}
+
+    REDRAW EACH OCCUPIED PANEL INDEPENDENTLY:
+    - Keep the grid layout exactly; do not change panel count or positions.
+    - In each panel, replace the listed elements per their reference images.
+    - KEEP FAITHFUL to each source panel: clothing, hair, accessories, pose, gesture, \
+camera angle, framing, background, lighting, color grade.
+    - Panels are INDEPENDENT — no content bleed across boundaries.
+
+    PRESERVE:
+    - {badges} badge labels in top-left of each occupied panel MUST stay visible.
+    - Bottom black timestamp strip on each occupied panel MUST stay visible.
+
+    REMOVE all original subtitles, captions, watermarks, on-screen text.
+    Do not add any new text.
+
+    Output: a single {size} image with the same grid layout. Photoreal, magazine-grade.""")
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="storyboard-studio pipeline orchestrator")
+    ap.add_argument("--source", help="Source video path (required for first run)")
+    ap.add_argument("--elements", help="elements.json with replacement element specs")
+    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", required=True, help="Run directory (manifest + outputs)")
+    ap.add_argument("--mode", choices=["quick", "manual"], default="quick")
+    ap.add_argument("--from-stage", type=int, default=1)
+    ap.add_argument("--to-stage", type=int, default=7)
+    # Stage 1
+    ap.add_argument("--gemini-model", default="gemini-3.1-pro-preview")
+    ap.add_argument("--max-chunk-duration", type=int, default=14)
+    # Stage 4
+    ap.add_argument("--redraw-prompt-template", default=None,
+                    help="Path to a custom redraw prompt template file.")
+    # Stage 5
+    ap.add_argument("--ratio", default="9:16")
+    ap.add_argument("--resolution", default="720p", choices=["480p", "720p", "1080p"])
+    ap.add_argument("--seedance-model", default="dreamina-seedance-2-0-260128")
+    # Stage 6
+    ap.add_argument("--target-lufs", type=float, default=-14.0)
+    ap.add_argument("--bgm-db", type=float, default=-8.0)
+    ap.add_argument("--crossfade-ms", type=int, default=200)
+    # Stage 7
+    # --subtitle-font removed: Stage 7 now uses ffmpeg subtitles filter (ASS file), font via fontsdir
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir).expanduser().resolve()
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    manifest = _load_manifest(out_dir)
+    if args.source:
+        manifest["source"] = args.source
+    if args.elements:
+        manifest["elements_file"] = args.elements
+    _save_manifest(out_dir, manifest)
+
+    source = manifest.get("source") or sys.exit("--source required on first run")
+    elements = (json.loads(Path(manifest["elements_file"]).read_text())
+                if manifest.get("elements_file") else [])
+    prompt_template = (Path(args.redraw_prompt_template).read_text()
+                       if args.redraw_prompt_template else DEFAULT_REDRAW_PROMPT)
+
+    runners = {
+        1: lambda: stage_1(manifest, out_dir, source, args.gemini_model, args.max_chunk_duration),
+        2: lambda: stage_2(manifest, out_dir, source),   # ffmpeg frame extract + panel compose
+        3: lambda: stage_3(manifest, out_dir),            # user reviews panels + JSON
+        4: lambda: stage_4(manifest, out_dir, elements, prompt_template),
+        5: lambda: stage_5(manifest, out_dir, args.ratio, args.resolution, args.seedance_model),
+        6: lambda: stage_6(manifest, out_dir, source, args.target_lufs, args.bgm_db, args.crossfade_ms),
+        7: lambda: stage_7(manifest, out_dir),
+    }
+
+    print(f"== storyboard-studio ==  mode={args.mode}  stages={args.from_stage}→{args.to_stage}  out={out_dir}",
+          file=sys.stderr)
+
+    for stage_num in range(args.from_stage, args.to_stage + 1):
+        runners[stage_num]()
+        if args.mode == "manual":
+            nxt = stage_num + 1
+            print(f"[manual] stage {stage_num} done. Re-run with --from-stage {nxt}.", file=sys.stderr)
+            return
+
+    final = (manifest.get("outputs", {}).get("stitch_and_subtitles", {}).get("final_with_subtitles")
+             or manifest.get("outputs", {}).get("audio_postprocess", {}).get("final_mp4", "(not yet)"))
+    print(f"\n✓ Pipeline complete. Final: {final}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Stage 2 — Gemini 3.1 Pro directly watches the source video and outputs
+a complete scene analysis JSON: chunk plan, per-scene descriptions
+(static + motion), VO with punctuation and timestamps, voice tone,
+and video type/purpose.
+
+Inputs:  source video file (mp4/mov/etc.)
+Outputs: <out>/scene_analysis.json
+
+Replaces the old ASR → forced-align → storyboard-sheet approach with a
+single Gemini video-understanding call. No ASR, no forced-align required.
+
+Usage:
+    python3 scene_analyze_cli.py \\
+        --video tmp/run-01/source.mp4 \\
+        --out-dir tmp/run-01 \\
+        [--model gemini-3.1-pro-preview] \\
+        [--max-chunk-duration 14]
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import base64
+import json
+import os
+import sys
+import urllib.request
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Gemini call
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def call_gemini_video(video_path: Path, prompt: str, model: str,
+                      max_tokens: int = 8192) -> dict:
+    """Send video bytes + prompt to Gemini via LiteLLM proxy.
+    Video is base64-encoded and sent as image_url with data: URI.
+    Gemini 1.5 / 2.x / 3.x accept video/mp4 this way through LiteLLM.
+    """
+    base = os.environ["LITELLM_API_BASE"].rstrip("/")
+    key  = os.environ["LITELLM_API_KEY"]
+
+    video_bytes = video_path.read_bytes()
+    video_b64   = base64.b64encode(video_bytes).decode()
+    print(f"  video size: {len(video_bytes)/1024:.0f} KB, model: {model}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    payload = {
+        "model": model,
+        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
+        "temperature": 0.2,
+        "messages": [
+            {
+                "role": "user",
+                "content": [
+                    {"type": "text", "text": prompt},
+                    {
+                        "type": "image_url",
+                        "image_url": {"url": f"data:video/mp4;base64,{video_b64}"}
+                    }
+                ]
+            }
+        ]
+    }
+
+    req = urllib.request.Request(
+        f"{base}/v1/chat/completions",
+        data=json.dumps(payload).encode(),
+        headers={
+            "Authorization": f"Bearer {key}",
+            "Content-Type": "application/json",
+            "User-Agent": "storyboard-studio/1.0",
+        }
+    )
+    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=300) as r:
+        return json.loads(r.read())
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Prompt
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+GEMINI_PROMPT = """\
+You are a professional video analyst and storyboard director. Watch this \
+marketing video carefully — audio, visuals, and motion — then output a \
+single JSON object describing the full video for downstream AI video \
+re-generation. Output ONLY raw JSON starting with { and ending with }. \
+No markdown, no backticks, no explanation.
+
+SCHEMA:
+{
+  "video_type": "Chinese label, e.g. 医美广告·效果展示",
+  "video_purpose": "Chinese 1-2 sentences: what the video achieves and who it targets",
+  "voice_tone": {
+    "gender": "female | male",
+    "age_range": "e.g. mid-20s to early-30s",
+    "language": "e.g. Mandarin Chinese",
+    "tone": "e.g. confident, professional, persuasive, warm",
+    "pace": "slow | medium | fast",
+    "characteristic": "e.g. clear, articulate, energetic"
+  },
+  "chunks": [
+    {
+      "chunk_id": 1,
+      "start": 0.0,
+      "end": 13.2,
+      "narrative_label": "hook | setup | pain-points | claim | proof | cta | ...",
+      "voiceover": [
+        {"start": 0.0, "end": 3.0, "text": "台词句子，带自然中文标点，"},
+        {"start": 3.6, "end": 5.1, "text": "下一句台词，"}
+      ],
+      "scenes": [
+        {
+          "scene_id": 1,
+          "start": 0.0,
+          "end": 2.1,
+          "frame_time": 0.5,
+          "shot_type": "close-up | medium | wide | beauty-shot | over-shoulder | product-shot | landscape | ...",
+          "camera_motion": "static | dolly-in | pan-left | pan-right | tilt-up | tilt-down | handheld | zoom-in | zoom-out | ...",
+          "subject": "Describe the primary subject(s) of the shot generically and specifically — could be a person, product, object, animal, scenery, text card, graphic, etc. Include appearance details relevant for redrawing.",
+          "visual_static": "English. Static composition description for reference-frame redrawing: framing, subject position, background, lighting, color palette, facial expression if person.",
+          "visual_motion": "English. Dynamic action description for video model: what moves, how it moves, sequence of actions within the shot, camera movement, any transitions. Use temporal verbs (raises, tilts, slides, fades, etc.)."
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  ]
+}
+
+RULES:
+1. Chunks: max {MAX_CHUNK_DURATION}s each. Split ONLY at natural sentence/breath \
+boundaries in the VO (never mid-sentence). Prefer narrative arc transitions \
+(hook→setup→claim→cta). Aim for 2-4 chunks for a 30-40s video.
+2. voiceover: Write EXACTLY what you hear, including every word. \
+Use natural Chinese punctuation (，。！？…). Split into one entry per \
+breath-pause / sentence boundary. Each entry must have start/end times \
+accurate to ±0.5s.
+3. scenes — CRITICAL: One scene = one continuous camera shot. \
+Capture EVERY hard cut and scene transition, no matter how brief. \
+Do NOT merge two shots into one scene even if subjects are similar. \
+A missed shot boundary means a missing storyboard panel — err on the side \
+of more scenes rather than fewer. Split at every visible cut, wipe, or \
+dissolved transition.
+4. frame_time: For each scene, set frame_time = start + 0.5 (i.e. 0.5 seconds \
+after the scene starts). This avoids capturing transition blur. \
+Exception: if the scene is shorter than 1.0s, set frame_time = start + (end - start) / 2.
+5. subject: Be generic and specific — "young East-Asian woman, black top, \
+shoulder-length hair" or "glass serum bottle on marble surface" or \
+"animated diagram of facial bone structure" etc.
+6. Output ONLY the JSON object. No other text.
+"""
+
+
+def build_prompt(max_chunk_duration: int = 14) -> str:
+    return GEMINI_PROMPT.replace("{MAX_CHUNK_DURATION}", str(max_chunk_duration))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Post-processing helpers
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def flatten_voiceover_combined(chunk: dict) -> str:
+    """Concatenate voiceover entries into a single string for convenience."""
+    return "".join(v["text"] for v in chunk.get("voiceover", []))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Main
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
+        description="Stage 2 — Gemini video understanding → scene_analysis.json"
+    )
+    ap.add_argument("--video", required=True, help="Source video file (mp4/mov)")
+    ap.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
+                    help="LiteLLM model name (default: gemini-3.1-pro-preview)")
+    ap.add_argument("--max-chunk-duration", type=int, default=14,
+                    help="Max seconds per chunk (default: 14)")
+    ap.add_argument("--max-tokens", type=int, default=8192)
+    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", "-o", required=True)
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    video_path = Path(args.video).expanduser().resolve()
+    if not video_path.is_file():
+        sys.exit(f"video not found: {video_path}")
+
+    out_dir = Path(args.out_dir).expanduser().resolve()
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    prompt = build_prompt(args.max_chunk_duration)
+    print(
+        f"[Stage 2] Gemini video analysis\n"
+        f"  video:  {video_path}\n"
+        f"  model:  {args.model}\n"
+        f"  prompt: {len(prompt)} chars",
+        file=sys.stderr
+    )
+
+    resp = call_gemini_video(video_path, prompt, args.model, args.max_tokens)
+
+    usage = resp.get("usage", {})
+    finish = resp["choices"][0].get("finish_reason", "?")
+    print(
+        f"  finish_reason={finish}  "
+        f"total_tokens={usage.get('total_tokens', '?')}",
+        file=sys.stderr
+    )
+    if finish != "stop":
+        print(f"  WARNING: finish_reason={finish} — response may be truncated. "
+              f"Try --max-tokens {args.max_tokens * 2}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    content = resp["choices"][0]["message"]["content"].strip()
+    # Strip accidental markdown fences
+    if content.startswith("```"):
+        content = content.split("```", 2)[1]
+        if content.startswith("json"):
+            content = content[4:]
+        content = content.strip()
+    # Strip trailing fence
+    if content.endswith("```"):
+        content = content[:-3].rstrip()
+
+    parsed = json.loads(content)
+
+    # Add convenience voiceover_combined field to each chunk
+    # Also ensure every scene has a valid frame_time (Gemini may forget)
+    for chunk in parsed.get("chunks", []):
+        chunk["voiceover_combined"] = flatten_voiceover_combined(chunk)
+        for scene in chunk.get("scenes", []):
+            s_start = float(scene.get("start", 0))
+            s_end   = float(scene.get("end", s_start + 1))
+            duration = s_end - s_start
+            if "frame_time" not in scene or scene["frame_time"] is None:
+                if duration >= 1.0:
+                    scene["frame_time"] = round(s_start + 0.5, 3)
+                else:
+                    scene["frame_time"] = round(s_start + duration / 2, 3)
+            else:
+                # Clamp to valid range
+                ft = float(scene["frame_time"])
+                ft = max(s_start, min(ft, s_end - 0.033))  # at least 1 frame before end
+                scene["frame_time"] = round(ft, 3)
+
+    out_path = out_dir / "scene_analysis.json"
+    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(parsed, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+
+    # Summary for stdout
+    summary = {
+        "scene_analysis_json": str(out_path),
+        "video_type": parsed.get("video_type", "(not detected)"),
+        "video_purpose": parsed.get("video_purpose", "(not detected)"),
+        "chunks": len(parsed.get("chunks", [])),
+        "total_scenes": sum(
+            len(c.get("scenes", [])) for c in parsed.get("chunks", [])
+        ),
+        "voice_tone": parsed.get("voice_tone", {}),
+        "chunk_summary": [
+            {
+                "id": c["chunk_id"],
+                "start": c["start"],
+                "end": c["end"],
+                "duration": round(c["end"] - c["start"], 2),
+                "label": c.get("narrative_label", ""),
+                "scenes": len(c.get("scenes", [])),
+                "voiceover_preview": c["voiceover_combined"][:60] + "…"
+                    if len(c["voiceover_combined"]) > 60 else c["voiceover_combined"],
+            }
+            for c in parsed.get("chunks", [])
+        ],
+    }
+    print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+
+    # User review notice
+    print(
+        "\n[Stage 2 → User Review Required]\n"
+        f'  video_type   = {parsed.get("video_type", "(not detected)")}\n'
+        f'  video_purpose= {parsed.get("video_purpose", "(not detected)")}\n'
+        "\nPlease review the voiceover text and timestamps in scene_analysis.json.\n"
+        "Correct any ASR errors, then confirm before proceeding to Stage 3.\n"
+        "Edit scene_analysis.json directly — video_type, video_purpose, and\n"
+        "voiceover entries will be used verbatim in downstream stages.",
+        file=sys.stderr
+    )
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

@@ -0,0 +1,326 @@
+---
+name: style-duplicate
+description: >
+  Style Duplicate — analyze a reference short-form video and generate a new version
+  by substituting subject, product, person, scene, or voiceover. Powered by
+  Gemini 3.1 Pro (deep scene analysis), claude-sonnet-4-6 (Prompt composition), gpt-image-2
+  (Storyboard reference image), and Seedance 2.0 (video generation).
+  Supports TikTok / Reels / Instagram / 小红书 / 口播带货 vertical video formats.
+metadata:
+  openclaw:
+    requires:
+      bins: [ffmpeg, ffprobe, uv]
+      env:
+        - ECAP_PROXY_BASE_URL
+        - USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN
+        - LITELLM_API_BASE
+        - LITELLM_API_KEY
+        - OPENAI_BASE_URL
+        - OPENAI_API_KEY
+---
+
+# Style Duplicate Skill
+
+## Overview
+
+Style Duplicate takes a reference marketing video and generates a new version
+by substituting any combination of: subject/product, brand/logo, person,
+scene, or voiceover. The output preserves the original video's pacing,
+camera style, and edit rhythm while replacing the specified elements.
+
+Unlike **storyboard-studio** (exact frame-by-frame replication), style-duplicate
+is **content-driven**: the user defines what changes, and the pipeline rebuilds
+each scene accordingly.
+
+---
+
+## 7-Stage Pipeline
+
+| # | Stage | Script | Description |
+|---|-------|--------|-------------|
+| 0 | 预处理输入视频 | `prepare-source/prepare.sh` | 本地文件或 URL 报取，压缩到 ≤10MB |
+| 1 | Gemini Analysis | `style_analyze_cli.py` | Deep 6-field scene analysis |
+| 2 | Substitution Collection | *(dialog)* | User defines what to replace |
+| 3 | Prompt Composition | `prompt_composer_cli.py` | claude-sonnet-4-6 builds per-chunk Seedance prompts |
+| 4 | Storyboard (optional) | `storyboard_gen_cli.py` | gpt-image-2 consistency reference sheet |
+| 5 | Seedance Generation | `run_pipeline.py` (concurrent) | Up to 10 chunks in parallel |
+| 6 | Audio Postprocess | *storyboard-studio/audio_postprocess_cli.py* | Demucs BGM separation + stitch |
+| 7 | Subtitles | `video-transcribe` + `video-burn-subtitle` | word-level forced-align + LLM 分段 |
+
+Stages 6–7 使用 `video-editor` + 字幕三件套公共层。
+
+---
+
+## Usage Flow
+
+### User initiates with a video
+
+When the user sends a video file or URL and asks to duplicate/remake it:
+
+1. **Acknowledge receipt** — tell the user you're analyzing the video with Gemini.
+2. **Step 0: 预处理输入**（本地文件或任意 URL）：
+   ```bash
+   SOURCE=$(bash .agents/skills/prepare-source/scripts/prepare.sh \
+     -i "<用户输入的路径或URL>" \
+     -o runs/<run_id>/source.mp4)
+   ```
+3. **Run Stages 1–2** via pipeline:
+   ```bash
+   python3 scripts/run_pipeline.py \
+     --source  "$SOURCE" \
+     --out-dir runs/<run_id>/ \
+     --from-stage 1 --only-stage 1
+   ```
+4. **Ask Step 2 questions** (see below), write `substitutions.json`, then proceed.
+
+---
+
+### Step 2 — Substitution Questions
+
+After Stage 1 completes, present ONLY this question block to the user:
+
+> 我已理解参考视频结构。请确认是否需要替换以下内容：
+>
+> 1. **主体/商品**：是否保留原商品，还是替换成新商品？
+> 2. **品牌/Logo**：是否保留原品牌，还是替换成目标品牌？
+> 3. **人物**：是否保留原人物类型，还是替换性别、年龄、职业、风格？
+> 4. **场景**：是否保留原场景，还是替换成新场景？
+> 5. **口播**：是否保留原口播结构，还是改成新的口播内容？
+> 6. **其他要求**：是否有指定风格、比例、时长、平台或禁忌？
+
+Write user responses to `runs/<run_id>/substitutions.json`:
+```json
+{
+  "subject":   "Replace with XX serum",
+  "logo":      "",
+  "person":    "Replace with 25yo Asian woman, white blazer, minimal makeup",
+  "scene":     "",
+  "voiceover": "",
+  "extra":     "Little Red Book style"
+}
+```
+Leave any field empty (`""`) if the user says "keep original" or skips it.
+
+If the user says "不替换 / 直接复刻 / 按原视频来" → write all empty strings and proceed.
+
+---
+
+### Step 3 — Prompt Composition + User Review
+
+Run Stage 3 and **show the output Markdown to the user** for review:
+```bash
+python3 scripts/run_pipeline.py \
+  --source  <video_path> \
+  --out-dir runs/<run_id>/ \
+  --subs    runs/<run_id>/substitutions.json \
+  --only-stage 3
+```
+Then display `runs/<run_id>/chunk_prompts.md` to the user.
+
+Ask:
+> 以上是根据您的替换要求生成的分 Chunk Prompt。是否需要调整任何内容？
+> 如果满意，请告诉我是否需要生成"主体一致性 Storyboard 参考图"。
+
+---
+
+### Step 4 — Storyboard (Optional)
+
+Ask the user:
+
+> 是否需要我继续生成一张"主体一致性 Storyboard 参考图"？
+>
+> 这张图不是具体分镜复刻图，而是控制多段 Chunk 之间核心主体一致性的视觉参考，包括：
+> 1. 人物一致性（年龄感、服装、妆容、气质）
+> 2. 商品一致性（外观、颜色、材质、包装）
+> 3. 场景一致性（空间风格、光线、背景）
+> 4. 镜头距离参考
+> 5. 色彩与质感参考
+
+If user says yes:
+```bash
+python3 scripts/run_pipeline.py \
+  --source  <video_path> \
+  --out-dir runs/<run_id>/ \
+  --only-stage 4
+```
+Show the generated image `runs/<run_id>/storyboard_ref.png` to the user and confirm before proceeding.
+
+If user says no → pass `--skip-storyboard` in the next command.
+
+---
+
+### Step 5–7 — Video Generation
+
+Run the full pipeline from Stage 5 onward:
+```bash
+python3 scripts/run_pipeline.py \
+  --source    <video_path> \
+  --out-dir   runs/<run_id>/ \
+  --from-stage 5 \
+  --resolution 720p \
+  [--skip-storyboard]
+```
+
+The pipeline:
+- Submits all chunks concurrently (max 10 workers)
+- Polls until all Seedance tasks complete
+- Separates BGM via Demucs, stitches chunks
+- Burns subtitles via `video-burn-subtitle`：方正金陵 Bold 64px, LLM 语义分段, 词级 forced-align 时间轴
+
+
+> ⚠️ **字幕阶段强制约束 — 必须使用 pack 内置 skill，禁止自行实现**
+>
+> Seedance 所有 chunk 下载完成后，进入字幕流程时：
+> - **时间轴对齐**：必须调用 `video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh`（内部使用 `zooclaw-forcedalign` 生成 word-level SRT）
+> - **字幕分段+烧录**：必须调用 `video-burn-subtitle/scripts/burn.sh`（内部使用 `llm_segment.py` + `srt_to_ass.py`）
+> - **禁止**：自写 ASS/SRT 生成逻辑、手动调 ffmpeg subtitles filter、使用 `subtitle_burn.py`（已废弃）
+>
+> 完整命令见本文件 "Subtitle Burn" 节。
+
+### Step 5.5 — 字幕擦除（on-demand，共享 A/B/C）
+
+Seedance 有时会将中文字幕烧入画面。触发条件：用户明确反馈（字幕乱码 / 有乱字幕 / erase subtitles）。
+
+```bash
+python3 .agents/skills/seedance-runner/scripts/erase_subtitles.py \
+  --video-url "<chunk ARK URL from seedance_results.json>"
+```
+
+返回的 `result.video_url` 下载后替换对应 `chunks/chunk_NN.mp4`，再继续合并。
+
+> ⚠️ **必须使用原始 ARK URL**：字幕擦除 API 只接受 Seedance 返回的原始 `video_url`（`ark://` 或 `https://ark-cn-beijing...`），不接受本地下载文件或重上传。ARK URL 24h TTL，擦除必须在生成后当天内完成。
+> 字幕擦除后输出的 chunk 码率可能是其他 chunk 的3−4倍，`concat.py` 会自动检测并重编码归一，无需手动处理。
+
+---
+
+## Schema Reference
+
+### style_analysis.json (Stage 1 output)
+
+```json
+{
+  "meta": {
+    "ratio": "9:16",
+    "duration_s": 35.1,
+    "language": "Mandarin Chinese",
+    "video_type": "美妆种草·产品测评",
+    "video_purpose": "..."
+  },
+  "voice_tone": { "gender": "female", "age_range": "mid-20s", ... },
+  "style_tags": {
+    "color_palette": "warm white, cream",
+    "lighting": "soft frontal fill",
+    "visual_feel": "medical-aesthetic, lifestyle",
+    "camera_habits": "heavy close-up, handheld micro-shake",
+    "edit_rhythm": "fast-paced jump cuts every 1-2s"
+  },
+  "chunks": [
+    {
+      "chunk_id": 1, "start": 0.0, "end": 12.5,
+      "narrative_label": "hook",
+      "voiceover": [{"start": 0.0, "end": 3.1, "text": "台词…"}],
+      "scenes": [
+        {
+          "scene_id": 1, "start": 0.0, "end": 2.8, "frame_time": 0.5,
+          "subject":     {"description": "...", "position": "..."},
+          "action":      {"person": "...", "product": "...", "interaction": "..."},
+          "environment": {"space": "...", "background": "...", "lighting_detail": "..."},
+          "camera":      {"shot_type": "close-up", "motion": "static", ...},
+          "style":       {"color": "...", "commercial_feel": "...", ...},
+          "audio":       {"voiceover_text": "...", "voice_style": "...", "ambient": "..."}
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### substitutions.json (Stage 2 output)
+
+```json
+{
+  "subject":   "string or empty",
+  "logo":      "string or empty",
+  "person":    "string or empty",
+  "scene":     "string or empty",
+  "voiceover": "string or empty",
+  "extra":     "string or empty"
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## Video Ratio Detection
+
+Video ratio is auto-detected from the source file via `ffprobe` in Stage 1.
+Supported Seedance 2.0 ratios: `21:9`, `16:9`, `4:3`, `1:1`, `3:4`, `9:16`.
+The detected ratio is stored in `style_analysis.json["meta"]["ratio"]` and
+propagated to all Seedance submissions automatically.
+
+---
+
+> 🎙️ **音频纯净度要求**：`video-transcribe` 必须使用无 BGM 的干净音频。
+> - **首选**：在 `audio_postprocess`（BGM 混入）**之前**调用，传入 `stitched.mp4`（Seedance 原始合并，只有 VO + 环境音）
+> - **备选**：已混入 BGM 时，传入 `stitched_bgm.mp4` 并加 `--vocals-only` flag（Demucs 先分离人声再 ASR）
+> - ❌ 禁止：直接把 BGM 混音后的视频送 ASR（BGM 会严重干扰识别精度）
+
+## Subtitle Specification (v2, shared A/B/C)
+
+字幕烧录由三个共享 skill 完成，不再使用旧 ASS 手写逻辑。
+
+```bash
+export ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL=https://forced-align.g.yesy.online
+
+# 1. ASR 转录
+bash {skills}/zooclaw-asr/scripts/transcribe.sh \
+    "$FINAL_BGM" -o transcript.txt -f text
+
+# 2. Forced-align → word-level SRT（80ms 精度）
+bash {skills}/video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh \
+    -i "$FINAL_BGM" -o words.srt
+
+# 3. LLM 分段 + ASS burn
+bash {skills}/video-burn-subtitle/scripts/burn.sh \
+    -i "$FINAL_BGM" -s words.srt \
+    --orientation portrait \
+    -o final_with_subtitles.mp4
+```
+
+| 参数 | 值 |
+|---|---|
+| 字体 | 方正金陵 Bold (fzjingling.ttf，内置于 video-burn-subtitle/assets/) |
+| 字号 | 64px (portrait) / 38px (landscape) |
+| 每行最大 | 8 CJK 字符 |
+| 位置 | `pos(vw/2, vh×2/3)` |
+| 样式 | 白字无描边，黑色阴影 70% 透明度 -45° |
+| 时间精度 | 80ms 词级 forced-align |
+| 分段方式 | LLM 语义切割，语气词随前句，静默≥800ms 强制断句 |
+
+详细配置见 `video-editor` skill — "Subtitle Burn" 节。
+
+---
+
+## Seedance 2.0 Key Rules
+
+- Model: `dreamina-seedance-2-0-260128` (Pro; Fast reserved for drafts only)
+- Duration: 4–15s per chunk
+- Image content items **must** include `"role": "reference_image"`
+- Storyboard panel is passed as `Image 1` (registered via `CreateAsset → asset://`)
+- Wait 20s after `CreateAsset` before submitting
+- No `role` field on text content items
+
+---
+
+## Known Pitfalls
+
+1. **Gemini JSON truncation**: `--max-tokens 10000` is required; lower values truncate
+2. **Seedance face privacy rejection**: auto-handled via `CreateAsset → asset://` bypass
+3. **Concurrent Seedance limit**: `max_workers=10`; reduce if rate-limited (HTTP 429)
+4. **Stage 4 failure is non-fatal**: pipeline continues without storyboard ref image
+5. **Ratio from source**: always use ffprobe ratio, never ask user to specify manually
+6. **Demucs first run**: ~500MB model download cached at `~/.cache/torch/hub/`
+7. **CJK subtitle rendering**: must use ASS file; `force_style` on SRT corrupts Chinese
+8. **Stage 3 max_tokens**: default is 8192; for very long videos (>5 chunks) increase to 12000
+9. **VO substitution subtitles**: when voiceover substitution is active, Stage 7 reads subtitles from `chunk_prompts.json` (substituted text), not `style_analysis.json`; timing is scene-based approximation
+10. **MarginV is dynamic**: calculated as `video_height × 0.30`; old value was `× 0.20` (too low); hardcoded 256 was only correct for 1280px-tall videos at the old ratio
+11. **Pipeline stage order**: always run stages in order 1→2→3→4→5→6→7; use `--only-stage N` for single-stage runs, `--from-stage N` for resume only when prior stages are already done

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Stage 3 — Take style_analysis.json + substitutions.json and call claude-sonnet-4-6
+to produce per-Chunk Seedance prompts in the standard 6-field format.
+
+Inputs:
+    style_analysis.json   (Stage 1 output)
+    substitutions.json    (Stage 2 — user's replacement decisions)
+
+Outputs:
+    <out>/chunk_prompts.json    machine-readable list of per-chunk prompts
+    <out>/chunk_prompts.md      human-readable Markdown for user review
+
+substitutions.json schema:
+{
+  "subject":   "Replace original product with XX serum",   // or "" to keep
+  "logo":      "Replace with BrandY logo",                 // or "" to keep
+  "person":    "Replace with 25yo Asian woman, minimal makeup, white blazer",
+  "scene":     "",                                         // keep original
+  "voiceover": "Replace with: <new script text>",          // or "" to keep
+  "extra":     "Little Red Book style, no explicit claims"
+}
+Any field left empty means "keep original".
+
+Usage:
+    python3 prompt_composer_cli.py \\
+        --analysis  runs/run-01/style_analysis.json \\
+        --subs      runs/run-01/substitutions.json \\
+        --out-dir   runs/run-01/
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import os
+import sys
+import urllib.request
+import re
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# LLM call (claude-sonnet-4-6 via LiteLLM)
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def call_llm(messages: list[dict], model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6",
+             max_tokens: int = 8192, temperature: float = 0.3) -> str:
+    base = os.environ["LITELLM_API_BASE"].rstrip("/")
+    key  = os.environ["LITELLM_API_KEY"]
+
+    payload = {
+        "model": model,
+        "messages": messages,
+        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
+        "temperature": temperature,
+    }
+    req = urllib.request.Request(
+        f"{base}/v1/chat/completions",
+        data=json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8"),
+        headers={
+            "Authorization": f"Bearer {key}",
+            "Content-Type": "application/json",
+            "User-Agent": "style-duplicate/1.0",
+        },
+    )
+    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as r:
+        resp = json.loads(r.read())
+    return resp["choices"][0]["message"]["content"].strip()
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Prompt builder
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
+You are a Seedance 2.0 video generation prompt architect specialising in style-transfer
+with element substitution.
+
+Your task: given a structured video scene analysis (Stage 1 Gemini output) and the
+user's substitution instructions, produce per-chunk Seedance-ready prompts that:
+  1. MAXIMALLY PRESERVE the original video's visual DNA — its shot language, edit
+     rhythm, action choreography, lighting mood, color palette, and commercial feel.
+  2. SURGICALLY REPLACE only the specified elements (person, product, scene, VO),
+     keeping every non-substituted detail exactly as analysed.
+
+Think of this as "same skeleton, swapped skin": the bones (camera, motion, action
+structure, scene order, pacing) never change; only the identity of the actors/products
+/backgrounds is swapped per the substitution instructions.
+
+Output ONLY a JSON array. Each element is one chunk. No markdown, no explanation.
+
+OUTPUT SCHEMA (JSON array):
+[
+  {
+    "chunk_id": 1,
+    "start": 0.0,
+    "end": 12.5,
+    "duration": 12.5,
+    "narrative_label": "hook",
+    "ratio": "9:16",
+    "voiceover_combined": "full VO text for this chunk",
+    "scenes": [
+      {
+        "scene_id": 1,
+        "start": 0.0,
+        "end": 2.8,
+        "subject":     "Who/what is in frame — appearance, clothing, product, brand.",
+        "action":      "Exact actions happening — micro-gestures, gaze, lip movement.",
+        "environment": "Space, background, props, lighting direction, color mood.",
+        "camera":      "Shot type, camera motion, composition, stability.",
+        "style":       "Color palette, texture, commercial feel, depth of field.",
+        "audio": {
+          "voiceover_text": "Exact words spoken in this scene window.",
+          "voice_style": "Voice tone and delivery.",
+          "ambient": "Ambient/operating sounds or 'no notable ambient sound'."
+        }
+      }
+    ]
+  }
+]
+
+════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+FIELD LOCK TABLE — follow this exactly:
+════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+
+FIELD       | DEFAULT RULE          | UNLOCK CONDITION
+------------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------------
+camera      | LOCKED — copy exactly | never changed
+style       | LOCKED — copy exactly | never changed
+action      | LOCKED — copy exactly | only adapt phrasing if the substituted subject
+            |                      | makes the original action physically impossible
+environment | LOCKED — copy exactly | unlock ONLY when 'scene' substitution is provided
+subject     | LOCKED — copy exactly | unlock when 'subject' or 'logo' substitution given
+person desc | part of subject field | unlock when 'person' substitution given
+audio.voiceover_text | LOCKED        | unlock when 'voiceover' substitution given
+
+IMPORTANT: "LOCKED" means copy the original analysed value verbatim (translated to
+English if needed) — do NOT paraphrase, enrich, or invent detail. Locked fields are
+the visual backbone; changing them breaks style fidelity.
+
+For UNLOCKED fields: integrate the substitution smoothly. Keep all non-substituted
+details in that field intact (e.g. if only the product changes, keep the person's
+original outfit, pose, and micro-actions; only swap the product name/appearance).
+
+════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+VOICEOVER SUBSTITUTION (when provided):
+════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+Distribute the new VO script across scenes proportionally to their original duration.
+Match sentence breaks to scene boundaries where possible.
+Do not add VO text to scenes that had no VO originally.
+
+════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+QUALITY RULES:
+════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+- subject (when unlocked): age-feel, gender, outfit color+material, product name+color+finish.
+- action (when unlocked): micro-actions — finger placement, gaze direction, lip movement.
+- environment (when unlocked): lighting direction, background texture, prop placement.
+- camera: shot type + motion + composition in one phrase, e.g. "ECU face, static, centered".
+- style: color temperature, retouching level, commercial feel.
+- Do NOT add subtitles, watermarks, or on-screen text — those go in Constraints only.
+"""
+
+CONSTRAINTS_BLOCK = """\
+【Chunk Constraints】
+No subtitles, title text, stickers, price tags, promotional text, or any overlay text in the frame.
+Brand logos, product packaging, and text naturally present on the product itself may appear.
+All explanatory information must be conveyed through actions, camera language, and voiceover only.
+No BGM or background music. Voiceover is audio-only — never render it as on-screen text."""
+
+
+def build_user_message(analysis: dict, subs: dict) -> str:
+    """Build the user message with explicit LOCKED / REPLACEABLE field separation.
+
+    Locked fields (camera, style, action backbone) are extracted and presented
+    separately so the model understands they must be copied verbatim.
+    Replaceable fields are clearly marked with the substitution target.
+    """
+    # --- Summarize active substitutions ---
+    active_subs: dict[str, str] = {
+        k: v.strip() for k, v in subs.items() if v and v.strip()
+    }
+    sub_lines = [f"  {k}: {v}" for k, v in active_subs.items()]
+    subs_text = "\n".join(sub_lines) if sub_lines else "  (none — keep all original)"
+
+    # --- Which fields are unlocked? ---
+    unlock_subject = bool(active_subs.get("subject") or active_subs.get("logo"))
+    unlock_person  = bool(active_subs.get("person"))
+    unlock_env     = bool(active_subs.get("scene"))
+    unlock_vo      = bool(active_subs.get("voiceover"))
+
+    # --- Build per-chunk layered representation ---
+    layered_chunks = []
+    for chunk in analysis.get("chunks", []):
+        layered_scenes = []
+        for scene in chunk.get("scenes", []):
+            cam   = scene.get("camera", {})
+            sty   = scene.get("style",  {})
+            subj  = scene.get("subject", {})
+            act   = scene.get("action",  {})
+            env   = scene.get("environment", {})
+            audio = scene.get("audio", {})
+            if not isinstance(audio, dict):
+                audio = {}
+
+            def _str(v: object) -> str:
+                return json.dumps(v, ensure_ascii=False) if isinstance(v, dict) else str(v)
+
+            locked: dict[str, str] = {
+                "camera": _str(cam),
+                "style":  _str(sty),
+            }
+            # action is locked unless substitution makes it physically impossible
+            locked["action_blueprint"] = _str(act)
+
+            replaceable: dict[str, object] = {}
+
+            # subject / person
+            if unlock_subject or unlock_person:
+                replaceable["subject_original"]     = _str(subj)
+                replaceable["subject_substitution"]  = (
+                    active_subs.get("subject") or active_subs.get("logo") or ""
+                )
+                replaceable["person_substitution"]   = active_subs.get("person", "")
+                replaceable["instruction"] = (
+                    "Swap the person/product identity as specified. "
+                    "Keep all other subject details (pose context, props not being replaced) intact."
+                )
+            else:
+                locked["subject"] = _str(subj)
+
+            # environment
+            if unlock_env:
+                replaceable["environment_original"]    = _str(env)
+                replaceable["environment_substitution"] = active_subs.get("scene", "")
+            else:
+                locked["environment"] = _str(env)
+
+            # voiceover
+            vo_text = ""
+            if isinstance(audio, dict):
+                vo_text = audio.get("voiceover_text", "") or ""
+            if unlock_vo:
+                replaceable["voiceover_original"]    = vo_text
+                replaceable["voiceover_substitution"] = "(distribute new VO script proportionally)"
+            else:
+                locked["voiceover_text"] = vo_text
+
+            layered_scenes.append({
+                "scene_id": scene.get("scene_id", scene.get("id", 0)),
+                "start":    scene.get("start", 0),
+                "end":      scene.get("end",   0),
+                "LOCKED_copy_verbatim":     locked,
+                "REPLACEABLE_apply_subs":   replaceable or "(none — all locked)",
+                "audio_meta": {
+                    "voice_style": audio.get("voice_style", ""),
+                    "ambient":     audio.get("ambient", ""),
+                },
+            })
+
+        layered_chunks.append({
+            "chunk_id":        chunk.get("chunk_id", chunk.get("id", 0)),
+            "start":           chunk.get("start", 0),
+            "end":             chunk.get("end",   0),
+            "narrative_label": chunk.get("narrative_label", ""),
+            "ratio":           chunk.get("ratio",  analysis.get("meta", {}).get("ratio", "9:16")),
+            "scenes": layered_scenes,
+        })
+
+    global_style = {
+        "meta":       analysis.get("meta", {}),
+        "voice_tone": analysis.get("voice_tone", {}),
+        "style_tags": analysis.get("style_tags", {}),
+    }
+
+    unlock_summary = {
+        "subject_unlocked":     unlock_subject,
+        "person_unlocked":      unlock_person,
+        "environment_unlocked": unlock_env,
+        "voiceover_unlocked":   unlock_vo,
+        "camera_locked":        True,
+        "style_locked":         True,
+        "action_locked":        True,
+    }
+
+    return (
+        f"SUBSTITUTIONS (active):\n{subs_text}\n\n"
+        f"FIELD UNLOCK STATUS:\n{json.dumps(unlock_summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)}\n\n"
+        f"GLOBAL STYLE (always locked — preserve across all chunks):\n"
+        f"{json.dumps(global_style, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)}\n\n"
+        f"LAYERED SCENE DATA:\n"
+        f"{json.dumps(layered_chunks, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)}\n\n"
+        f"NEW VOICEOVER SCRIPT (apply if voiceover_unlocked=true, else ignore):\n"
+        f"{active_subs.get('voiceover', '(none)')}"
+    )
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Markdown formatter for user review
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def render_markdown(chunks: list[dict]) -> str:
+    lines: list[str] = []
+    for chunk in chunks:
+        cid   = chunk["chunk_id"]
+        start = chunk["start"]
+        end   = chunk["end"]
+        dur   = chunk["duration"]
+        ratio = chunk.get("ratio", "9:16")
+        label = chunk.get("narrative_label", "")
+        vo    = chunk.get("voiceover_combined", "")
+        scenes = chunk.get("scenes", [])
+
+        lines += [
+            f"## Chunk {cid}｜{start:.1f}s – {end:.1f}s｜约 {dur:.0f} 秒  [{label}]",
+            "",
+            f"生成一条 {ratio} 短视频，当前 Chunk 时长约 {dur:.0f} 秒。",
+            "",
+        ]
+
+        for i, s in enumerate(scenes, 1):
+            s_start = s.get("start", 0)
+            s_end   = s.get("end",   0)
+            audio   = s.get("audio", {})
+            lines += [
+                f"### 细节分镜 {i}｜{s_start:.1f}s – {s_end:.1f}s",
+                "",
+                "【Subject 主体】",
+                s.get("subject", ""),
+                "",
+                "【Action 动作】",
+                s.get("action", ""),
+                "",
+                "【Environment 场景】",
+                s.get("environment", ""),
+                "",
+                "【Camera 镜头】",
+                s.get("camera", ""),
+                "",
+                "【Style 视觉风格】",
+                s.get("style", ""),
+                "",
+                "【Audio 声音】",
+                "口播/对白：“" + (audio.get("voiceover_text", "") or "无口播") + "”。",
+                f"声音描述：{audio.get('voice_style', '')}",
+                f"环境音/操作音：{audio.get('ambient', '无明显环境音')}",
+                "",
+                "---",
+                "",
+            ]
+
+        lines += [
+            CONSTRAINTS_BLOCK,
+            "",
+            "---",
+            "",
+        ]
+
+    return "\n".join(lines)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Main
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
+        description="Stage 3 — Compose per-chunk Seedance prompts via claude-sonnet-4-6"
+    )
+    ap.add_argument("--analysis",  required=True, help="style_analysis.json")
+    ap.add_argument("--subs",      required=True, help="substitutions.json")
+    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", "-o", required=True)
+    ap.add_argument("--model",     default="claude-sonnet-4-6")
+    ap.add_argument("--max-tokens", type=int, default=8192)
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    for var in ("LITELLM_API_BASE", "LITELLM_API_KEY"):
+        if not os.environ.get(var):
+            sys.exit(f"env {var} not set")
+
+    out_dir  = Path(args.out_dir).expanduser().resolve()
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    analysis = json.loads(Path(args.analysis).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    subs     = json.loads(Path(args.subs).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+    ratio    = analysis.get("meta", {}).get("ratio", "9:16")
+
+    print(
+        f"[Stage 3] Composing Chunk Prompts\n"
+        f"  model:  {args.model}\n"
+        f"  chunks: {len(analysis.get('chunks', []))}\n"
+        f"  subs:   {[k for k,v in subs.items() if v and v.strip()]}",
+        file=sys.stderr,
+    )
+
+    user_msg = build_user_message(analysis, subs)
+    messages = [
+        {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
+        {"role": "user",   "content": user_msg},
+    ]
+
+    raw = call_llm(messages, model=args.model, max_tokens=args.max_tokens)
+
+    # Strip markdown fences
+    raw = raw.strip()
+    if raw.startswith("```"):
+        raw = re.sub(r"^```[a-z]*\s*", "", raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)
+        raw = re.sub(r"```\s*$", "",          raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)

+
+    try:
+        chunks = json.loads(raw)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
+        raw_path = out_dir / "chunk_prompts_raw.txt"
+        raw_path.write_text(raw, encoding="utf-8")

+    # Inject ratio + duration fallback into each chunk (M3)
+    for chunk in chunks:

+            chunk["duration"] = round(float(chunk.get("end", 0)) - float(chunk.get("start", 0)), 2)
+
+    # Write JSON
+    json_path = out_dir / "chunk_prompts.json"
+    json_path.write_text(json.dumps(chunks, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+
+    # Write Markdown for user review
+    md_path = out_dir / "chunk_prompts.md"
+    md_path.write_text(render_markdown(chunks), encoding="utf-8")
+
+    print(
+        f"[Stage 3] Done → {json_path}\n"
+        f"  {len(chunks)} chunks written",
+        file=sys.stderr,
+    )
+    print(json.dumps({
+        "chunk_prompts_json": str(json_path),
+        "chunk_prompts_md":   str(md_path),
+        "chunks":             len(chunks),
+        "ratio":              ratio,
+    }, ensure_ascii=False))
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
