+# video-editor Skill
+
+Shared post-production layer for all Video Duplicate workflows (A/B/C).
+Handles everything after Seedance chunk generation: concat, BGM mix, subtitle burn.
+
+## Scripts
+
+### concat.py — ffmpeg concat only (NO audio mixing)
+
+```bash
+python3 concat.py \
+    --chunks-dir /path/to/chunks/ \
+    --out        /path/to/stitched.mp4
+```
+
+Optional: `--chunks-json seedance_results.json` to read chunk paths from state file
+instead of globbing `chunks/chunk_NN.mp4`.
+
+**Pipeline:**
+1. Write ffmpeg concat list from sorted `chunk_NN.mp4` files
+2. `ffmpeg -f concat` → `stitched.mp4` (video+audio copy, no re-encode)
+3. **Does NOT touch audio** — Seedance VO is preserved as-is
+
+⚠️ **Audio mixing is a SEPARATE step** — always use `audio_postprocess.py` after concat.
+
+---
+
+### audio_postprocess.py — Mix Seedance VO with Demucs BGM (CORRECT pipeline)
+
+**IMPORTANT: Never replace Seedance audio with original video audio directly.**
+Seedance generates human VO + ambient SFX. BGM must be added on top, not substituted.
+
+```bash
+# Step 1: Run Demucs to extract pure music from source video
+uv run --with demucs --with soundfile \
+  python3 -m demucs --two-stems vocals \
+  --out runs/r01/demucs_out/ \
+  /path/to/source.mp4
+# → demucs_out/htdemucs/<source_name>/no_vocals.wav  ← pure BGM
+# → demucs_out/htdemucs/<source_name>/vocals.wav     ← reference only
+
+# Step 2: Mix Seedance VO + extracted BGM
+python3 audio_postprocess.py \
+    --video   runs/r01/stitched.mp4 \
+    --bgm     runs/r01/demucs_out/htdemucs/<source_name>/no_vocals.wav \
+    --output  runs/r01/stitched_with_bgm.mp4 \
+    --bgm-db  -8 \
+    --fade-out 0.5
+```
+
+**Parameters:**
+- `--bgm-db`: BGM volume relative to VO (default -8 dB). Tune to taste:
+  - `-6` dB: BGM clearly audible, VO dominant
+  - `-10` dB: BGM very subtle, VO dominant
+  - `-4` dB: BGM prominent (use only for music-heavy segments)
+- `--vo-db`: VO volume adjustment (default 0 dB, no change)
+- `--fade-out`: BGM fade-out at end in seconds (default 0.5s)
+
+**Demucs torchaudio note:** If torchaudio save fails with torchcodec error,
+run demucs via subprocess with the soundfile monkey-patch (see `run_pipeline.py`).
+
+### Subtitle Burn — three-skill pipeline (canonical, v2)
+
+**subtitle_burn.py is retired.** Subtitles are now generated via three dedicated skills
+that produce word-accurate timing through real forced alignment.
+
+#### Pipeline overview
+
+```
+video → [video-transcribe] → words.srt
+                                 ↓
+                        [video-burn-subtitle]
+                          LLM segmentation
+                          ASS generation
+                          ffmpeg burn
+                                 ↓
+                          final_subtitled.mp4
+```
+
+#### Step 1 — ASR (zooclaw-asr)
+
+```bash
+bash {skills}/zooclaw-asr/scripts/transcribe.sh \
+    "$VIDEO" -o transcript.txt -f text
+```
+
+#### Step 2 — Word-level forced alignment (video-transcribe)
+
+> ⚠️ **必须使用干净音频**：ASR 和 forced-align 要求纯人声内容，BGM 会严重干扰识别精度。有两种方案：
+>
+> **方案 A（首推）**：在 `audio_postprocess`（BGM 混入）**之前**调用本脚本，传入 `stitched.mp4`。
+> Seedance 输出只有 VO + 环境音，无 BGM，无需额外处理。
+>
+> **方案 B（BGM 已混入时的兼容方案）**：传入 `stitched_bgm.mp4` 并加 `--vocals-only` flag，
+> 脚本会先用 Demucs 分离出干净人声再送 ASR。
+
+```bash
+export ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL=https://forced-align.g.yesy.online
+
+# 方案 A（首推）：BGM 混入之前调用，输入为纯静 Seedance 合并视频
+bash {skills}/video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh \
+    -i "$STITCHED" \
+    -o words.srt
+
+# 方案 B：已混入 BGM 时，加 --vocals-only（Demucs 分离干净人声）
+bash {skills}/video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh \
+    -i "$STITCHED_BGM" \
+    --vocals-only \
+    -o words.srt
+```
+
+Outputs one SRT entry per character/word with 80ms-level precision.
+
+#### Step 3 — LLM segmentation + burn (video-burn-subtitle)
+
+```bash
+bash {skills}/video-burn-subtitle/scripts/burn.sh \
+    -i "$VIDEO_WITH_BGM" \
+    -s words.srt \
+    --orientation portrait \
+    -o final_subtitled.mp4
+```
+
+**Auto-detected settings (portrait 720×1280):**
+- Font: 方正金陵 Bold (fzjingling.ttf, bundled in skill assets)
+- Font size: 64px
+- Max chars/line: 8
+- Position: `pos(vw/2, vh×2/3)`
+- Style: white, no outline, black shadow 70% opacity at -45°
+
+**LLM segmentation rules (enforced by llm_segment.py):**
+- Max 8 CJK chars per line (CJK=1.0, ASCII=0.5 width)
+- Particles (了啊呢哦吧) follow previous phrase
+- Reduplicated words / number sequences / brand names kept whole
+- Force break at silence ≥ 800ms
+- Min 4 chars per entry
+
+## Integration pattern
+
+Workflow run_pipeline.py calls video-editor + subtitle skills as shell subprocesses:
+
+```python
+import subprocess, os
+from pathlib import Path
+
+_SKILLS     = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent  # .agents/skills/
+_VE_CONCAT  = str(_SKILLS / "video-editor" / "scripts" / "concat.py")
+_ASR        = str(_SKILLS / "zooclaw-asr" / "scripts" / "transcribe.sh")
+_TRANSCRIBE = str(_SKILLS / "video-transcribe" / "scripts" / "transcribe.sh")
+_BURN       = str(_SKILLS / "video-burn-subtitle" / "scripts" / "burn.sh")
+
+env = {**os.environ,
+       "ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL": "https://forced-align.g.yesy.online"}
+
+# 1. concat (Seedance chunks → stitched.mp4, VO-only audio, no BGM yet)
+_run(["python3", _VE_CONCAT, "--source", src, "--chunks-dir", chunks, "--out", stitched])
+
+# 2. 字幕时间轴对齐——必须在 BGM 混入之前运行，使用纯净 Seedance VO 音频
+# stitched.mp4 = Seedance 输出合并，只有 VO+环境音，无 BGM，ASR 准确度最高
+_run(["bash", _TRANSCRIBE, "-i", stitched, "-o", words_srt], env=env)
+
+# 3. BGM mix (stitched.mp4 + Demucs BGM → stitched_bgm.mp4)
+_run(["python3", _VE_AUDIO, "--video", stitched, "--source", src,
+      "--output", stitched_bgm, "--bgm-db", "-8"])
+
+# 4. burn subtitles (words.srt 已对齐到纯 VO 时间轴，直接烧到含 BGM 的最终视频)
+_run(["bash", _BURN, "-i", stitched_bgm, "-s", words_srt,
+      "--orientation", "portrait", "-o", final_out], env=env)
+
+# 备用：如果工作流已经混入 BGM 才进入字幕阶段，用 --vocals-only 减载 BGM 干扰
+# _run(["bash", _TRANSCRIBE, "-i", stitched_bgm, "--vocals-only", "-o", words_srt], env=env)
+```
+
+**Required env vars:**
+- `USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN` — zooclaw ASR + forced-align auth
+- `ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL` — `https://forced-align.g.yesy.online`
+- `OPENAI_API_KEY` + `OPENAI_BASE_URL` — LLM segmentation (claude-sonnet-4-6 via litellm)
+
+---
+
+## References
+
+| File | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| `references/audio-loudness-standards.md` | BGM 混音调参指南：LUFS 目标、voice/BGM 分离规则、amix 陷阱、crossfade 参数（经过 spike 验证） |
+| `references/subtitle-postprocess-notes.md` | 字幕后处理历史注记（已归档，新流程不再使用 postprocess.py） |
+
+## Skills
+
+| Skill dir | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| `video-editor/` | concat.py 、audio_postprocess.py （concat + BGM mix） |
+| `zooclaw-asr/` | ASR 转录（第一步，得到文本） |
+| `video-transcribe/` | ASR + forced-align → word-level SRT |
+| `video-burn-subtitle/` | LLM 分段 + ASS 生成 + ffmpeg burn |
+| `zooclaw-forcedalign/` | 底层强制对齐 API（video-transcribe 依赖） |
+
+## Dependencies
+
+- `ffmpeg` (system): concat, subtitle burn, audio mix
+- `uv` + `demucs` + `torchaudio` + `soundfile`: BGM separation (Python 3.11)
+- `fzjingling.ttf`: bundled in `video-burn-subtitle/assets/`（安装到 `~/.local/share/fonts/`）
+- `USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN`: ASR + forced-align 认证
+- `ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL`: `https://forced-align.g.yesy.online`
+- `OPENAI_API_KEY` + `OPENAI_BASE_URL`: LLM 分段（claude-sonnet-4-6 via litellm）

+# Audio Loudness Standards & Mixing Cheatsheet
+
+Validated 2026-05-15 on a 17s Chinese marketing clip. Use this when tuning
+`stitch_cli.py` and interpreting `audio_analyze_cli.py` output.
+
+---
+
+## Integrated Loudness (LUFS) targets
+
+| Platform | Target | Notes |
+|---|---|---|
+| TikTok / Reels / Shorts | **-14 LUFS** | Our `stitch_cli.py` default |
+| YouTube | -14 LUFS | Auto-normalizes louder uploads |
+| Spotify (audio) | -14 LUFS | |
+| Apple Music | -16 LUFS | |
+| Broadcast TV (EBU R128) | -23 LUFS | Not relevant for social |
+
+LUFS = Loudness Units relative to Full Scale. "Integrated" = average over the
+entire program, weighted to match human perception.
+
+**True peak target**: ≤ -1.5 dBFS (room for inter-sample peaks during
+streaming codec re-encode). `loudnorm=TP=-1.5` handles this.
+
+---
+
+## Voice / BGM separation (rule of thumb)
+
+| Content type | Voice − BGM (LU) | Why |
+|---|---|---|
+| Dialogue-driven (口播带货, podcast, lecture) | **10–15 LU** | Voice intelligibility wins |
+| Mixed (vlog, short story) | 8–10 LU | Some music drive, some narration |
+| Music-driven | 6–10 LU | Music = primary content |
+| **Hard floor** | > 6 LU | Below this, BGM steals attention |
+
+For storyboard-studio (marketing 口播), default target is **10 LU separation**.
+
+---
+
+## How chunk-to-chunk voice drift sneaks in
+
+Each Seedance task generates its own prosody/gain — empirically observed
+**2–4 LU drift** between independently-generated chunks (spike: chunk 1 = -9.3 LUFS, chunk 2 = -12.3 LUFS, drift 3 LU). Without normalization, the concat sounds like the speaker "leaned closer/farther from the mic" between cuts.
+
+**Fix**: `loudnorm I=-14:LRA=11:TP=-1.5` per chunk. One-pass is fine for
+short-form (<60s). For broadcast accuracy use two-pass (`audio_analyze_cli`
++ measured values into loudnorm).
+
+---
+
+## The amix `normalize` trap
+
+`ffmpeg amix=inputs=2` defaults to `normalize=1`, which divides each input
+by the input count to prevent clipping. For 2 inputs, this is a **silent
+-6dB attenuation** on both voice and BGM. The result:
+
+| Spike attempt | BGM dB flag | amix normalize | Voice LUFS | Final LUFS | BGM audibility |
+|---|---|---|---|---|---|
+| v1 | -12 | default (1) | -16.8 | -16.8 | barely audible (-31 LUFS) |
+| v2 | -5 | default (1) | -14 (normalized) | -19.6 | still too soft |
+| **v3 (production)** | -8 | **0** + alimiter 0.97 | -14 (normalized) | **-13.5** | ~-22 LUFS = clear |
+
+**Lesson**: always use `amix=normalize=0` + an `alimiter` to catch peak
+clipping. Apply BGM volume attenuation **before** amix, not after.
+
+---
+
+## Recommended `stitch_cli.py` flags
+
+```bash
+python3 stitch_cli.py \
+  --chunks chunk_01.mp4 chunk_02.mp4 ... \
+  --bgm no_vocals.wav \
+  --target-lufs -14 \
+  --bgm-db -8 \
+  --crossfade-ms 200 \
+  --limiter-peak 0.97 \
+  --out final.mp4
+```
+
+## Constant vs Sidechain Ducking
+
+`stitch_cli.py --duck-mode` chooses how BGM behaves under voice:
+
+| Mode | Behavior | Best for |
+|---|---|---|
+| `constant` *(default)* | BGM stays at `--bgm-db` baseline the whole time | Simple, predictable, the right default for short-form口播 |
+| `sidechain` *(opt-in)* | BGM ducks `--duck-ratio` further when voice exceeds `--duck-threshold`; recovers after `--duck-release-ms` of silence | Dialogue with natural pauses (>0.5s); broadcast cinematic feel |
+
+Default sidechain params (validated for Chinese 口播):
+- `--duck-threshold 0.05` (linear; ~−26 dBFS) — triggers on speech, not noise floor
+- `--duck-ratio 8.0` — strong ducking (~6–10 dB more attenuation while voice plays)
+- `--duck-attack-ms 20` — fast attack catches start of words
+- `--duck-release-ms 300` — keeps BGM down across short between-word gaps; recovers in actual breath pauses
+
+**Caveat from spike validation (2026-05-15)**: on a 17s dense口播 clip with all sentence gaps < 0.32s, sidechain and constant produced almost identical loudness (-13.7 vs -13.5 LUFS). The difference shows up on clips with > 0.5s voice pauses where BGM gets to recover audibly. For short 17s dense clips, either mode works; for >30s scripts with natural pacing, sidechain is recognizably more cinematic.
+
+## Tune BGM dB Per-Job
+
+
+
+```bash
+python3 audio_analyze_cli.py \
+  --voice chunk_01.mp4 --voice chunk_02.mp4 \
+  --bgm no_vocals.wav \
+  --target-final-lufs -14 --target-separation-lu 10
+# Reads voice + BGM LUFS, prints recommended_bgm_db
+```
+
+---
+
+## Crossfade at concat boundaries
+
+200ms `xfade` (video) + `acrossfade` (audio) is the spike-validated sweet
+spot for marketing口播:
+- < 100ms: still hear clicks
+- 150-300ms: smooth, no perceptual lag
+- > 400ms: noticeable cross-dissolve which can look like a stylistic transition (sometimes desirable)
+
+For each boundary, the cumulative offset must be `sum(prev_durations) − xfade_s` to avoid frame skipping.
+
+---
+
+## Chunk-boundary placement (Stage 4 chunk planner)
+
+Independent of audio mixing: the choice of where to place a chunk boundary
+affects how seamless the final VO sounds.
+
+**Rule**: among sentence boundaries within ±2s of the target split point
+(usually ≤14s mark for a Seedance chunk), pick the one whose
+sentence-to-next-sentence **gap is largest** (≥ 0.3s preferred). Gaps < 0.2s
+indicate continuous口播 with no natural breath — cutting there will sound
+abrupt regardless of audio mixing.
+
+The 2026-05-14 spike split at gap 0.16s (S05 → S06) and the user perceived
+"the sentence was cut in half". Re-planned to gap 0.32s (S06 → S07) would
+have read naturally. This rule is now in `scene_analyze_cli.py`'s LLM prompt.

+# Subtitle Post-processing Notes
+
+Captures upstream patches applied to `postprocess.py`.
+File now lives at: **`video-editor/scripts/forced-align/postprocess.py`**
+This reference doc now lives at: **`video-editor/references/subtitle-postprocess-notes.md`**
+(both moved from `storyboard-studio/sibling-skills/subtitle-data/` in v1.6.0)
+
+These are sibling-skill vendor mods — if the upstream zooclaw/auto-caption skill is ever refreshed, **re-apply these patches** before running.
+
+---
+
+## Patch 1 (2026-05-15) — Approximate-number idiom detection
+
+### Problem
+
+Original `_cn_to_arabic` aggressively converted ALL consecutive Chinese numeral chars to Arabic. This broke on approximate idioms:
+
+- "两三万" → `_cn_to_arabic` parsed "两三" as last-digit-only (=3) × 10000 = **30000** (wrong; "两三万" is a range, not a precise number)
+- "五六万" → 60000 (wrong)
+- "一两个" → 12 (wrong)
+
+ASR (Qwen3) outputs the correct Chinese string; only the postprocess merge step corrupts it.
+
+### Fix
+
+Two-part patch in `postprocess.py`:
+
+1. **`_cn_to_arabic` now refuses approximate idioms**:
+   ```python
+   _APPROX_DIGIT_PAIRS = {"一两","两三","三四","四五","五六","六七","七八","八九"}
+   def _has_approximate_idiom(s): return any(p in s for p in _APPROX_DIGIT_PAIRS)
+   # in _cn_to_arabic: return None if approximate
+   ```
+
+2. **`merge_cn_numbers` fallback no longer retries sub-sequences** on `arabic is None`:
+   ```python
+   # Old (buggy): out.append(items[i]); i += 1
+   #              → "两三万" rejected → "两" kept → next iter "三万" Arabicized → "两30000"
+   # New: when arabic is None on multi-char run, keep ALL chars and skip past
+   if j - i > 1:
+       for k in range(i, j): out.append(items[k])
+       i = j; continue
+   ```
+
+### Preserved behaviors (intentionally NOT changed)
+
+| Pattern | Behavior |
+|---|---|
+| 三万 / 五千 / 三百 | → 30000 / 5000 / 300 (Arabicized, useful) |
+| 十一点十二 | → "11.12" via `merge_decimal_points` (useful) |
+| 二零二四年 | Still has a separate upstream bug (returns `4` not `2024`); not our patch's concern |
+
+### Design principle
+
+> **Trust the ASR's lexical output. Post-process should only do safe, lossless
+> transforms.** Approximate-number idioms have no single Arabic representation —
+> any conversion is wrong. Same logic applies to AABB reduplication, brand+model
+> units, and other idioms the upstream rules already handle correctly.
+
+User feedback (2026-05-15):
+> 是不是根据 zooclaw asr 自己的能力判断就行，不要强加特殊逻辑？
+
+Verbatim correct: ASR (Qwen3-ASR-1.7B) outputs proper Chinese ("要两三万") from
+the audio; the merge_cn_numbers post-process should only convert when it can do
+so faithfully. The patch enforces that.
+
+---
+
+## Future patches to consider
+
+- `_cn_to_arabic` "二零二四" returns `4`, not `2024` — needs special-case
+  digit-only sequences without unit chars. Out of scope for Patch 1.
+- Width cap pagination occasionally splits verb phrases ("...朋友来 / 体验感受"
+  vs natural "...的朋友 / 来体验感受"). No simple rule fix; relies on review step.
+- Numeric measure-word blacklist `_KEEP_CN_AFTER = "倍次遍位成番轮分"` could be
+  extended (个/些/天/年/层 etc.) but each addition risks new false positives.
+  Defer; rely on review.

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""audio_postprocess.py — Mix Seedance VO audio with Demucs-extracted BGM.
+
+Correct audio pipeline for video-duplicate workflows:
+  1. Seedance-generated video already has human VO + ambient SFX (no BGM)
+  2. Demucs extracts pure music (no_vocals.wav) from the original reference video
+  3. This script mixes the two: Seedance VO is kept at 0 dB, BGM is attenuated
+
+Usage:
+    python3 audio_postprocess.py \\
+        --video   runs/r01/stitched.mp4          \\  # Seedance concat (has VO)
+        --bgm     runs/r01/demucs_out/.../no_vocals.wav \\
+        --output  runs/r01/stitched_with_bgm.mp4 \\
+        [--bgm-db -8]                                # BGM attenuation (default -8 dB)
+        [--fade-out 0.5]                             # BGM fade-out seconds at end
+
+The output replaces the video's audio track with the mixed result.
+VO is always preserved; BGM is looped/trimmed to match video duration.
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import os
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+def run(cmd: list[str], check: bool = True) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
+    print(f"[audio_postprocess] $ {' '.join(cmd)}", file=sys.stderr)
+    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
+    if result.stdout:
+        print(result.stdout, file=sys.stderr)
+    if result.stderr:
+        print(result.stderr, file=sys.stderr)
+    if check and result.returncode != 0:
+        sys.exit(f"[audio_postprocess] Command failed (rc={result.returncode})")
+    return result
+
+
+def get_duration(path: str) -> float:
+    r = run(["ffprobe", "-v", "error", "-show_entries", "format=duration",
+             "-of", "csv=p=0", path])
+    return float(r.stdout.strip())
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Mix Seedance VO with Demucs BGM")
+    ap.add_argument("--video",    required=True, help="Input video with Seedance VO audio")
+    ap.add_argument("--bgm",      required=True, help="Demucs no_vocals.wav (pure music)")
+    ap.add_argument("--output",   required=True, help="Output mixed video path")
+    ap.add_argument("--bgm-db",   type=float, default=-8.0,
+                    help="BGM volume adjustment in dB (default: -8.0, negative = quieter)")
+    ap.add_argument("--vo-db",    type=float, default=0.0,
+                    help="VO volume adjustment in dB (default: 0.0, no change)")
+    ap.add_argument("--fade-out", type=float, default=0.5,
+                    help="BGM fade-out duration in seconds at end (default: 0.5)")
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    video_path  = args.video
+    bgm_path    = args.bgm
+    output_path = args.output
+    bgm_db      = args.bgm_db
+    vo_db       = args.vo_db
+    fade_out    = args.fade_out
+
+    # Validate inputs
+    for p, label in [(video_path, "video"), (bgm_path, "bgm")]:
+        if not Path(p).exists():
+            sys.exit(f"[audio_postprocess] {label} not found: {p}")
+
+    Path(output_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    vid_dur = get_duration(video_path)
+    bgm_dur = get_duration(bgm_path)
+    print(f"[audio_postprocess] video={vid_dur:.2f}s  bgm={bgm_dur:.2f}s  "
+          f"bgm_db={bgm_db:+.1f}  vo_db={vo_db:+.1f}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    # Build ffmpeg filter_complex:
+    # [0:a] = Seedance VO track (stream 0, audio)
+    # [1:a] = Demucs BGM (stream 1, audio)
+    # BGM: loop if shorter than video, trim to video duration, apply volume + fade-out
+    # VO:  apply optional volume adjustment
+    # amix both streams, normalize=0 to preserve individual levels
+
+    fade_start = max(0.0, vid_dur - fade_out)
+
+    # Loop BGM if needed (aloop=-1 = infinite loop, size=samples estimate)
+    bgm_loop = ""
+    if bgm_dur < vid_dur:
+        # aloop: loop count = ceil(vid_dur / bgm_dur) + 1 for safety
+        import math
+        loops = math.ceil(vid_dur / bgm_dur) + 1
+        bgm_loop = f"aloop=loop={loops}:size=2e+09,"
+
+    filter_complex = (
+        f"[1:a]{bgm_loop}"
+        f"atrim=0:{vid_dur:.3f},"
+        f"afade=t=out:st={fade_start:.3f}:d={fade_out},"
+        f"volume={bgm_db:.1f}dB"
+        f"[bgm];"
+        f"[0:a]volume={vo_db:.1f}dB[vo];"
+        f"[vo][bgm]amix=inputs=2:normalize=0[aout]"
+    )
+
+    cmd = [
+        "ffmpeg", "-y",
+        "-i", video_path,          # stream 0: video + VO
+        "-i", bgm_path,            # stream 1: BGM wav
+        "-filter_complex", filter_complex,
+        "-map", "0:v",             # keep original video track
+        "-map", "[aout]",          # use mixed audio
+        "-c:v", "copy",
+        "-c:a", "aac", "-b:a", "192k",
+        "-shortest",
+        output_path
+    ]
+
+    run(cmd)
+    out_dur = get_duration(output_path)
+    out_size = Path(output_path).stat().st_size // 1024
+    print(f"[audio_postprocess] ✅ done → {output_path} ({out_dur:.2f}s, {out_size}KB)",
+          file=sys.stderr)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""video-editor: concat — ffmpeg concat + Demucs BGM separation + audio mix.
+
+Reads chunk mp4 paths from seedance_results.json (or --chunks list),
+stitches them with ffmpeg concat, separates BGM from source via Demucs,
+then mixes BGM into the stitched video.
+
+Input:
+    --source     Original reference mp4 (for BGM extraction)
+    --chunks-dir Directory containing seedance chunk mp4s (chunk_NN.mp4)
+    --out        Output mp4 path
+
+Output:
+    <out>                  Final stitched+BGM mp4
+    <out-dir>/stitched.mp4 Intermediate concat (no BGM)
+
+Usage:
+    python3 concat.py \\
+        --source  runs/run-01/source.mp4 \\
+        --chunks-dir runs/run-01/chunks/ \\
+        --out     runs/run-01/stitched_with_bgm.mp4
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import os
+import subprocess
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+_THIS_DIR   = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
+_SKILL_DIR  = _THIS_DIR.parent
+# audio_postprocess_cli.py lives in storyboard-studio sibling skill
+_AUDIO_POST = (
+    _SKILL_DIR.parent / "storyboard-studio" / "scripts" / "audio_postprocess_cli.py"
+)
+
+
+def _run(cmd: list[str], check: bool = True) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
+    print(f"  $ {' '.join(cmd[:8])} …", file=sys.stderr)
+    result = subprocess.run(cmd)
+    if check and result.returncode != 0:
+        sys.exit(f"Command failed (rc={result.returncode}): {cmd[0]}")
+    return result
+
+
+def _get_duration(video_path: Path) -> float:
+    """Return video duration in seconds using ffprobe."""
+    result = subprocess.run(
+        [
+            "ffprobe", "-v", "quiet",
+            "-print_format", "json",
+            "-show_format",
+            str(video_path),
+        ],
+        capture_output=True, text=True,
+    )
+    if result.returncode != 0:
+        return 0.0
+    try:
+        info = json.loads(result.stdout)
+        return float(info["format"]["duration"])
+    except (KeyError, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError):
+        return 0.0
+
+
+
+# ── Bitrate normalisation helpers ───────────────────────────────────────
+
+def _get_video_bitrate(path: Path) -> int:
+    """Return video stream bitrate in kbps, or estimate from file size / duration."""
+    r = subprocess.run(
+        ["ffprobe", "-v", "error", "-select_streams", "v:0",
+         "-show_entries", "stream=bit_rate", "-of", "csv=p=0", str(path)],
+        capture_output=True, text=True,
+    )
+    try:
+        return int(r.stdout.strip()) // 1000
+    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
+        try:
+            dur = _get_duration(path)
+            return int(path.stat().st_size * 8 / dur / 1000) if dur > 0 else 0
+        except Exception:
+            return 0
+
+
+def _normalize_chunk(src: Path, out: Path, crf: int = 20) -> Path:
+    """Re-encode one chunk to h264 crf20 / aac 128k baseline for safe concat."""
+    print(f"  [concat] normalising {src.name} → {out.name}", file=sys.stderr)
+    subprocess.run([
+        "ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(src),
+        "-c:v", "libx264", "-crf", str(crf), "-preset", "fast", "-r", "24",
+        "-c:a", "aac", "-ar", "44100", "-ac", "2", "-b:a", "128k",
+        str(out),
+    ], check=True)
+    return out
+
+
+# If any chunk's bitrate exceeds median by this factor, normalise all before concat.
+_BITRATE_SKEW_THRESHOLD = 2.5
+
+def concat_and_mix(
+    source_video: Path,
+    chunk_paths: list[str],
+    out_path: Path,
+) -> None:
+    out_dir = out_path.parent
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    if not chunk_paths:
+        sys.exit("[concat] No chunk mp4s provided.")
+
+    # ── 1. Bitrate skew check + optional normalisation ────────────────────
+    # After subtitle erasure the output file can be 3-4x larger than other chunks.
+    # Mixing bitrates with -c copy results in a container whose video-stream
+    # metadata duration reflects only the first chunk, truncating downstream tools.
+    # Detect skew above threshold and re-encode all chunks to a uniform baseline.
+    abs_paths = [Path(pp).resolve() for pp in chunk_paths]
+    bitrates  = [_get_video_bitrate(pp) for pp in abs_paths]
+    valid_br  = [b for b in bitrates if b > 0]
+
+    if valid_br:
+        median_br = sorted(valid_br)[len(valid_br) // 2]
+        max_br    = max(valid_br)
+        skew      = max_br / median_br if median_br > 0 else 1.0
+        print(f"  [concat] chunk bitrates kbps={bitrates}  median={median_br}  "
+              f"max={max_br}  skew={skew:.1f}x", file=sys.stderr)
+
+        if skew > _BITRATE_SKEW_THRESHOLD:
+            print(f"  [concat] skew {skew:.1f}x > {_BITRATE_SKEW_THRESHOLD}x — "
+                  "normalising all chunks before concat", file=sys.stderr)
+            norm_dir = out_dir / "chunks_norm"
+            norm_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
+            normalised: list[Path] = []
+            for i, src_chunk in enumerate(abs_paths):
+                norm_out = norm_dir / f"chunk_{i:02d}_norm.mp4"
+                if not norm_out.exists():
+                    _normalize_chunk(src_chunk, norm_out)
+                normalised.append(norm_out)
+            abs_paths = normalised
+        else:
+            print(f"  [concat] bitrate skew OK ({skew:.1f}x) — using -c copy",
+                  file=sys.stderr)
+
+    # ── 2. ffmpeg concat ────────────────────────────────────────────
+    concat_list = out_dir / "concat_list.txt"
+    concat_list.write_text("\n".join(f"file '{pp}'" for pp in abs_paths))
+
+    stitched = out_dir / "stitched.mp4"
+    _run([
+        "ffmpeg", "-y",
+        "-f", "concat", "-safe", "0",
+        "-i", str(concat_list),
+        "-c", "copy",
+        str(stitched),
+    ])
+
+    # ── 3. Demucs BGM separation ────────────────────────────────────────────
+    audio_dir = out_dir / "audio_split"
+    audio_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
+
+    if _AUDIO_POST.is_file():
+        _run([
+            "uv", "run",
+            "--with", "demucs",
+            "--with", "torchaudio",
+            "--with", "soundfile",
+            "--python", "3.11",
+            "--",
+            "python3", str(_AUDIO_POST),
+            "--source",  str(source_video),
+            "--out-dir", str(audio_dir),
+        ])
+    else:
+        print(f"  [warn] audio_postprocess_cli not found at {_AUDIO_POST}; "
+              "will copy source audio track directly.", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    # ── 4. Find BGM stem ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    bgm_files = list(audio_dir.rglob("no_vocals.mp3"))
+    if not bgm_files:
+        bgm_files = list(audio_dir.rglob("no_vocals.wav"))
+    bgm_path = str(bgm_files[0]) if bgm_files else ""
+
+    # ── 5. Mix audio: Seedance VO (chunk audio) + Demucs BGM ───────────────
+    # Audio strategy:
+    #   - Seedance chunks carry their own VO + environment audio (no BGM since
+    #     Seedance is instructed not to generate background music)
+    #   - Demucs no_vocals BGM from source video is mixed in at -3dB as background
+    #   - If Demucs BGM not available, fall back to Seedance audio only (no BGM)
+    #   - afade on BGM tail: fade starts at (duration - 1.5s) to avoid hard cut at end
+    if bgm_path:
+        print(f"  [audio] Mixing Seedance VO + Demucs BGM ({bgm_path})", file=sys.stderr)
+
+        # Get stitched video duration to correctly position the BGM fade-out
+        total_dur = _get_duration(stitched)
+        fade_dur  = 1.5   # seconds for fade-out tail
+        fade_st   = max(0.0, total_dur - fade_dur) if total_dur > 0 else 0.0
+        print(f"  [audio] stitched duration={total_dur:.2f}s  BGM fade-out starts at {fade_st:.2f}s",
+              file=sys.stderr)
+
+        # afade: fade out only the last 1.5s of BGM (st = absolute start of fade)
+        bgm_filter = (
+            f"[1:a]volume=-3dB,afade=t=out:st={fade_st:.3f}:d={fade_dur}[bgm];"
+            "[0:a][bgm]amix=inputs=2:duration=first:dropout_transition=1[aout]"
+        )
+        _run([
+            "ffmpeg", "-y",
+            "-i", str(stitched),        # 0: stitched video (Seedance VO in audio track)
+            "-i", bgm_path,             # 1: Demucs no_vocals BGM
+            "-filter_complex", bgm_filter,
+            "-map", "0:v",
+            "-map", "[aout]",
+            "-c:v", "copy",
+            "-c:a", "aac", "-b:a", "192k",
+            str(out_path),
+        ])
+    else:
+        # Fallback: keep Seedance VO audio only (no BGM available)
+        print("  [warn] No Demucs BGM found; keeping Seedance audio only.", file=sys.stderr)
+        _run([
+            "ffmpeg", "-y",
+            "-i", str(stitched),
+            "-map", "0:v",
+            "-map", "0:a",
+            "-c", "copy",
+            str(out_path),
+        ])
+
+    print(f"[concat] Done → {out_path}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Concat chunks + BGM mix")
+    ap.add_argument("--source",      required=True, help="Source reference video")
+    ap.add_argument("--chunks-dir",  required=True, help="Directory with chunk_NN.mp4 files")
+    ap.add_argument("--chunks-json", default=None,
+                    help="seedance_results.json path (auto-detected if omitted)")
+    ap.add_argument("--out",         required=True, help="Output mp4 path")
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    chunks_dir = Path(args.chunks_dir)
+
+    # Resolve chunk paths from json or glob
+    if args.chunks_json:
+        state = json.loads(Path(args.chunks_json).read_text())
+        raw = state.get("mp4_paths", {})
+        chunk_paths = [raw[k] for k in sorted(raw, key=int)]
+    else:
+        chunk_paths = [str(p) for p in sorted(chunks_dir.glob("chunk_*.mp4"))]
+
+    if not chunk_paths:
+        sys.exit(f"[concat] No chunk mp4s found in {chunks_dir}")
+
+    concat_and_mix(
+        source_video = Path(args.source),
+        chunk_paths  = chunk_paths,
+        out_path     = Path(args.out),
+    )
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

@@ -0,0 +1,242 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+# auto-caption: video/audio + zooclaw ASR + forced alignment → caption data.
+# Outputs JSON / SRT / VTT to stdout (or --output file).
+#
+# This script is a thin orchestrator. It calls sibling zooclaw skills as
+# scripts, runs ffmpeg silencedetect, and pipes everything through
+# postprocess.py for hallucination filtering and grouping.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+SKILL_DIR="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
+SKILLS_PARENT="$(cd "${SKILL_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
+
+ASR_SKILL_DIR="${ZOOCLAW_ASR_PATH:-${SKILLS_PARENT}/zooclaw-asr}"
+ALIGN_SKILL_DIR="${ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_PATH:-${SKILLS_PARENT}/zooclaw-forcedalign}"
+
+MAX_DURATION_S=200
+MAX_BYTES=$((50 * 1024 * 1024))   # 50 MiB
+
+# --- Defaults ---
+INPUT=""
+OUTPUT=""
+FORMAT="json"
+GROUP="none"
+LANGUAGE=""
+HOTWORDS=""
+KEEP_TEMP=0
+
+usage() {
+    cat >&2 <<EOF
+Usage: caption.sh [OPTIONS] <media>
+
+Arguments:
+  <media>                  Video/audio path or URL (also via -i).
+
+Options:
+  -i, --input <path|url>
+  -o, --output <path>
+  -f, --format <fmt>       json (default) | srt | vtt
+  -g, --group <preset>     none (default) | tiktok | subtitle
+  -l, --language <name>    e.g. Chinese, English (default: auto-detect)
+  -p, --hotwords <words>   Hotwords for ASR biasing
+      --keep-temp          Keep intermediate audio + JSON for debugging
+  -h, --help
+
+Supported video: mp4 mov mkv webm m4v avi  (auto-extracted to 16 kHz mono wav).
+Supported audio: wav mp3 flac ogg m4a.
+Hard limits:     duration <= ${MAX_DURATION_S}s, size <= 50 MiB.
+EOF
+    exit 1
+}
+
+POSITIONAL=()
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        -i|--input)      INPUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -o|--output)     OUTPUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -f|--format)     FORMAT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -g|--group)      GROUP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -l|--language)   LANGUAGE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -p|--hotwords)   HOTWORDS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        --keep-temp)     KEEP_TEMP=1; shift ;;
+        -h|--help)       usage ;;
+        -*)              echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; usage ;;
+        *)               POSITIONAL+=("$1"); shift ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+for arg in "${POSITIONAL[@]+"${POSITIONAL[@]}"}"; do
+    if [[ -z "$INPUT" ]]; then INPUT="$arg"
+    else echo "Unexpected extra argument: $arg" >&2; usage
+    fi
+done
+
+[[ -z "$INPUT" ]] && { echo "Error: no media input specified" >&2; usage; }
+
+case "$FORMAT" in json|srt|vtt) ;; *) echo "Error: unsupported --format '${FORMAT}'" >&2; exit 1 ;; esac
+case "$GROUP"  in none|tiktok|subtitle) ;; *) echo "Error: unsupported --group '${GROUP}'" >&2; exit 1 ;; esac
+
+# --- Prerequisite checks --------------------------------------------------
+for bin in curl ffmpeg ffprobe python3; do
+    command -v "$bin" >/dev/null || { echo "Error: '$bin' is required" >&2; exit 1; }
+done
+if [[ -z "${USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN env var is not set (required by zooclaw siblings)" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+if [[ ! -x "${ASR_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/transcribe.sh" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: zooclaw-asr not found at ${ASR_SKILL_DIR}" >&2
+    echo "       Set ZOOCLAW_ASR_PATH to override." >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+if [[ ! -x "${ALIGN_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/align.sh" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: zooclaw-forcedalign not found at ${ALIGN_SKILL_DIR}" >&2
+    echo "       Set ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_PATH to override." >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+# --- Cleanup management ---------------------------------------------------
+CLEANUP_FILES=()
+cleanup() {
+    [[ "$KEEP_TEMP" -eq 1 ]] && { echo "Kept temp files: ${CLEANUP_FILES[@]+"${CLEANUP_FILES[@]}"}" >&2; return; }
+    for f in "${CLEANUP_FILES[@]+"${CLEANUP_FILES[@]}"}"; do rm -rf "$f"; done
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+is_url() { [[ "$1" =~ ^https?:// ]]; }
+
+ext_of() {
+    local name="${1##*/}"
+    name="${name%%\?*}"
+    name="${name##*.}"
+    printf '%s' "$name" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'
+}
+
+VIDEO_EXTS="mp4|mov|mkv|webm|m4v|avi"
+AUDIO_EXTS="wav|mp3|flac|ogg|m4a"
+
+# --- Resolve input to a local file ---------------------------------------
+if is_url "$INPUT"; then
+    EXT=$(ext_of "$INPUT")
+    [[ ! "$EXT" =~ ^(${VIDEO_EXTS}|${AUDIO_EXTS})$ ]] && EXT="mp4"
+    TMP_MEDIA=$(mktemp -u /tmp/auto_caption.XXXXXX).${EXT}
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$TMP_MEDIA")
+    echo "Downloading media..." >&2
+    curl -sSL "$INPUT" -o "$TMP_MEDIA" || { echo "Error: download failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+    MEDIA_PATH="$TMP_MEDIA"
+elif [[ -f "$INPUT" ]]; then
+    MEDIA_PATH="$INPUT"
+else
+    echo "Error: file not found: $INPUT" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+# --- Extract 16 kHz mono wav (skip if input is already audio) ------------
+HAS_VIDEO=0
+ORIENTATION="unknown"
+if ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=codec_type \
+        -of csv=p=0 "$MEDIA_PATH" 2>/dev/null | grep -q video; then
+    HAS_VIDEO=1
+    # Detect orientation — informs aspect-aware caption pagination in postprocess.
+    VDIMS=$(ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=width,height \
+            -of csv=p=0 "$MEDIA_PATH" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
+    VW=${VDIMS%,*}
+    VH=${VDIMS#*,}
+    if [[ -n "$VW" && -n "$VH" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$VH" -gt "$VW" ]]; then ORIENTATION="vertical"
+        elif [[ "$VW" -gt "$VH" ]]; then ORIENTATION="horizontal"
+        else ORIENTATION="square"
+        fi
+    fi
+fi
+
+# Use mp3 (128k) instead of pcm_s16le wav to avoid HTTP 413 on forced-align upload.
+# 16kHz pcm wav = ~1.1MB/35s which exceeds the nginx upload limit;
+# 128k mp3 = ~560KB/35s, well within limits.
+if [[ "$HAS_VIDEO" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    AUDIO_PATH=$(mktemp -u /tmp/auto_caption_audio.XXXXXX).mp3
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$AUDIO_PATH")
+    echo "Extracting audio with ffmpeg..." >&2
+    ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
+        -i "$MEDIA_PATH" -vn -ac 1 -ar 16000 -c:a libmp3lame -ab 128k "$AUDIO_PATH" \
+        || { echo "Error: ffmpeg failed to extract audio" >&2; exit 1; }
+else
+    EXT=$(ext_of "$MEDIA_PATH")
+    if [[ "$EXT" == "mp3" ]]; then
+        AUDIO_PATH="$MEDIA_PATH"
+    else
+        # Convert non-mp3 audio to 16kHz mono mp3 for consistent small file size
+        AUDIO_PATH=$(mktemp -u /tmp/auto_caption_audio.XXXXXX).mp3
+        CLEANUP_FILES+=("$AUDIO_PATH")
+        echo "Resampling audio to 16 kHz mono mp3..." >&2
+        ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
+            -i "$MEDIA_PATH" -vn -ac 1 -ar 16000 -c:a libmp3lame -ab 128k "$AUDIO_PATH" \
+            || { echo "Error: ffmpeg failed to resample audio" >&2; exit 1; }
+    fi
+fi
+
+# --- Sanity checks (size + duration) -------------------------------------
+DURATION=$(ffprobe -v quiet -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$AUDIO_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+[[ -z "$DURATION" ]] && DURATION=0
+SIZE=$(stat -f%z "$AUDIO_PATH" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$AUDIO_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
+
+[[ "$SIZE" -eq 0 ]] && { echo "Error: extracted audio is empty" >&2; exit 1; }
+[[ "$SIZE" -gt "$MAX_BYTES" ]] && { echo "Error: audio size ${SIZE}B exceeds 50 MiB" >&2; exit 1; }
+if awk -v d="$DURATION" -v m="$MAX_DURATION_S" 'BEGIN{exit (d+0 > m+0) ? 0 : 1}'; then
+    echo "Error: audio duration ${DURATION}s exceeds limit ${MAX_DURATION_S}s" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+DURATION_MS=$(python3 -c "print(int(float('${DURATION}') * 1000))")
+
+# --- Step 1: ASR (audio -> transcript text) ------------------------------
+echo "[1/3] Transcribing with zooclaw-asr..." >&2
+ASR_ARGS=("$AUDIO_PATH" "-f" "text")
+[[ -n "$HOTWORDS" ]] && ASR_ARGS+=("-p" "$HOTWORDS")
+TRANSCRIPT=$(bash "${ASR_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/transcribe.sh" "${ASR_ARGS[@]}") \
+    || { echo "Error: zooclaw-asr failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+if [[ -z "${TRANSCRIPT//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: ASR returned empty transcript" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+# --- Step 2: Forced alignment (audio + text -> per-word timestamps) -------
+# NOTE: ecap-proxy /v1/audio/align returns 404 for standard tokens.
+# The correct gateway for forced-align is forced-align.g.yesy.online.
+echo "[2/3] Aligning with zooclaw-forcedalign..." >&2
+ALIGN_ARGS=("$AUDIO_PATH" "$TRANSCRIPT" "-f" "json")
+[[ -n "$LANGUAGE" ]] && ALIGN_ARGS+=("-l" "$LANGUAGE")
+ALIGNMENT_JSON=$(ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL="${ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL:-https://forced-align.g.yesy.online}" \
+    bash "${ALIGN_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/align.sh" "${ALIGN_ARGS[@]}") \
+    || { echo "Error: zooclaw-forcedalign failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+# --- Step 2.5: Silence detection (for hallucination + grouping heuristics) -
+SILENCE_LOG=$(ffmpeg -i "$AUDIO_PATH" -af silencedetect=noise=-30dB:d=0.15 -f null - 2>&1 \
+    | grep -E "silence_(start|end)" || true)
+
+# --- Step 3: Post-process (filter + group + render) -----------------------
+echo "[3/3] Post-processing (filter + group=${GROUP} format=${FORMAT})..." >&2
+
+RESULT=$(
+    ALIGNMENT_JSON="$ALIGNMENT_JSON" \
+    SILENCE_LOG="$SILENCE_LOG" \
+    GROUP="$GROUP" \
+    FORMAT="$FORMAT" \
+    DURATION_MS="$DURATION_MS" \
+    ORIENTATION="$ORIENTATION" \
+    python3 "${SCRIPT_DIR}/postprocess.py"
+)
+
+if [[ -z "$RESULT" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: post-process produced empty output" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+if [[ -n "$OUTPUT" ]]; then
+    printf '%s\n' "$RESULT" > "$OUTPUT"
+    echo "Saved: ${OUTPUT}" >&2
+else
+    printf '%s\n' "$RESULT"
+fi

+"""
+Post-process forced-alignment output:
+  - convert seconds -> ms
+  - drop ASR hallucinations (common end-of-clip filler phrases sitting in silent regions)
+  - strip phantom punctuation (text-level punct with no audible pause)
+  - normalize numbers, group items into pages per GROUP preset
+  - render JSON / SRT / VTT
+
+Inputs (via env vars set by caption.sh):
+  ALIGNMENT_JSON  raw JSON from zooclaw-forcedalign
+  SILENCE_LOG     ffmpeg silencedetect log lines (may be empty)
+  GROUP           "none" | "tiktok" | "subtitle"
+  FORMAT          "json" | "srt" | "vtt"
+  DURATION_MS     total audio duration in ms (string)
+  ORIENTATION     "vertical" | "horizontal" | "square" | "unknown"
+
+Segmentation rules (in pipeline order, see founder-post/SKILL.md for full
+table with examples):
+
+  1. merge_cn_numbers      — Chinese numerals → Arabic ('十一' → '11')
+  2. Measure-word blacklist — 倍/次/遍/位/成/番/轮/分 keep preceding number
+                              in Chinese AND fold the measure word into one
+                              item ('一百倍' stays as one phrase)
+  3. merge_decimal_points  — (N, '点', N) → "N.N" ('十一点十二' → '11.12')
+  4. AABB look-ahead       — never split a 4-char AABB idiom (心心念念) across pages
+  5. Brand+model unit      — multi-digit number after a Latin run peels the
+                              Latin run forward so brand+model stay together
+                              ('Chanel Classic 11.12' never splits)
+  6. Sentence-starter rule — items starting with 哦/啊/哇/嗯/呃 begin a new
+                              page when current page has substantive content
+  7. Prosody soft break    — gap ≥120ms forces a break (forced-align bound
+                              words give 0ms gaps, so any real gap = phrase
+                              boundary, even without punctuation)
+  8. Width cap             — per-orientation hard cap (vertical=26,
+                              horizontal=56, square=36 visual width units)
+  9. Max page duration     — 4500ms safety net for the tiktok preset
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import json
+import os
+import re
+import sys
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Configuration (in ms)
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+PHANTOM_PUNCT_GAP_MS = 150            # punct removed if gap to next < this
+HALLUCINATION_SILENCE_MS = 800        # if a phrase sits inside silence > this, drop it
+
+# Aspect-aware page-width caps, expressed in "visual width units" where a CJK
+# character counts as 2 (it occupies ~2x the rendered width of a Latin char in
+# typical bold sans-serif caption typography). Vertical videos have very limited
+# width so need tighter caps; horizontal videos can fit much more.
+GROUP_PRESETS = {
+    "tiktok": {
+        "hard_break_ms": 500, "soft_break_ms": 120,
+        # Forced-align typically returns 0-ms gaps between consecutive items;
+        # any non-trivial gap (~120ms+) is a real speech pause and a strong
+        # phrase-boundary signal even WITHOUT punctuation. We use it as a
+        # soft break when the page already has substantive content.
+        "soft_break_re": r"[,.!?;:，。！？；：]$",
+        # Only fire for "disaster" runs (ASR returns no punctuation + no silence).
+        # Forcing breaks at arbitrary indices chops words like "时间" → "时" / "间",
+        # so keep these loose-but-not-loose-enough-to-cause-overflow.
+        # Vertical raised 22 → 26 so semantic units like "Chanel Classic 11.12"
+        # and "好看一百倍" don't get split across pages.
+        "max_page_ms": 4500,
+        "max_page_width": {
+            "vertical":   26,
+            "horizontal": 56,
+            "square":     36,
+            "unknown":    40,
+        },
+    },
+    "subtitle": {
+        "hard_break_ms": 800, "soft_break_ms": 200,
+        "soft_break_re": r"[.!?。！？]$",
+        "max_page_ms": 8000,
+        "max_page_width": {
+            "vertical":   40,
+            "horizontal": 96,
+            "square":     60,
+            "unknown":    72,
+        },
+    },
+}
+
+PUNCT_RE = re.compile(
+    r"^["
+    r",.!?;:，。！？；：、…—\-·"
+    r"\"'“”‘’"
+    r"()\[\]{}（）【】《》「」『』"
+    r"]+$"
+)
+
+# Char is CJK / Korean / fullwidth — neighbors don't need a space.
+_CJK_RE = re.compile(
+    r"[　-〿぀-鿿가-힯＀-￯]"
+)
+
+# Chinese exclamation / phrase-start particles. Items beginning with these
+# almost always start a new utterance — break before them rather than gluing
+# them to the prior phrase. Kept conservative to avoid false positives.
+SENTENCE_STARTERS = set("哦啊哇嗯呃")
+
+
+def _is_sticky_number(text: str) -> bool:
+    """True if text is a multi-digit number / decimal — should attach to a
+    preceding Latin name as a brand-model unit (Chanel 11.12, iPhone 15, etc.)."""
+    if not text:
+        return False
+    bare = text.replace(".", "").replace("-", "")
+    return bare.isdigit() and len(bare) >= 2
+
+
+def _trailing_latin_run_start(items: list[dict], current_idx: list[int]) -> int | None:
+    """If the current page ends with a run of Latin/non-CJK items, return the
+    POSITION in current_idx where that run starts. None if the last item is
+    CJK (no trailing Latin run to split off)."""
+    if not current_idx:
+        return None
+    last_text = items[current_idx[-1]]["text"]
+    if not last_text or _CJK_RE.match(last_text):
+        return None
+    pos = len(current_idx) - 1
+    while pos > 0:
+        prev_text = items[current_idx[pos - 1]]["text"]
+        if not prev_text or _CJK_RE.match(prev_text):
+            break
+        pos -= 1
+    return pos
+
+
+def _starts_aabb(items: list[dict], i: int) -> bool:
+    """True if items[i..i+3] forms a Chinese AABB reduplication idiom
+    (心心念念, 干干净净, 想来想去, etc.). All four items must be single CJK
+    characters with pattern A,A,B,B where A≠B. Such phrases should never be
+    split across pages — they're a lexical unit."""
+    if i + 3 >= len(items):
+        return False
+    texts = [items[i + k]["text"] for k in range(4)]
+    if any(len(t) != 1 for t in texts):
+        return False
+    if not all(_CJK_RE.match(t) for t in texts):
+        return False
+    return texts[0] == texts[1] and texts[2] == texts[3] and texts[0] != texts[2]
+
+
+def visual_width(text: str) -> int:
+    """Approximate the rendered width of a text run, in "Latin-char units".
+    CJK / fullwidth chars are ~2x wider than typical bold Latin in caption fonts."""
+    cjk = sum(1 for c in text if _CJK_RE.match(c))
+    return cjk * 2 + (len(text) - cjk)
+
+
+def join_word_texts(texts: list[str]) -> str:
+    """Concatenate word texts, inserting a space only between adjacent
+    Latin/digit boundaries. CJK chars stay glued."""
+    out: list[str] = []
+    for t in texts:
+        if not t:
+            continue
+        if out and not _CJK_RE.match(out[-1][-1]) and not _CJK_RE.match(t[0]):
+            out.append(" ")
+        out.append(t)
+    return "".join(out)
+
+# Common end-of-clip hallucinations the ASR latches onto when fed trailing
+# silence. Matched case-insensitively against the trimmed text of a page.
+HALLUCINATION_PATTERNS = [
+    re.compile(r"^谢谢(大家|观看|收看|大家观看)?[。.!！]?$"),
+    re.compile(r"^謝謝(大家|觀看|收看|大家觀看)?[。.!！]?$"),
+    re.compile(r"^感谢观看[。.!！]?$"),
+    re.compile(r"^thanks?\s+for\s+watching[.!]?$", re.IGNORECASE),
+    re.compile(r"^please\s+subscribe[.!]?$", re.IGNORECASE),
+    re.compile(r"^字幕(由|提供|.*志愿者).*$"),
+]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Helpers
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+def parse_silences(log: str) -> list[dict]:
+    """Pair silence_start/silence_end lines into [{start_ms, end_ms, duration_ms}]."""
+    starts = [float(m.group(1)) for m in re.finditer(r"silence_start: ([\d.]+)", log)]
+    end_pairs = re.findall(r"silence_end: ([\d.]+) \| silence_duration: ([\d.]+)", log)
+    silences = []
+    for i in range(min(len(starts), len(end_pairs))):
+        end_s, dur_s = end_pairs[i]
+        silences.append(
+            {
+                "start_ms": int(starts[i] * 1000),
+                "end_ms": int(float(end_s) * 1000),
+                "duration_ms": int(float(dur_s) * 1000),
+            }
+        )
+    return silences
+
+
+def to_items(alignment: dict) -> list[dict]:
+    """Convert forced-alignment items (seconds, possibly empty text) to ms items."""
+    out = []
+    for raw in alignment.get("items", []):
+        text = raw.get("text", "")
+        if not text or not text.strip():
+            continue
+        out.append(
+            {
+                "text": text,
+                "start_ms": int(round(raw["start_time"] * 1000)),
+                "end_ms": int(round(raw["end_time"] * 1000)),
+            }
+        )
+    return out
+
+
+def strip_phantom_punct(items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    """Drop trailing punctuation where the gap to next item is too short to be a real pause."""
+    for k in range(len(items) - 1):
+        gap = items[k + 1]["start_ms"] - items[k]["end_ms"]
+        if gap < PHANTOM_PUNCT_GAP_MS:
+            text = items[k]["text"]
+            while text and PUNCT_RE.match(text[-1]):
+                text = text[:-1]
+            items[k]["text"] = text
+    return [it for it in items if it["text"].strip()]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Chinese numeral → Arabic digit conversion
+# ASR returns Chinese digits character-by-character (十/一/点/十/二). We merge
+# consecutive Chinese-numeric items and convert to Arabic so captions read
+# "11点12" instead of "十一点十二" — visually clearer in short-form video.
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+_CN_DIGITS = {"零": 0, "〇": 0, "一": 1, "二": 2, "两": 2, "三": 3, "四": 4,
+              "五": 5, "六": 6, "七": 7, "八": 8, "九": 9}
+_CN_UNITS = {"十": 10, "百": 100, "千": 1000}
+_CN_NUM_CHARS = set(_CN_DIGITS) | set(_CN_UNITS) | {"万", "亿"}
+
+# Approximate-number Chinese idioms: two consecutive digit chars with no unit
+# between them, expressing a range/fuzzy quantity (e.g. "两三万" = "20k–30k" range,
+# "五六个" = "5 or 6"). No single Arabic digit can represent these accurately, so
+# we refuse the Arabicization and let the original Chinese chars survive into
+# the rendered subtitle. Trust the ASR's lexical output; do not over-process.
+_APPROX_DIGIT_PAIRS = {
+    "一两", "两三", "三四", "四五", "五六", "六七", "七八", "八九",
+}
+
+
+def _has_approximate_idiom(s: str) -> bool:
+    return any(p in s for p in _APPROX_DIGIT_PAIRS)
+
+
+def _cn_to_arabic(s: str) -> int | None:
+    """Convert a pure-Chinese-numeral string to an int. Returns None on any non-numeric input
+    OR on detected approximate-number idioms (which have no single Arabic equivalent).
+    Handles up to 亿 by recursing on the 万/亿 split."""
+    if not s or any(c not in _CN_NUM_CHARS for c in s):
+        return None
+    if _has_approximate_idiom(s):
+        return None
+    if "亿" in s:
+        hi, _, lo = s.partition("亿")
+        hi_v = _cn_to_arabic(hi) if hi else 1
+        lo_v = _cn_to_arabic(lo) if lo else 0
+        return None if hi_v is None or lo_v is None else hi_v * 100_000_000 + lo_v
+    if "万" in s:
+        hi, _, lo = s.partition("万")
+        hi_v = _cn_to_arabic(hi) if hi else 1
+        lo_v = _cn_to_arabic(lo) if lo else 0
+        return None if hi_v is None or lo_v is None else hi_v * 10_000 + lo_v
+    total = 0
+    current = 0
+    for c in s:
+        if c in _CN_DIGITS:
+            current = _CN_DIGITS[c]
+        elif c in _CN_UNITS:
+            unit = _CN_UNITS[c]
+            if current == 0:
+                current = 1  # 十 alone = 10
+            total += current * unit
+            current = 0
+    return total + current
+
+
+def merge_decimal_points(items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    """Merge a (digit-item, '点', digit-item) triple into a single decimal item:
+    '11', '点', '12' -> '11.12'. Common pattern in product names spoken in Chinese
+    (e.g. "Chanel Classic 十一点十二" -> "Chanel Classic 11.12")."""
+    out: list[dict] = []
+    i = 0
+    n = len(items)
+    while i < n:
+        if (
+            i + 2 < n
+            and items[i]["text"].isdigit()
+            and items[i + 1]["text"] == "点"
+            and items[i + 2]["text"].isdigit()
+        ):
+            out.append({
+                "text": f"{items[i]['text']}.{items[i + 2]['text']}",
+                "start_ms": items[i]["start_ms"],
+                "end_ms": items[i + 2]["end_ms"],
+            })
+            i += 3
+            continue
+        out.append(items[i])
+        i += 1
+    return out
+
+
+# Measure words / qualifiers where Chinese numerals are idiomatic and should
+# NOT be converted to Arabic ("一百倍" stays Chinese; "11.12" still converts
+# because Chanel-model-number is not followed by these). Keep this list
+# conservative — qualifiers like 年/月/日/岁 are commonly Arabicized in modern
+# usage (e.g. "30岁", "2024年") so they're intentionally not included.
+_KEEP_CN_AFTER = set("倍次遍位成番轮分")
+
+
+def merge_cn_numbers(items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    """Merge consecutive items whose text consists entirely of Chinese numeral
+    characters. Default: convert to Arabic digits. Exception: if the next item
+    starts with a measure word from _KEEP_CN_AFTER, keep the phrase in Chinese
+    and fold the measure word into the same item so pagination doesn't split it."""
+    out: list[dict] = []
+    i = 0
+    n = len(items)
+    while i < n:
+        j = i
+        while j < n and items[j]["text"] and all(c in _CN_NUM_CHARS for c in items[j]["text"]):
+            j += 1
+        if j > i:
+            combined = "".join(items[k]["text"] for k in range(i, j))
+            # Check if the next item starts with an idiomatic measure word.
+            keep_cn = (
+                j < n
+                and items[j]["text"]
+                and items[j]["text"][0] in _KEEP_CN_AFTER
+            )
+            if keep_cn:
+                # Don't Arabicize. Fold the numeral run AND the measure-word
+                # item into a single phrase so it stays together.
+                out.append({
+                    "text": combined + items[j]["text"],
+                    "start_ms": items[i]["start_ms"],
+                    "end_ms": items[j]["end_ms"],
+                })
+                i = j + 1
+                continue
+            arabic = _cn_to_arabic(combined)
+            # Only fold if conversion succeeded AND we actually merged ≥1 chars.
+            # Skip single-char items that are ambiguous (e.g. "一" said as 'a').
+            if arabic is not None and (j - i > 1 or len(combined) > 1):
+                out.append({
+                    "text": str(arabic),
+                    "start_ms": items[i]["start_ms"],
+                    "end_ms": items[j - 1]["end_ms"],
+                })
+                i = j
+                continue
+            # arabic is None on multi-char run (e.g. approximate idiom like 两三万).
+            # Keep ALL chars in the run as separate items — do NOT advance by 1
+            # and retry, which would let a sub-sequence (e.g. "三万") sneak through
+            # the next iteration and get Arabicized, producing "两30000".
+            if j - i > 1:
+                for k in range(i, j):
+                    out.append(items[k])
+                i = j
+                continue
+        out.append(items[i])
+        i += 1
+    return out
+
+
+def drop_hallucinations(items: list[dict], silences: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    """Trim trailing items whose text matches a known hallucination AND whose
+    start sits inside a long silent region.
+    """
+    if not items:
+        return items
+    long_silences = [s for s in silences if s["duration_ms"] >= HALLUCINATION_SILENCE_MS]
+
+    kept = list(items)
+    # Walk from the end; once we hit a real (non-hallucinated) item, stop.
+    while kept:
+        last = kept[-1]
+        text = last["text"].strip()
+        is_hallucination = any(p.match(text) for p in HALLUCINATION_PATTERNS)
+        sits_in_silence = any(
+            sil["start_ms"] - 100 <= last["start_ms"] <= sil["end_ms"]
+            or sil["start_ms"] <= last["start_ms"] and last["end_ms"] <= sil["end_ms"]
+            for sil in long_silences
+        )
+        if is_hallucination and (sits_in_silence or len(kept) <= 3):
+            kept.pop()
+        else:
+            break
+    return kept
+
+
+def group_items(items: list[dict], preset_name: str, orientation: str = "unknown") -> list[dict]:
+    """Walk items and group into pages per the named preset.
+
+    `orientation` ("vertical" / "horizontal" / "square" / "unknown") picks a
+    tighter or looser max page width — vertical videos have narrow displays
+    and can't fit nearly as much text on one line.
+    """
+    if preset_name == "none" or not items:
+        return []

+    soft_ms = preset["soft_break_ms"]

+    width_table = preset.get("max_page_width", {})
+    max_page_width = width_table.get(orientation, width_table.get("unknown", 10**9))
+
+    pages = []
+    current_idx: list[int] = []
+    page_start_ms = 0
+    page_width = 0
+
+    def flush(end_idx: int) -> None:
+        nonlocal current_idx, page_width
+        if not current_idx:
+            return
+        text = join_word_texts([items[j]["text"] for j in current_idx])
+        pages.append(
+            {
+                "start_ms": page_start_ms,
+                "end_ms": items[current_idx[-1]]["end_ms"],
+                "text": text,
+                "item_indices": list(current_idx),
+            }
+        )
+        current_idx = []
+        page_width = 0
+
+    def split_at(pos: int) -> None:
+        """Emit current_idx[0..pos-1] as a page; keep current_idx[pos..] as
+        the new current page. Used by the brand+model rule to peel off a
+        trailing Latin run so it stays attached to a following model number."""
+        nonlocal current_idx, page_width, page_start_ms
+        if pos <= 0 or pos >= len(current_idx):
+            return
+        first, rest = current_idx[:pos], current_idx[pos:]
+        text = join_word_texts([items[j]["text"] for j in first])
+        pages.append(
+            {
+                "start_ms": page_start_ms,
+                "end_ms": items[first[-1]]["end_ms"],
+                "text": text,
+                "item_indices": first,
+            }
+        )
+        current_idx = rest
+        page_width = sum(visual_width(items[j]["text"]) for j in current_idx)
+        page_start_ms = items[current_idx[0]]["start_ms"]
+
+    for i, item in enumerate(items):
+        item_w = visual_width(item["text"])
+        # Sentence-starter heuristic: items beginning with these Chinese
+        # exclamation chars are nearly always the start of a new utterance
+        # ("哦我的天", "啊真的吗"). Force a flush BEFORE them when the current
+        # page already has substantive content (≥4 width units = 2 Chinese
+        # chars). Prevents the prior phrase from absorbing the exclamation
+        # just because the width cap allowed it.
+        starts_new_sentence = bool(item["text"]) and item["text"][0] in SENTENCE_STARTERS
+        # AABB look-ahead: if this item starts a 4-char reduplication idiom AND
+        # adding the whole idiom would overflow the page, flush BEFORE the
+        # idiom so it stays whole on the next page. Lets "心心念念的" stay as
+        # one unit even when the natural width cut would split it mid-idiom.
+        aabb_overflow = False
+        if current_idx and _starts_aabb(items, i):
+            aabb_w = sum(visual_width(items[i + k]["text"]) for k in range(4))
+            if page_width + aabb_w > max_page_width:
+                aabb_overflow = True
+        # Brand+model unit: if this item is a multi-digit number AND adding it
+        # would overflow AND the page ends with a Latin run (brand name like
+        # "Chanel Classic"), peel the Latin run off so brand+model stay
+        # together on the next page.
+        if (
+            current_idx
+            and page_width + item_w > max_page_width
+            and _is_sticky_number(item["text"])
+        ):
+            latin_pos = _trailing_latin_run_start(items, current_idx)
+            if latin_pos is not None and latin_pos > 0:
+                split_at(latin_pos)
+        force_flush = (
+            current_idx and (
+                page_width + item_w > max_page_width
+                or (starts_new_sentence and page_width >= 4)
+                or aabb_overflow
+            )
+        )
+        if force_flush:
+            flush(i - 1)
+        if not current_idx:
+            page_start_ms = item["start_ms"]
+        current_idx.append(i)
+        page_width += item_w
+        page_duration = item["end_ms"] - page_start_ms
+        gap = items[i + 1]["start_ms"] - item["end_ms"] if i + 1 < len(items) else 10**9
+        ends_with_break_punct = bool(soft_re.search(item["text"]))
+        # Break logic (any of):
+        #   hard_break: pause ≥ hard_ms (always)
+        #   punct soft break: pause ≥ soft_ms AND item ends with punctuation
+        #   prosody soft break: pause ≥ soft_ms AND page has ≥4 width (≥2 chars)
+        #                       — uses raw pause as a phrase-boundary cue when
+        #                       ASR didn't emit punctuation
+        #   max-duration: page got too long
+        if (
+            gap >= hard_ms
+            or (gap >= soft_ms and (ends_with_break_punct or page_width >= 4))
+            or page_duration >= max_page_ms
+        ):
+            flush(i)
+    flush(len(items) - 1)
+    return pages
+
+
+def fmt_ts(ms: int, sep: str) -> str:
+    if ms < 0:
+        ms = 0
+    h, rem = divmod(ms, 3600_000)
+    m, rem = divmod(rem, 60_000)
+    s, ms2 = divmod(rem, 1000)
+    return f"{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}{sep}{ms2:03d}"
+
+
+def render_json(items, pages, language, duration_ms):
+    payload: dict = {
+        "language": language,
+        "duration_ms": duration_ms,
+        "items": [
+            {"text": it["text"], "start_ms": it["start_ms"], "end_ms": it["end_ms"]}
+            for it in items
+        ],
+    }
+    if pages:
+        payload["pages"] = pages
+    return json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
+
+
+def render_srt(units: list[dict]) -> str:
+    out = []
+    for idx, u in enumerate(units, 1):
+        out.append(str(idx))
+        out.append(f"{fmt_ts(u['start_ms'], ',')} --> {fmt_ts(u['end_ms'], ',')}")
+        out.append(u["text"])
+        out.append("")
+    return "\n".join(out).rstrip()
+
+
+def render_vtt(units: list[dict]) -> str:
+    out = ["WEBVTT", ""]
+    for u in units:
+        out.append(f"{fmt_ts(u['start_ms'], '.')} --> {fmt_ts(u['end_ms'], '.')}")
+        out.append(u["text"])
+        out.append("")
+    return "\n".join(out).rstrip()
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Main
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+def main() -> int:
+    alignment = json.loads(os.environ["ALIGNMENT_JSON"])
+    silence_log = os.environ.get("SILENCE_LOG", "")
+    group = os.environ.get("GROUP", "none")
+    fmt = os.environ.get("FORMAT", "json")
+    duration_ms = int(os.environ.get("DURATION_MS", "0"))
+    orientation = os.environ.get("ORIENTATION", "unknown")
+
+    # SRT/VTT default to phrase-level grouping; otherwise we'd emit one entry
+    # per word which is nearly unusable as a subtitle file.
+    effective_group = group
+    if fmt in ("srt", "vtt") and group == "none":
+        print(
+            "Note: --format srt/vtt with --group none auto-promoted to --group subtitle",
+            file=sys.stderr,
+        )
+        effective_group = "subtitle"

+    items = strip_phantom_punct(items)
+    items = drop_hallucinations(items, silences)
+    items = merge_cn_numbers(items)
+    items = merge_decimal_points(items)
+    pages = group_items(items, effective_group, orientation)
+    language = alignment.get("language") or "auto"
+
+    if fmt == "json":
+        print(render_json(items, pages, language, duration_ms))
+    elif fmt in ("srt", "vtt"):
+        units = pages if pages else items
+        units_for_subtitle = [
+            {"start_ms": u["start_ms"], "end_ms": u["end_ms"], "text": u["text"]}
+            for u in units
+        ]
+        renderer = render_srt if fmt == "srt" else render_vtt
+        print(renderer(units_for_subtitle))
+    else:
+        print(f"Error: unknown format {fmt!r}", file=sys.stderr)
+        return 1
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    sys.exit(main())

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""video-editor: subtitle_burn — ASS dual-layer subtitle generation and burn.
+
+Spec (v1.3.4 canonical):
+  - Font:     Noto Sans SC Bold, 70pt
+  - MarginV:  int(video_height × 0.30)
+  - Shadow:   dual-layer (Shad + Text styles)
+    - Layer 0 Shad: \\blur2, offset (-2.12, +2.12) = 45°, distance ≈ 3px
+    - Layer 1 Text: clean white at base position
+  - Outline:  0 (disables libass built-in shadow engine)
+  - Line-breaking: semantic-aware, ≤10 chars per line, orphan rebalance
+
+Input:
+    --video      Video to burn subtitles into
+    --captions   JSON file with captions array: [{"start": s, "end": s, "text": "..."}]
+                 OR pass --plan to read captions from generation_plan.json
+    --out        Output mp4 path
+
+Output:
+    <out>                  Final video with subtitles burned in
+    <out-dir>/captions.ass ASS subtitle file
+
+Usage:
+    python3 subtitle_burn.py \\
+        --video   runs/run-01/stitched_with_bgm.mp4 \\
+        --captions runs/run-01/captions.json \\
+        --out     runs/run-01/final_with_subtitles.mp4
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+import math
+import re
+import subprocess
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Timing helpers
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def ms_to_ass(ms: int) -> str:
+    h, r = divmod(ms, 3_600_000)
+    m, r = divmod(r, 60_000)
+    s, f = divmod(r, 1_000)
+    return f"{h:d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}.{f // 10:02d}"
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Semantic line-breaking
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+_PUNCT_SPLIT = re.compile(r'[，。！？、；：…,!?]')
+# Natural break preference: after these function words
+_BREAK_AFTER = set('的地得了着过也都还是就又再被把不无具有可以将从到且而对于除并')
+
+# ── v2 spec: max 8 chars per line, one line at a time ──────────────────────
+MAX_CHARS = 8
+
+
+def _split_to_lines(text: str) -> list[str]:
+    """Split text into lines of ≤ MAX_CHARS each, using natural boundaries.
+
+    Rules (in priority order):
+    1. Split at Chinese punctuation first
+    2. Then prefer splitting after semantic boundary words
+    3. Hard split at MAX_CHARS if no boundary found
+    4. Orphan rebalance: if last segment ≤ 2 chars, rebalance whole text
+    """
+    text = text.strip()
+    if not text:
+        return []
+
+    # Step 1: pre-split at punctuation
+    parts = [p.strip() for p in _PUNCT_SPLIT.split(text) if p.strip()]
+    if not parts:
+        return []
+
+    lines: list[str] = []
+    for part in parts:
+        # Step 2+3: split each part into ≤ MAX_CHARS chunks
+        while len(part) > MAX_CHARS:
+            cut = MAX_CHARS
+            # Try to find a semantic boundary at or before MAX_CHARS
+            for i in range(cut, max(cut - 4, 1), -1):
+                if part[i - 1] in _BREAK_AFTER:
+                    cut = i
+                    break
+            lines.append(part[:cut])
+            part = part[cut:]
+        if part:
+            lines.append(part)
+
+    # Step 4: orphan rebalance — if last line ≤ 2 chars, merge back and re-split
+    if lines and len(lines[-1]) <= 2 and len(lines) >= 2:
+        combined = ''.join(lines[-2:])
+        lines = lines[:-2]
+        half = math.ceil(len(combined) / 2)
+        lines.extend([combined[:half], combined[half:]])
+
+    return [l for l in lines if l]
+
+
+def split_caption(cap: dict) -> list[dict]:
+    """Split one caption into single-line segments, each ≤ MAX_CHARS.
+
+    Each output segment has its own proportional time window so only
+    ONE line is visible at any time.
+    """
+    raw         = cap["text"].replace("{", "").replace("}", "")
+    raw         = raw.replace("\u3000", "").replace(" ", "").strip()  # strip spaces — they count as chars but add no value in CJK subtitles
+    start_ms    = cap.get("start_ms") or int(float(cap.get("start", 0)) * 1000)
+    end_ms      = cap.get("end_ms")   or int(float(cap.get("end",   0)) * 1000)
+    duration_ms = end_ms - start_ms
+
+    lines = _split_to_lines(raw)
+    if not lines:
+        return []
+
+    total_chars = sum(len(l) for l in lines) or 1
+    result: list[dict] = []
+    cursor = start_ms
+
+    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
+        seg_dur = int(duration_ms * len(line) / total_chars)
+        s_ms    = cursor
+        e_ms    = cursor + seg_dur if i < len(lines) - 1 else end_ms
+        # Guarantee minimum 300ms per line (readability floor)
+        if e_ms - s_ms < 300 and i < len(lines) - 1:
+            e_ms = s_ms + 300
+        result.append({"start_ms": s_ms, "end_ms": e_ms, "text": line})
+        cursor = e_ms
+
+    return result
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# ASS generation
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def build_ass(captions: list[dict], vid_w: int, vid_h: int) -> str:
+    margin_v = int(vid_h * 0.30)
+    base_x   = vid_w / 2
+    base_y   = vid_h - margin_v
+    shad_dx  = -2.12
+    shad_dy  =  2.12
+
+    style_format = (
+        "Format: Name, Fontname, Fontsize, PrimaryColour, SecondaryColour, OutlineColour, "
+        "BackColour, Bold, Italic, Underline, StrikeOut, ScaleX, ScaleY, Spacing, Angle, "
+        "BorderStyle, Outline, Shadow, Alignment, MarginL, MarginR, MarginV, Encoding"
+    )
+    style_shad = (
+        f"Style: Shad,Noto Sans SC,70,"
+        f"&H80000000,&H000000FF,&H00000000,&H00000000,"
+        f"1,0,0,0,100,100,0,0,1,0,0,2,10,10,{margin_v},1"
+    )
+    style_text = (
+        f"Style: Text,Noto Sans SC,70,"
+        "&H00FFFFFF,&H000000FF,&H00000000,&H00000000,"
+        f"1,0,0,0,100,100,0,0,1,0,0,2,10,10,{margin_v},1"
+    )
+
+    lines = [
+        "[Script Info]",
+        "ScriptType: v4.00+",
+        f"PlayResX: {vid_w}",
+        f"PlayResY: {vid_h}",
+        "WrapStyle: 0",
+        "",
+        "[V4+ Styles]",
+        style_format,
+        style_shad,
+        style_text,
+        "",
+        "[Events]",
+        "Format: Layer, Start, End, Style, Name, MarginL, MarginR, MarginV, Effect, Text",
+    ]
+
+    # Expand and sort all caption segments
+    split_caps: list[dict] = []
+    for cap in captions:
+        split_caps.extend(split_caption(cap))
+    split_caps.sort(key=lambda c: c["start_ms"])
+
+    for cap in split_caps:
+        t0  = ms_to_ass(cap["start_ms"])
+        t1  = ms_to_ass(cap["end_ms"])
+        txt = cap["text"]
+        sx  = base_x + shad_dx
+        sy  = base_y + shad_dy
+
+        # String concat to avoid Python escape interpretation of \a \b \p etc.
+        shad_tag = "{" + "\\an2" + "\\pos(" + f"{sx:.1f},{sy:.1f}" + ")" + "\\blur2" + "}"
+        text_tag = "{" + "\\an2" + "\\pos(" + f"{base_x:.1f},{base_y:.1f}" + ")" + "}"
+
+        lines.append(f"Dialogue: 0,{t0},{t1},Shad,,0,0,0,,{shad_tag}{txt}")
+        lines.append(f"Dialogue: 1,{t0},{t1},Text,,0,0,0,,{text_tag}{txt}")
+
+    return "\n".join(lines)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# ffprobe helper
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def probe_resolution(video_path: Path) -> tuple[int, int]:
+    result = subprocess.run(
+        ["ffprobe", "-v", "quiet", "-print_format", "json", "-show_streams",
+         str(video_path)],
+        capture_output=True, text=True,
+    )
+    try:
+        for s in json.loads(result.stdout).get("streams", []):
+            if s.get("codec_type") == "video":
+                return int(s.get("width", 720)), int(s.get("height", 1280))
+    except Exception:
+        pass
+    return 720, 1280
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Main burn
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def burn(video_in: Path, captions: list[dict], out_path: Path) -> None:
+    if not captions:
+        print("[subtitle_burn] No captions; copying input to output.", file=sys.stderr)
+        import shutil
+        shutil.copy2(str(video_in), str(out_path))
+        return
+
+    out_dir = out_path.parent
+    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    vid_w, vid_h = probe_resolution(video_in)
+    ass_content  = build_ass(captions, vid_w, vid_h)
+
+    ass_path = out_dir / "captions.ass"
+    ass_path.write_text(ass_content, encoding="utf-8")
+    print(f"[subtitle_burn] ASS written → {ass_path} "
+          f"({len(captions)} caption(s), {vid_w}×{vid_h})", file=sys.stderr)
+
+    ass_esc = str(ass_path).replace("\\", "/").replace(":", "\\:")
+    result  = subprocess.run([
+        "ffmpeg", "-y",
+        "-i",  str(video_in),
+        "-vf", f"subtitles={ass_esc}:fontsdir=/usr/share/fonts/truetype/noto",
+        "-c:v", "libx264", "-crf", "18", "-preset", "fast",
+        "-c:a", "copy",
+        str(out_path),
+    ])
+    if result.returncode != 0:
+        sys.exit(f"[subtitle_burn] ffmpeg failed (rc={result.returncode})")
+
+    print(f"[subtitle_burn] Done → {out_path}", file=sys.stderr)
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ASS subtitle burn")
+    ap.add_argument("--video",    required=True, help="Input video")
+    ap.add_argument("--captions", default=None,
+                    help="JSON file with captions array [{start,end,text}]")
+    ap.add_argument("--plan",     default=None,
+                    help="generation_plan.json (reads captions from plan.captions)")
+    ap.add_argument("--out",      required=True, help="Output mp4 path")
+    args = ap.parse_args()
+
+    if args.plan:
+        plan     = json.loads(Path(args.plan).read_text())
+        captions = plan.get("captions", [])
+    elif args.captions:
+        data     = json.loads(Path(args.captions).read_text())
+        captions = data if isinstance(data, list) else data.get("captions", data.get("items", []))
+    else:
+        sys.exit("[subtitle_burn] Provide --captions or --plan")
+
+    burn(
+        video_in = Path(args.video),
+        captions = captions,
+        out_path = Path(args.out),
+    )
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
