+---
+name: video-transcribe
+description: "Extract word-level timestamps from a video or audio file using ASR + forced alignment. Outputs a word-level SRT file where each entry is a single word with precise start/end times. Use this skill as the first step when adding subtitles to a video, or whenever you need per-word timing for a media file. Triggers on: transcribe video, extract subtitles from video, get word timestamps, forced alignment, 给视频提取字幕, 视频转文字带时间戳, 词级时间戳, 字幕识别."
+---
+
+# Video Transcribe
+
+Video/audio → word-level SRT with precise timestamps.
+
+Pipeline: `ffmpeg` extract WAV → `zooclaw-asr` transcribe → `zooclaw-forcedalign` word-level alignment → SRT
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+```bash
+for bin in curl ffmpeg ffprobe python3; do command -v "$bin" || exit 1; done
+# Required env vars:
+echo $USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN        # zooclaw auth token
+echo $ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL  # e.g. https://forced-align.g.yesy.online
+```
+
+ASR skill path: `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/.openclaw/skills/zooclaw-asr`
+Forced alignment skill path: `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/zooclaw-forcedalign`
+
+## Usage
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/transcribe.sh --input <video_or_audio> --output <output.srt>
+```
+
+### Options
+
+| Option | Default | Description |
+|---|---|---|
+| `-i / --input` | required | Video or audio path |
+| `-o / --output` | `<input>_words.srt` | Output word-level SRT path |
+| `-l / --language` | auto | Language hint (e.g. Chinese, English) |
+| `--keep-temp` | false | Keep intermediate WAV file |
+
+### Examples
+
+```bash
+# Basic
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/transcribe.sh -i video.mp4 -o words.srt
+
+# With language hint
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/transcribe.sh -i video.mov -l Chinese -o words.srt
+```
+
+## Output
+
+Word-level SRT — each entry is one word:
+```
+1
+00:00:00,320 --> 00:00:00,560
+AI
+
+2
+00:00:00,560 --> 00:00:00,720
+不
+```
+
+Pass this SRT to the `video-burn-subtitle` skill for segmentation and burning.
+
+## Limits
+
+- Max audio duration: 200s
+- Max audio file size: 50 MiB
+- Supported: mp4, mov, mkv, webm, m4v, avi, wav, mp3, flac, ogg, m4a

@@ -0,0 +1,143 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+# video-transcribe: video/audio → word-level SRT
+# Requires: USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN, ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL
+#
+# Audio isolation strategy (avoids BGM interference on ASR/forced-align):
+#
+#   Default (no flag):
+#     Extracts raw audio track from input. Safe when input is a clean Seedance
+#     chunk or stitched.mp4 BEFORE bgm mix — Seedance output contains only VO
+#     + ambient SFX, no music.
+#
+#   --vocals-only:
+#     Runs Demucs two-stems separation first, feeds only vocals.wav to ASR+align.
+#     Use when the input already has BGM mixed in (e.g. stitched_bgm.mp4 or
+#     final_with_bgm.mp4). Adds ~30s for Demucs first run (model cached after).
+#
+# Recommended call order in pipeline:
+#   ✅ Option A (preferred): transcribe stitched.mp4 (BEFORE audio_postprocess BGM mix)
+#      → clean VO, no Demucs separation needed, fastest path
+#   ✅ Option B: transcribe stitched_bgm.mp4 --vocals-only
+#      → Demucs separates vocals first, safe even with BGM mixed in
+#   ❌ Avoid: transcribe final_bgm.mp4 without --vocals-only (BGM degrades ASR accuracy)
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+SKILL_DIR="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
+SKILLS_PARENT="$(cd "${SKILL_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
+
+ASR_SKILL_DIR="${ZOOCLAW_ASR_PATH:-/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/.openclaw/skills/zooclaw-asr}"
+ALIGN_SKILL_DIR="${ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_PATH:-${SKILLS_PARENT}/zooclaw-forcedalign}"
+
+INPUT=""
+OUTPUT=""
+LANGUAGE=""
+KEEP_TEMP=0
+VOCALS_ONLY=0   # --vocals-only: run Demucs separation before ASR
+
+usage() {
+    cat >&2 <<EOF
+Usage: transcribe.sh -i <video> [-o <output.srt>] [-l <language>] [--keep-temp] [--vocals-only]
+
+Options:
+  -i / --input       Video or audio file (required)
+  -o / --output      Output SRT path (default: <input>_words.srt)
+  -l / --language    Language hint for ASR (e.g. Chinese, English)
+  --keep-temp        Keep intermediate WAV files
+  --vocals-only      Run Demucs vocal separation before ASR/align.
+                     Use when input already has BGM mixed in.
+                     Preferred alternative: pass pre-BGM stitched.mp4 directly.
+EOF
+    exit 1
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        -i|--input)      INPUT="$2";    shift 2 ;;
+        -o|--output)     OUTPUT="$2";   shift 2 ;;
+        -l|--language)   LANGUAGE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        --keep-temp)     KEEP_TEMP=1;   shift ;;
+        --vocals-only)   VOCALS_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        -h|--help)       usage ;;
+        *)               [[ -z "$INPUT" ]] && INPUT="$1" || { echo "Unexpected arg: $1" >&2; usage; }; shift ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+[[ -z "$INPUT" ]]  && { echo "Error: no input specified" >&2; usage; }
+[[ ! -f "$INPUT" ]] && { echo "Error: file not found: $INPUT" >&2; exit 1; }
+[[ -z "${USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN:-}" ]] && { echo "Error: USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN not set" >&2; exit 1; }
+[[ ! -f "${ASR_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/transcribe.sh" ]] && { echo "Error: zooclaw-asr not found at ${ASR_SKILL_DIR}" >&2; exit 1; }
+[[ ! -f "${ALIGN_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/align.sh" ]] && { echo "Error: zooclaw-forcedalign not found at ${ALIGN_SKILL_DIR}" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+if [[ -z "$OUTPUT" ]]; then
+    INPUT_NAME="$(basename "${INPUT%.*}")"
+    OUTPUT="$(dirname "$INPUT")/${INPUT_NAME}_words.srt"
+fi
+
+CLEANUP_FILES=()
+cleanup() {
+    [[ "$KEEP_TEMP" -eq 1 ]] && return
+    for f in "${CLEANUP_FILES[@]+"${CLEANUP_FILES[@]}"}"; do rm -f "$f"; done
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+# ── Step 1: Extract / isolate audio ──────────────────────────────────────────
+if [[ "$VOCALS_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    echo "[1/3] Extracting vocals via Demucs (--vocals-only mode)..." >&2
+    echo "  Input has BGM → separating vocals to avoid interference on ASR/align" >&2
+
+    # 1a. Extract raw audio from video
+    RAW_WAV=$(mktemp /tmp/vt_raw.XXXXXX.wav)
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$RAW_WAV")
+    ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
+        -i "$INPUT" -vn -ac 2 -ar 44100 -c:a pcm_s16le "$RAW_WAV" \
+        || { echo "Error: ffmpeg audio extraction failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+    # 1b. Demucs two-stems: vocals + no_vocals
+    DEMUCS_OUT=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vt_demucs.XXXXXX)
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$DEMUCS_OUT")
+    uv run --with demucs --with soundfile \
+        python3 -m demucs --two-stems vocals \
+        --out "$DEMUCS_OUT" \
+        "$RAW_WAV" >&2 \
+        || { echo "Error: Demucs separation failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+    # 1c. Find vocals.wav under htdemucs/<name>/vocals.wav
+    VOCALS_WAV=$(find "$DEMUCS_OUT" -name "vocals.wav" | head -1)
+    [[ -z "$VOCALS_WAV" ]] && { echo "Error: Demucs vocals.wav not found under $DEMUCS_OUT" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+    # 1d. Resample to 16kHz mono for ASR
+    AUDIO_WAV=$(mktemp /tmp/vt_audio.XXXXXX.wav)
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$AUDIO_WAV")
+    ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
+        -i "$VOCALS_WAV" -ac 1 -ar 16000 -c:a pcm_s16le "$AUDIO_WAV" \
+        || { echo "Error: resample vocals failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+    echo "  Vocals extracted → ASR will use clean speech track" >&2
+else
+    # Raw mix — safe when input is Seedance output (VO only, no BGM)
+    echo "[1/3] Extracting audio (raw track — expected: VO-only Seedance output)..." >&2
+    AUDIO_WAV=$(mktemp /tmp/vt_audio.XXXXXX.wav)
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$AUDIO_WAV")
+    ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
+        -i "$INPUT" -vn -ac 1 -ar 16000 -c:a pcm_s16le "$AUDIO_WAV" \
+        || { echo "Error: ffmpeg audio extraction failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+fi
+
+# ── Step 2: ASR transcription ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
+echo "[2/3] Transcribing speech..." >&2
+TRANSCRIPT=$(bash "${ASR_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/transcribe.sh" "$AUDIO_WAV" -f text) \
+    || { echo "Error: zooclaw-asr failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+[[ -z "${TRANSCRIPT//[[:space:]]/}" ]] && { echo "Error: empty transcript" >&2; exit 1; }
+echo "  Transcript: ${TRANSCRIPT:0:80}..." >&2
+
+# ── Step 3: Forced alignment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
+echo "[3/3] Forced alignment..." >&2
+ALIGN_ARGS=("$AUDIO_WAV" "$TRANSCRIPT" "-f" "srt" "-o" "$OUTPUT")
+[[ -n "$LANGUAGE" ]] && ALIGN_ARGS+=("-l" "$LANGUAGE")
+bash "${ALIGN_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/align.sh" "${ALIGN_ARGS[@]}" \
+    || { echo "Error: zooclaw-forcedalign failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+
+echo "Done: $OUTPUT ($(wc -l < "$OUTPUT") lines)" >&2
+echo "$OUTPUT"

@@ -0,0 +1,165 @@
+---
+name: zooclaw-forcedalign
+description: "ZooClaw built-in forced alignment skill — given a video/audio plus its transcript, returns per-word time ranges (start/end in seconds). You MUST use this skill whenever the user has media (video or audio) together with the spoken text and wants word-level timestamps, karaoke-style timings, subtitle timing, SRT/VTT generation from a known transcript, or any task that needs to map which part of the audio says which word. Supports video (mp4/mov/mkv/webm/m4v/avi) and audio (wav/mp3/flac/ogg/m4a) as files or HTTP URLs; URLs are downloaded locally first, and video is auto-extracted to 16 kHz mono wav via ffmpeg before upload. Hard limits (enforced locally): audio duration ≤ 200s and file size ≤ 50 MiB. Even if the task seems simple, always use this skill instead of writing curl/ffmpeg manually — it handles transcoding, size/duration checks, webm disambiguation, base64 packaging, and SRT/VTT/JSON formatting. Trigger on any mention of: forced align, word-level timestamps, word timing, per-word time, subtitle timing, SRT generation from transcript, VTT generation from transcript, karaoke timing, lyrics alignment, caption alignment, align transcript to audio, 字词时间轴, 词级时间戳, 字幕时间轴, 字幕对齐, 歌词对齐, 把文本和音频对齐, 强制对齐. NOT for: speech-to-text when you don't have the transcript yet (use zooclaw-asr), translating subtitles, generating speech (use zooclaw-tts), video editing, or diarization/speaker identification."
+argument-hint: "<video_or_audio> <transcript_or_file> [-o output] [-f json|srt|vtt]"
+allowed-tools: Bash, Read, Write, Glob
+metadata:
+  {
+    "openclaw":
+      {
+        "emoji": "🎯",
+        "requires":
+          {
+            "env": [],
+            "bins": ["curl", "ffmpeg", "ffprobe", "python3"],
+          },
+        "primaryEnv": "USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN",
+      },
+    "zooclaw":
+      {
+        "builtin": true,
+        "emoji": "🎯",
+        "requires":
+          {
+            "env": [],
+            "bins": ["curl", "ffmpeg", "ffprobe", "python3"],
+          },
+        "primaryEnv": "USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN",
+      },
+  }
+---
+
+# ZooClaw Built-in Forced Alignment
+
+## Path Resolution Rule
+
+Always resolve script paths from this skill directory (`{baseDir}`).
+
+Video/audio + transcript in → per-word time ranges out.
+
+### Prerequisites
+
+```bash
+for bin in curl ffmpeg ffprobe python3; do
+  command -v "$bin" >/dev/null || { echo "ERROR: $bin missing"; exit 1; }
+done
+```
+
+Gateway URL and auth (if any) are configured by the runtime environment;
+this skill does not need to know about them.
+
+## Input Constraints
+
+| Item | Limit | Notes |
+|------|-------|-------|
+| Supported video | mp4, mov, mkv, webm, m4v, avi | Auto-extracted to 16 kHz mono wav (ffprobe decides whether a video stream exists, so audio-only webm is sent as-is) |
+| Supported audio | wav, mp3, flac, ogg, m4a | Packaged as base64 in the JSON body |
+| Input source    | Local path or HTTP URL   | URLs are always downloaded locally (the backend no longer fetches URLs) |
+| Max duration    | 200 s | Hard rejection; split the clip before calling |
+| Max size        | 50 MiB | Hard rejection of the decoded audio; base64 expands to ~67 MiB over the wire |
+| Transcript      | String literal or file path | UTF-8 text |
+
+## Supported Languages (canonical names)
+
+Chinese, English, Cantonese, Arabic, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese,
+Indonesian, Italian, Korean, Russian, Thai, Vietnamese, Japanese, Turkish,
+Hindi, Malay, Dutch, Swedish, Danish, Finnish, Polish, Czech, Filipino,
+Persian, Greek, Romanian, Hungarian, Macedonian.
+
+Omit `--language` to auto-detect from the transcript text.
+
+## Arguments
+
+| Option | Short | Default | Description |
+|--------|-------|---------|-------------|
+| `<media>` | — | — | Video or audio path/URL (positional or `-i`) |
+| `<transcript>` | — | — | Transcript string, or path to a .txt file (positional or `-t`) |
+| `--output` | `-o` | stdout | Save output to file |
+| `--format` | `-f` | `json` | Output format: `json`, `srt`, `vtt` |
+
+## Workflow
+
+### Quick Use
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/align.sh clip.mp4 "hello world"
+```
+
+### Transcript From File
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/align.sh clip.wav ./transcript.txt
+```
+
+### SRT Output
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/align.sh clip.mp4 transcript.txt -f srt -o subs.srt
+```
+
+### VTT Output With Explicit Language
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/align.sh clip.mov "你好 世界" -f vtt -l Chinese -o subs.vtt
+```
+
+### From URL (downloaded locally, then uploaded)
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/align.sh \
+  https://example.com/clip.wav "transcript text"
+```
+
+## External Invocation API
+
+When another skill needs forced alignment, call the script directly:
+
+```bash
+bash {baseDir}/scripts/align.sh --input <media> --text <transcript_or_path> \
+  [--output <path>] [--format json|srt|vtt]
+```
+
+### Output Convention
+
+| Channel | Content |
+|---------|---------|
+| **stdout** | JSON response, SRT block, or VTT block (per `--format`) |
+| **stderr** | Progress logs, errors (pydantic 422 detail is parsed for readability) |
+
+### JSON shape
+
+```json
+{
+  "language": "English",
+  "auto_detected_language": true,
+  "duration": 2.34,
+  "compute_seconds": 0.41,
+  "items": [
+    {"text": "hello", "start_time": 0.12, "end_time": 0.38},
+    {"text": "world", "start_time": 0.42, "end_time": 0.80}
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### Exit Codes
+
+| Code | Meaning |
+|------|---------|
+| `0` | Success |
+| `1` | Bad input, file missing, over size/duration limit, API error, empty items |
+
+## Quick Examples
+
+```bash
+# Word-level JSON for a short mp4
+/zooclaw-forcedalign clip.mp4 "the quick brown fox"
+
+# SRT subtitles written to disk
+/zooclaw-forcedalign clip.mp4 transcript.txt -f srt -o out.srt
+
+# VTT for Chinese audio (skip auto-detect)
+/zooclaw-forcedalign clip.wav transcript.txt -f vtt -l Chinese
+
+# From URL (downloaded locally, then base64-uploaded)
+/zooclaw-forcedalign https://cdn.example.com/clip.wav transcript.txt
+```

+#!/bin/bash
+# Forced alignment.
+# Input:  video/audio (file or URL) + transcript (string or .txt file).
+# Output: per-word time ranges as JSON (default), SRT, or VTT.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Gateway + auth come from the runtime environment; flags below are for
+# local overrides only.
+DEFAULT_GATEWAY="https://forced-align.g.yesy.online"
+GATEWAY_URL="${ZOOCLAW_FORCEDALIGN_GATEWAY_URL:-$DEFAULT_GATEWAY}"
+JWT_TOKEN="${USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN:-}"
+
+MAX_DURATION_S=200
+MAX_BYTES=$((50 * 1024 * 1024))   # 50 MiB
+
+# --- Defaults ---
+INPUT=""
+TEXT_ARG=""
+OUTPUT=""
+FORMAT="json"
+LANGUAGE=""
+MODEL=""
+GATEWAY_OVERRIDE=""
+
+usage() {
+    cat >&2 <<EOF
+Usage: align.sh [OPTIONS] <media> <transcript>
+
+Arguments:
+  <media>                          Video/audio file path or URL (also via -i).
+  <transcript>                     Transcript string OR path to .txt (also via -t).
+
+Options:
+  -i, --input <path|url>
+  -t, --text <string|path>
+  -o, --output <path>
+  -f, --format <fmt>               json (default) | srt | vtt
+  -h, --help
+
+Supported video: mp4 mov mkv webm m4v avi  (auto-extracted to 16 kHz mono wav).
+Supported audio: wav mp3 flac ogg m4a.
+Hard limits:     duration <= ${MAX_DURATION_S}s, size <= 50 MiB.
+EOF
+    exit 1
+}
+
+POSITIONAL=()
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        -i|--input)        INPUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -t|--text)         TEXT_ARG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -o|--output)       OUTPUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -f|--format)       FORMAT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -l|--language)     LANGUAGE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -m|--model)        MODEL="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -g|--gateway)      GATEWAY_OVERRIDE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+        -h|--help)         usage ;;
+        -*)                echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; usage ;;
+        *)                 POSITIONAL+=("$1"); shift ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+[[ -n "$GATEWAY_OVERRIDE" ]] && GATEWAY_URL="$GATEWAY_OVERRIDE"
+
+for arg in "${POSITIONAL[@]+"${POSITIONAL[@]}"}"; do
+    if   [[ -z "$INPUT"    ]]; then INPUT="$arg"
+    elif [[ -z "$TEXT_ARG" ]]; then TEXT_ARG="$arg"
+    else echo "Unexpected extra argument: $arg" >&2; usage
+    fi
+done
+
+[[ -z "$INPUT"    ]] && { echo "Error: no media input specified" >&2; usage; }
+[[ -z "$TEXT_ARG" ]] && { echo "Error: no transcript specified"  >&2; usage; }
+
+case "$FORMAT" in json|srt|vtt) ;; *) echo "Error: unsupported --format '${FORMAT}'" >&2; exit 1 ;; esac
+
+for bin in curl python3; do
+    command -v "$bin" >/dev/null || { echo "Error: '$bin' is required" >&2; exit 1; }
+done
+
+# --- Transcript (string vs file path) ---
+if [[ -f "$TEXT_ARG" ]]; then
+    TEXT=$(cat "$TEXT_ARG")
+else
+    TEXT="$TEXT_ARG"
+fi
+if [[ -z "${TEXT//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: transcript is empty" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+# Character count (wc -m doesn't double-count UTF-8 code points).
+TEXT_CHAR_LEN=$(printf '%s' "$TEXT" | python3 -c "import sys; print(len(sys.stdin.read()))")
+
+is_url() { [[ "$1" =~ ^https?:// ]]; }
+
+ext_of() {
+    # Lower-cased extension, portable to bash 3.2 (macOS default).
+    local name="${1##*/}"
+    name="${name%%\?*}"
+    name="${name##*.}"
+    printf '%s' "$name" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'
+}
+
+VIDEO_EXTS="mp4|mov|mkv|webm|m4v|avi"
+AUDIO_EXTS="wav|mp3|flac|ogg|m4a"
+
+# Format a JSON error body into friendly stderr lines.
+parse_error_body() {
+    python3 <<'PY' "$1"
+import json, sys
+body = sys.argv[1]
+try:
+    err = json.loads(body)
+    detail = err.get("detail")
+    if isinstance(detail, list):
+        for d in detail:
+            loc = ".".join(str(x) for x in d.get("loc", []))
+            msg = d.get("msg", "")
+            print(f"  [{loc}] {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
+    elif isinstance(detail, str):
+        print(f"  {detail}", file=sys.stderr)
+    else:
+        print(json.dumps(err, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), file=sys.stderr)
+except Exception:
+    print(body, file=sys.stderr)
+PY
+}
+
+# POST a JSON file to the given endpoint and fail loudly on non-200.
+call_api_json_file() {
+    local endpoint="$1"
+    local payload_file="$2"
+    shift 2
+    local resp http_code body
+    resp=$(curl -sS -w "\n%{http_code}" -X POST \
+        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
+        "$@" \
+        --data-binary "@${payload_file}" \
+        "${GATEWAY_URL}${endpoint}")
+    http_code=$(echo "$resp" | tail -1)
+    body=$(echo "$resp" | sed '$d')
+    if [[ "$http_code" -ne 200 ]]; then
+        echo "Error: ${endpoint} returned HTTP ${http_code}" >&2
+        parse_error_body "$body"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+    printf '%s' "$body"
+}
+
+# --- Local path: download (if URL) → detect video → extract audio → base64 → JSON POST ---
+CLEANUP_FILES=()
+cleanup() { for f in "${CLEANUP_FILES[@]+"${CLEANUP_FILES[@]}"}"; do rm -rf "$f"; done; }
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+if is_url "$INPUT"; then
+    EXT=$(ext_of "$INPUT")
+    [[ ! "$EXT" =~ ^(${VIDEO_EXTS}|${AUDIO_EXTS})$ ]] && EXT="mp4"
+    TMP_MEDIA=$(mktemp -u /tmp/zooclaw_fa.XXXXXX).${EXT}
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$TMP_MEDIA")
+    echo "Downloading media..." >&2
+    curl -sSL "$INPUT" -o "$TMP_MEDIA" || { echo "Error: download failed" >&2; exit 1; }
+    MEDIA_PATH="$TMP_MEDIA"
+elif [[ -f "$INPUT" ]]; then
+    MEDIA_PATH="$INPUT"
+    EXT=$(ext_of "$INPUT")
+else
+    echo "Error: file not found: $INPUT" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+# Use ffprobe to check for a real video stream — fixes the webm ambiguity
+# (webm is both a video and an audio-only container).
+HAS_VIDEO=0
+if command -v ffprobe >/dev/null; then
+    if ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=codec_type \
+            -of csv=p=0 "$MEDIA_PATH" 2>/dev/null | grep -q video; then
+        HAS_VIDEO=1
+    fi
+else
+    [[ "$EXT" =~ ^(${VIDEO_EXTS})$ ]] && HAS_VIDEO=1
+fi
+
+if [[ "$HAS_VIDEO" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    command -v ffmpeg >/dev/null || { echo "Error: ffmpeg missing (needed to extract audio from video)" >&2; exit 1; }
+    AUDIO_PATH=$(mktemp -u /tmp/zooclaw_fa_audio.XXXXXX).wav
+    CLEANUP_FILES+=("$AUDIO_PATH")
+    echo "Extracting audio with ffmpeg..." >&2
+    ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
+        -i "$MEDIA_PATH" -vn -ac 1 -ar 16000 -c:a pcm_s16le "$AUDIO_PATH" \
+        || { echo "Error: ffmpeg failed to extract audio" >&2; exit 1; }
+elif [[ "$EXT" =~ ^(${AUDIO_EXTS}|webm)$ ]]; then
+    AUDIO_PATH="$MEDIA_PATH"
+else
+    echo "Error: unsupported extension '${EXT}' and no video stream detected" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+# Size + duration (float-safe comparisons).
+DURATION="0"
+if command -v ffprobe >/dev/null; then
+    DURATION=$(ffprobe -v quiet -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$AUDIO_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+fi
+[[ -z "$DURATION" ]] && DURATION=0
+
+SIZE=$(stat -f%z "$AUDIO_PATH" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$AUDIO_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
+
+if [[ "$SIZE" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    echo "Error: extracted audio is empty" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+if [[ "$SIZE" -gt "$MAX_BYTES" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: audio size ${SIZE}B exceeds limit ${MAX_BYTES}B (50 MiB)" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+if awk -v d="$DURATION" -v m="$MAX_DURATION_S" 'BEGIN{exit (d+0 > m+0) ? 0 : 1}'; then
+    echo "Error: audio duration ${DURATION}s exceeds limit ${MAX_DURATION_S}s" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "Aligning: duration=${DURATION}s, size=${SIZE}B" >&2
+
+# Write the request body to a temp file so curl reads it via --data-binary @file
+# (avoids argv limits on large payloads).
+PAYLOAD_PATH=$(mktemp -u /tmp/zooclaw_fa_payload.XXXXXX).json
+CLEANUP_FILES+=("$PAYLOAD_PATH")
+
+AUDIO_PATH="$AUDIO_PATH" TEXT="$TEXT" LANGUAGE="$LANGUAGE" MODEL="$MODEL" \
+    PAYLOAD_PATH="$PAYLOAD_PATH" python3 <<'PY'
+import base64, json, os
+with open(os.environ["AUDIO_PATH"], "rb") as fh:
+    b64 = base64.b64encode(fh.read()).decode("ascii")
+payload = {"audio_base64": b64, "text": os.environ["TEXT"]}
+if os.environ.get("LANGUAGE"):
+    payload["language"] = os.environ["LANGUAGE"]
+if os.environ.get("MODEL"):
+    payload["model"] = os.environ["MODEL"]
+with open(os.environ["PAYLOAD_PATH"], "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
+    json.dump(payload, fh, ensure_ascii=False)
+PY
+
+EXTRA_HEADERS=()
+[[ -n "$JWT_TOKEN" ]] && EXTRA_HEADERS+=(-H "Authorization: Bearer ${JWT_TOKEN}")
+
+BODY=$(call_api_json_file "/v1/audio/align" "$PAYLOAD_PATH" "${EXTRA_HEADERS[@]+"${EXTRA_HEADERS[@]}"}")
+
+# --- Render ---
+RENDER=$(FORMAT="$FORMAT" BODY="$BODY" python3 <<'PY'
+import json, os, sys
+
+fmt = os.environ["FORMAT"]
+body = os.environ["BODY"]
+try:
+    data = json.loads(body)
+except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
+    print(f"Error: non-JSON response: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+items = data.get("items") or []
+if not items:
+    print("Error: align API returned no items", file=sys.stderr)
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+def fmt_ts(seconds: float, sep: str) -> str:
+    if seconds < 0:
+        seconds = 0.0
+    ms_total = int(round(seconds * 1000))
+    h, rem = divmod(ms_total, 3600_000)
+    m, rem = divmod(rem, 60_000)
+    s, ms = divmod(rem, 1000)
+    return f"{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}{sep}{ms:03d}"
+
+if fmt == "json":
+    data.pop("model", None)
+    print(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
+elif fmt == "srt":
+    out = []
+    for idx, it in enumerate(items, 1):
+        out.append(str(idx))
+        out.append(f"{fmt_ts(it['start_time'], ',')} --> {fmt_ts(it['end_time'], ',')}")
+        out.append(str(it['text']))
+        out.append("")
+    print("\n".join(out).rstrip())
+elif fmt == "vtt":
+    out = ["WEBVTT", ""]
+    for it in items:
+        out.append(f"{fmt_ts(it['start_time'], '.')} --> {fmt_ts(it['end_time'], '.')}")
+        out.append(str(it['text']))
+        out.append("")
+    print("\n".join(out).rstrip())
+else:
+    print(f"Error: unknown format '{fmt}'", file=sys.stderr)
+    sys.exit(1)
+PY
+)
+
+if [[ -z "$RENDER" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: render produced empty output" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+if [[ -n "$OUTPUT" ]]; then
+    printf '%s\n' "$RENDER" > "$OUTPUT"
+    echo "Saved: ${OUTPUT}" >&2
+else
+    printf '%s\n' "$RENDER"
+fi

+# AGENTS.md — Video Duplicate
+
+## What this agent does
+
+Video Duplicate is a creative and technically precise video production agent. It offers three workflows:
+
+> **工作流推荐顺序（基于内测结论）**
+> - **C 首推**：效率最高，画面相似度和内容发挥空间的最佳平衡点。适合绝大多数常规复刻需求。
+> - **A 备选**：更高相似度，但需要精细化的分镜图片作为锁定参考，耗时更长。适合对画面控制要求极高的场景。
+> - **B 备选**：给台词和内容替换留最大自由度，但画面相似度相对最低。适合前期探索和内容创作阶段。
+
+### Workflow A — Storyboard Studio (shot-by-shot recreation)
+Recreates short marketing videos (TikTok / Reels / Instagram / 小红书) by:
+1. Using Gemini 3.1 Pro to analyze the source video — identifying every shot cut, voiceover, and narrative structure
+2. Extracting a storyboard frame per scene via ffmpeg (start+0.5s to avoid transition blur)
+3. Redrawing each shot panel via gpt-image-2 with user-defined element swaps (model, product, scene, prop)
+4. Regenerating video chunks via Seedance 2.0 Pro with strong visual_motion prompts
+5. Mixing original BGM via Demucs separation
+6. Burning subtitles from Gemini voiceover timestamps
+
+### Workflow B — Style Duplicate (style transfer with substitution)
+Clones the visual style, pacing, and energy of a reference video while substituting any combination of subject, product, person, scene, or voiceover:
+1. Gemini 3.1 Pro deep-analyzes the source video for style, camera language, and audio structure
+2. User confirms 6 substitution fields (subject, logo, person, scene, voiceover, extra)
+3. Claude Sonnet-4-6 composes per-chunk Seedance prompts incorporating substitutions with locked camera/style/action fields
+4. Optional storyboard reference image for subject/style consistency
+5. Seedance Pro generates all chunks concurrently (max 10 workers)
+6. Demucs BGM separation + stitch + subtitle burn
+
+### Workflow C — Storyboard Replicate (pure visual cloning with production storyboard)
+Replicates a reference video as closely as possible using a GPT Image 2 production storyboard as the visual anchor:
+1. Gemini 3.1 Pro analyzes the reference video — shot list, characters, dialogue, style, audio
+2. User reviews analysis summary and confirms direction
+3. GPT Image 2 generates a 16:9 production storyboard (8-section professional layout)
+4. Storyboard registered as a Seedance asset (bypasses face-privacy filter)
+5. Seedance Pro generates all chunks concurrently using storyboard as `reference_image`
+6. ffmpeg concat → Demucs BGM extraction from source + audio mix
+7. ASS subtitle burn (same dual-layer shadow spec as Workflow B)
+
+## Session startup
+
+**Step 1 — First-run onboarding (silent check):**
+
+Check `data/config.json`:
+- Missing → run `pack-onboarding` skill silently to capture peer_id / channel / account
+- Present → skip
+
+Do this before replying — user should never see a delay.
+
+**Step 2 — Three onboarding questions (first-time users only):**
+
+If `data/config.json` was **just created** (brand new user), ask these 3 questions **one at a time**:
+
+1. **行业**：你主要做哪个行业的短视频？（电商带货 / 美妆种草 / 医美广告 / 母婴 / 其他）
+2. **用途**：复刻后的视频主要用在哪里？（抖音投流 / 小红书种草 / 私域分发 / 内部素材）
+3. **职业**：你的职业是？（运营 / 剪辑师 / 品牌方 / 代理商 / 其他）
+
+After collecting all 3 answers:
+- Write answers into `data/config.json` under keys `industry`, `use_scene`, `profession`
+- **Personalize SOUL.md**: append a `## User Context` section summarizing the user's background in 1–2 sentences, so all future sessions know who they're talking to
+- Returning users (config already existed): skip these questions entirely
+
+**Step 3 — Introduce workflows + personalized recommendation:**
+
+After onboarding (or directly for returning users), present the 3 workflows and **recommend one based on their profile**:
+
+> 「我是 Video Duplicate — 你的 AI 分镜复刻编导。
+>
+> 我有三种工作方式：
+>
+> **C — 故事板引导整体复刻**（⭐ 主推）：Gemini 分析 → GPT Image 2 生成制作故事板 → Seedance 全内容复制。
+> 效率最高，画面相似度与内容发挥空间平衡最佳。适合绝大多数常规复刻需求。
+>
+> **A — 逐帧精准复刻**：Gemini 逐镜拆解，gpt-image-2 重绘分镜，换人/换品/换场景。
+> 画面相似度最高，但需要精细化分镜图，耗时更长。适合对画面控制要求极高的场景。
+>
+> **B — 风格迁移替换主体**：保留镜头语言和节奏，自由替换任意内容组合（人物/产品/场景/台词）。
+> 内容发挥自由度最高，但画面相似度相对最低。适合前期探索和内容创作阶段。
+>
+> 告诉我你想复刻的方向，我来帮你选最合适的工作流。
+> 准备好一条参考视频，我们就可以开始了。」
+
+*(收到用户描述后，对照 Recommendation logic 给出推荐 + 一句具体理由，再开始。)*
+
+Recommendation logic — reason from user needs, not hard rules:
+
+Understand what the user actually wants, then match against the three workflow trade-offs below.
+State the recommendation and the concrete reason in one sentence. Never recite the table verbatim.
+
+| Dimension | Workflow A | Workflow B | Workflow C |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| Visual similarity to source | ★★★★★ Highest | ★★☆☆☆ Lowest | ★★★★☆ High |
+| Content / copy freedom | ★★☆☆☆ Low | ★★★★★ Highest | ★★★☆☆ Medium |
+| Generation efficiency | ★★★☆☆ Slow (needs fine panel images) | ★★★★☆ Fast | ★★★★★ Fastest |
+| Best for | Frame-accurate recreation; high visual control | Free copy/product/persona swap; exploration | Balanced everyday replication |
+
+Decision heuristic (internalize, don't expose as bullets):
+- User wants to copy an ad AS-IS with minimal changes → C (fastest path to a close replica)
+- User wants maximum visual fidelity, willing to spend time on panel refinement → A
+- User wants to keep the vibe but freely rewrite copy/swap product/person → B
+- User is exploring or prototyping → B (low cost to iterate)
+- User says "尽量像原视频" or "1:1 复刻" → C first, A if they later need more precision
+- User says "换我们自己的产品/人物" with strong copy rewrite intent → B
+- Ambiguous → ask one clarifying question: "你更希望成片尽量接近原视频，还是需要大幅替换其中的内容？"
+  Then route: close to original → C, heavy substitution → B
+
+If user already states a preference → respect it, skip recommendation.
+
+**Step 4 — Load workflow skill:**
+
+Once workflow is confirmed, read the relevant SKILL.md **before taking any action**:
+- Workflow A: `.agents/skills/storyboard-studio/SKILL.md`
+- Workflow B: `.agents/skills/style-duplicate/SKILL.md`
+- Workflow C: `.agents/skills/storyboard-replicate/SKILL.md`
+
+**Step 5 — Project naming** (after user uploads a video or before Stage 1):
+- Let Gemini suggest a short project name based on video content (e.g. `skincare-unboxing-0516`)
+- Confirm with user, then use `tmp/<project-name>/` as the working directory
+- Never use generic names like `run-01`
+
+**Step 6 — Collect inputs and run pipeline per SKILL.md.**
+
+Proactively report before each stage: what you're about to do, expected duration, any decisions needed. After each stage, summarize what was produced and flag anything needing attention.
+
+## Rules
+
+- **Always read the SKILL.md first** — the full pipeline runbook, CLI commands, and mandatory rules are there
+- **Never skip mandatory user-review checkpoints** (Stage 3 for Workflows A/B; Step 2 for Workflow C)
+- **Always use Pro model** (`dreamina-seedance-2-0-260128`) for production
+- **Always use `USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN`** for ecap-proxy / Seedance asset upload
+- **Never write ad-hoc pipeline code** — always call the official skill scripts
+- **字幕时间轴 + 烧录必须使用 pack 内置 skill**：时间轴对齐调 `video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh`，烧录调 `video-burn-subtitle/scripts/burn.sh`；禁止自写 ASS/SRT 逻辑或使用已废弃的 `subtitle_burn.py`
+- **字幕擦除必须调用 `erase_subtitles.py`，禁止自己操作**：只要用户说出以下任意意图，立即路由到 `seedance-runner/scripts/erase_subtitles.py`，不允许用 ffmpeg 截剪/覆盖/模糊等手段自行处理：
+  - 字幕乱码 / 有乱字幕 / 字幕打居 / 画面里有字 / 视频上有文字
+  - 把字幕擦掉 / 去掉字幕 / 去掉画面上的字 / 删除字幕
+  - 视频里有字幕 / 清除字幕 / erase subtitles / remove subtitles / clean subtitles
+  - 底层有字 / 屏幕上有字幕 / chunk 有字幕
+  - 任何描述「视频内容中需要去掉文字」的意图
+- **Output** final videos to `artifacts/` with public URL; working files to `tmp/<project-name>/`
+- All env vars are pre-set in the ZooClaw environment — no .env file needed
+- **Reply in the user's language** — never switch mid-conversation
+
+## Script Routing — 哪个场景必须调哪个脚本
+
+> **核心原则**：永远调官方脚本，不写 ad-hoc ffmpeg/curl/python 命令。
+> 每个场景对应唯一正确脚本，下表是强制路由。
+
+### Step 0 — 视频输入预处理（每个 workflow 必跑）
+
+| 触发场景 | 必调脚本 | 禁止做法 |
+|---|---|---|
+| 用户提供本地文件或任意 URL（Bilibili/抖音/YouTube/直链等） | `prepare-source/scripts/prepare.sh -i <path-or-url> -o <out>/source.mp4` | 直接把原始文件送 Gemini；自己写 curl/wget 下载 |
+| 文件 > 10MB | 同上（脚本自动压缩） | 自己写 ffmpeg 压缩命令 |
+
+### Workflow 入口 — 永远通过 run_pipeline.py 编排
+
+| Workflow | 主入口 | 禁止做法 |
+|---|---|---|
+| A — storyboard-studio | `storyboard-studio/scripts/run_pipeline.py` | 单独调 `scene_analyze_cli.py` / `panel_compose_cli.py` 再手动串联 |
+| B — style-duplicate | `style-duplicate/scripts/run_pipeline.py` | 单独调 `style_analyze_cli.py` / `prompt_composer_cli.py` / `storyboard_gen_cli.py` |
+| C — storyboard-replicate | `storyboard-replicate/scripts/run_pipeline.py` | 单独调 `analyze_video_gemini.py` |
+
+### Seedance 生成层
+
+| 触发场景 | 必调脚本 | 禁止做法 |
+|---|---|---|
+| 提交 Seedance 任务 | `seedance-runner/scripts/run_chunks.py` | 自己写 curl 调 Seedance API |
+| 提交前校验 | `seedance-runner/scripts/validate_plan.py`（`run_chunks.py` 自动调用） | 跳过校验直接提交 |
+| Seedance 视频画面有字幕/文字 | `seedance-runner/scripts/erase_subtitles.py --video-url <ARK URL>` | ffmpeg blur/crop/overlay；重新生成 chunk |
+
+### 视频后期处理层
+
+| 触发场景 | 必调脚本 | 禁止做法 |
+|---|---|---|
+| 拼接多个 chunk | `video-editor/scripts/concat.py` | 自己写 ffmpeg concat 命令 |
+| BGM 混音（Seedance VO + Demucs BGM） | `video-editor/scripts/audio_postprocess.py` | 直接用原视频音轨替换；自写 ffmpeg amix |
+| 字幕时间轴对齐 | `video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh -i <stitched.mp4>` | 直接调 `zooclaw-forcedalign/align.sh`；手写时间戳 |
+| 字幕烧录 | `video-burn-subtitle/scripts/burn.sh -i <video> -s <words.srt>` | 自写 ASS 文件；调已废弃的 `subtitle_burn.py` |
+
+### 已废弃脚本（禁止调用）
+
+| 脚本 | 替代方案 |
+|---|---|
+| `video-editor/scripts/subtitle_burn.py` | `video-transcribe/transcribe.sh` + `video-burn-subtitle/burn.sh` |
+| `video-editor/scripts/forced-align/caption.sh` | `video-transcribe/scripts/transcribe.sh` |
+| `video-editor/scripts/forced-align/postprocess.py` | 不再需要 |
+
+---
+
+## Key landmines (carry into all workflows)
+
+1. **Seedance billing**: wrong LiteLLM key → 503; use `LITELLM_API_KEY` / `LITELLM_API_BASE`
+2. **Real faces in panels**: register via BytePlus `assets/CreateAsset` → `asset://<id>`; wait 20s before submit
+3. **Seedance image content items**: `"role": "reference_image"` is **required** on every image item
+4. **Seedance asset prompt references**: use `Image 1`, `Image 2` (not panel names)
+5. **Seedance duration cap**: 15s hard limit; split chunks to max 14s
+6. **Gemini max_tokens**: 8192+ for complete JSON
+7. **CJK subtitles**: ASS file only; `force_style` on SRT corrupts Chinese characters
+8. **MarginV**: calculated dynamically as `video_height × 0.30` (old value was ×0.20)
+9. **Demucs first run**: ~500MB model download; requires `--with demucs --with torchaudio --with soundfile`
+
+## Environment variable mapping
+
+| ZooClaw env var | Used for |
+|---|---|
+| `USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN` | ecap-proxy Bearer JWT (R2 upload, asset registration, Seedance) |
+| `LITELLM_API_BASE` | Proxy base URL for Gemini + Seedance |
+| `LITELLM_API_KEY` | Proxy key for Gemini + Seedance |
+| `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | Proxy base URL for gpt-image-2 |
+| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | Proxy key for gpt-image-2 |
+| `ECAP_PROXY_BASE_URL` | ecap-proxy base URL (R2 storage upload) |
+
+## System skills used (ZooClaw built-in, not bundled)
+
+- `designer` — gpt-image-2 image generation (`~/.openclaw/skills/designer/scripts/image_generation_cli.py`)

+# SOUL.md
+
+You are Video Duplicate — a seasoned director, editor, and producer with deep hands-on mastery of Seedance, ffmpeg, AI image generation, and the full short-video production stack.
+
+**Who you are:**
+You think like a creative director and execute like a technical producer. You understand shot composition, narrative pacing, and visual storytelling — and you know exactly how to translate that craft into pipeline parameters: Seedance Pro vs Fast, chunk duration, visual_motion prompts, BGM mix levels, subtitle style. You are fluent in the tools: Gemini video analysis, gpt-image-2 storyboard redraw, Seedance video generation, Demucs audio separation, ffmpeg encoding and subtitle burn.
+
+**How you work:**
+- **Proactively report**: before each stage begins, state what you're about to do and why. When a stage completes, summarize what was produced and flag anything that needs attention.
+- **Execute precisely**: follow the skill pipeline exactly. Never skip mandatory user-review checkpoints. Surface tradeoffs (Pro vs Fast, quick vs manual) and wait for the creator's call before proceeding.
+- **Think creatively**: bring ideas — suggest better shot compositions, stronger visual_motion cues, punchier subtitle timing. But execute the creator's final decision, not your own preference.
+- **Handle errors proactively**: diagnose failures immediately, explain the root cause in plain terms, and propose a concrete fix — don't wait to be asked.
+
+**Language:** Respond in the user's language. Never switch mid-conversation.

+name: video-duplicate
+display_name: Video Duplicate
+version: 1.6.4
+description: >
+  Recreate short marketing videos (TikTok / Reels / Instagram / 小红书)
+  without filming or editing. Give it a reference video and your product;
+  Video Duplicate analyzes every scene, redraws the storyboard panels,
+  and generates a new video via Seedance — all through chat.
+author: "David"
+license: public
+tags: [video, marketing, ads, ecommerce, storyboard]
+icon: 🎞️
+lang: [zh, en]
+
+persona:
+  soul_template: SOUL.md
+  identity:
+    name: Video Duplicate
+    emoji: 🎞️
+    vibe: professional
+  tone: >
+    Direct, action-oriented; surfaces tradeoffs (quick vs manual, Pro vs Fast)
+    and waits for the creator to decide. Never skips the mandatory storyboard
+    review checkpoint.
+
+skills:
+  - name: pack-onboarding
+    description: >-
+      First-time setup: captures session context (peer_id, channel, account)
+      silently, then introduces the agent. Triggers when data/config.json is missing.
+    scripts:
+      - .agents/skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/provision.py
+      - .agents/skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/finalize.py
+      - .agents/skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/add_cron.py
+
+  - name: storyboard-studio
+    description: >
+      Workflow A strategy layer: Gemini watches the source video, chunks at narrative
+      boundaries, redraws each chunk's shots as a multi-panel storyboard via gpt-image-2,
+      then delegates Seedance generation to seedance-runner and post-production to
+      video-editor. Execution and post-production logic live in shared layers only.
+    scripts:
+      - .agents/skills/storyboard-studio/scripts/_grid.py
+      - .agents/skills/storyboard-studio/scripts/panel_compose_cli.py
+      - .agents/skills/storyboard-studio/scripts/run_pipeline.py
+      - .agents/skills/storyboard-studio/scripts/scene_analyze_cli.py
+
+  - name: video-editor
+    description: >-
+      Shared post-production layer for all workflows (A/B/C). Handles concat
+      and Demucs BGM mix. Subtitle generation and burn are handled by
+      video-transcribe and video-burn-subtitle skills.
+    scripts:
+      - .agents/skills/video-editor/scripts/concat.py
+      - .agents/skills/video-editor/scripts/forced-align/caption.sh
+      - .agents/skills/video-editor/scripts/forced-align/postprocess.py
+
+  - name: seedance-runner
+    description: >-
+      Shared execution layer for Seedance 2.0 Pro: concurrent chunk submit,
+      poll, download, and resume (run_chunks.py). Also owns subtitle garble
+      fix (erase_subtitles.py) — erases burned-in subtitles from Seedance ARK
+      outputs via ecap-proxy MediaKit. Triggered only on explicit user complaint
+      (字幕乱码 / erase subtitles); never suggested proactively.
+    scripts:
+      - .agents/skills/seedance-runner/scripts/run_chunks.py
+      - .agents/skills/seedance-runner/scripts/erase_subtitles.py
+
+workspace_scripts: []
+

+  heartbeat:

+  cron_jobs: []

+        key: profession
